A Beautiful Dream!.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hearth Magic]]
| objectsreq = None
| producedby = [[requires::Dream Catcher]]
}}

A magical dream harvested from a [[Dream Catcher]]. Dreams are used to create various magical objects. A Beautiful Dream's quality is determined solely by the quality of the [[Dream Catcher]] it is harvested from.

Be careful with your dreams. If dropped, they will disappear. Doing so gives the player a chance of triggering the Lore [[Experience Points|Letting go of Your Dream]]. Dreams can be placed in containers for storage.

Exceptionally, it is '''not''' needed to discover {{PAGENAME}} to unlock ability to build [[Hearth Fire]] or [[Personal Claim]]. 

==How to Acquire ==
Right click on a [[Dream Catcher]]. If it has caught any dreams and you have the [[Hearth Magic]] skill, you will be presented with an option to harvest the dream(s). 

==Quality==

Dream Catcher Q = <math>\frac{_{q}AvgBough+ _{q}AvgString}{2}</math>

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Dungeon Loot]]

[[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a weird sort of meat obtained from large ants.

==How to Acquire==

Defeat [[Large Ant|large ant]] in [[Ant Hill Dungeon]] and butcher corpse.

==Quality==

The quality of {{PAGENAME}} is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Apple Pie}}

Apple Pie, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[Food]] fills 400% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 10 [[CON]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as apple pies, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[Ashes|ash]].

The apple pie will be ready in 16 minutes and 40 seconds.

Aurochs.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(12/11)-(-12/11)round6}} | | baseq = 30 | | armor = 15 | hp = 350 | fhp = 250 | | loot = [[Fresh Aurochs Hide]] x2, [[Intestines]] x3, [[Entrails]] x3, [[Animal Fat]], [[Raw Wild Beef]] x14, [[Bone Material]] x6, [[Aurochs Hair]]* }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] ''Note: The singular noun is aurochs while the plural is aurochsen.'' Aurochs/Aurochsen (Oar-awks/Oar-aukzen, like English ox/oxen) are a now extinct cattle relative that once lived in Asia, Europe and North Africa. The last recorded aurochs died in Jaktorów Forest, Poland. Their most important gameplay function is that they can be [[Taming|tamed]] to become a [[cow]] or a [[bull]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you attack them. If you pull their hair there is a good chance they'll get angry and attack you, though. ''Note: Now, aurochsen spawn in herds, and directly attacking one will lead to the whole herd attacking you. Herd doesn't react if an aggro was result of pulling a hair.'' ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:2  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Low Horn Swipe}} {{:Mule Kick}} {{:Bucking}} {{:Thunder Over}} {{:Sudden Charge}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Roar of the Wild}} |} ===Strategy=== With around 60 UA/60 AGI you should be able to take on an aurochs and win by punching the crap out of it. As of w14, aurochsen have been given same amount of armor that boars have, likely necessitating a melee weapon. They've got a lot of HP and AGI and their firepower is not to be scoffed at. Don't let yourself get overwhelmed and focus on damage-dealing. Archery on horseback or from a boat is a good option early on. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Wild Beef]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *Right click the Aurochs and it will give you the option to "Pull Hair." Use this with extreme caution as it can equally get you a new Curiosity or a dead Hearthling. Each Aurochs yields only one [[Aurochs Hair]]. *You can get 2L of [[Milk]] from an aurochs. To do so, you need [[Clover]] and a [[Bucket]]. Pick up a clover from your inventory and right click on an aurochs. The aurochs will cautiously proceed to eat the clover. It is recommended to get as close as possible before right clicking the aurochs. Next, take your bucket and right click on the aurochs to milk it. Each Aurochs can only be milked once. *'''Trivia:''' The singular noun is aurochs while the plural is aurochsen. - Loftar *The singular is obviously "aurochs", seeing how it is cognate with English "ox". Accordingly, the only reasonable plural form is, of course, "aurochsen", seeing how "ox" still uses the Saxon plural form "oxen" even in Modern English. *Terrains on which {{PAGENAME}} can spawn, but rarely: [[Deep Tangle]], [[Timber Land]], [[Wald]]. {{Game Development}}

''Note: Now, aurochsen spawn in herds, and directly attacking one will lead to the whole herd attacking you. Herd doesn't react if an aggro was result of pulling a hair.''

[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Wild Beef]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
sections.Notes:
*Right click the Aurochs and it will give you the option to "Pull Hair." Use this with extreme caution as it can equally get you a new Curiosity or a dead Hearthling. Each Aurochs yields only one [[Aurochs Hair]].
*You can get 2L of [[Milk]] from an aurochs. To do so, you need [[Clover]] and a [[Bucket]]. Pick up a clover from your inventory and right click on an aurochs. The aurochs will cautiously proceed to eat the clover. It is recommended to get as close as possible before right clicking the aurochs. Next, take your bucket and right click on the aurochs to milk it. Each Aurochs can only be milked once.
*'''Trivia:''' The singular noun is aurochs while the plural is aurochsen. - Loftar
*The singular is obviously "aurochs", seeing how it is cognate with English "ox". Accordingly, the only reasonable plural form is, of course, "aurochsen", seeing how "ox" still uses the Saxon plural form "oxen" even in Modern English.
*Terrains on which {{PAGENAME}} can spawn, but rarely: [[Deep Tangle]], [[Timber Land]], [[Wald]]. <!--(This data can't be nicely integrated in the currently RoB's system)-->

sections.Strategy:
With around 60 UA/60 AGI <del>you should be able to take on an aurochs and win by punching the crap out of it.</del> As of w14, aurochsen have been given same amount of armor that boars have, likely necessitating a melee weapon.

They've got a lot of HP and AGI and their firepower is not to be scoffed at. Don't let yourself get overwhelmed and focus on damage-dealing. Archery on horseback or from a boat is a good option early on.

Badger.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(5/11)-(-5/11)round6}} | | baseq = 30 | | hp = 250 | fhp = 150 | | loot = [[Fresh Badger Hide]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]], [[Raw Badger]] x4, [[Bone Material]] x3, [[Scent Gland]] x0-1 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Badgers are short-legged omnivores, distant relative to [[Otter]] and [[Wolverine]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Very aggressive in a certain radius to everyone, including other animals. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:3 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Sudden Charge}} {{:Badgering}} | {{:Careful Approach}} |} ===Strategy=== Badgers are slightly tougher than [[Fox]]es, between them and [[Wolverine]]s. The easiest method to kill one, is to use a sling on the badger while on the other side of a large cliff. Make sure the cliff is wide enough so that the badger will not pathfind around it. Then, after hitting the badger until it runs from low health, break combat with it. Let the badger run away, then find it again. Make sure the move speed is set to sprint, and finish the badger off with unarmed or a few more hits of the sling. If you can take on a badger you can also take on an [[otter]], [[stoat]] and [[beaver]], and probably a [[mouflon]], but you'll want to strengthen up a bit before you take on bigger game like [[Aurochsen]] or [[Boar]]s due to high HP and armor. If a badger and a fox fight you can help out the fox and kill the badger as the badger is a bit tougher, and then kill the wounded fox afterwards if you want. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Badger]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *If a [[Badger]] and a fox are in close proximity, they will initiate combat and begin to fight one another. Invariably, the Badger will kill the fox. This can be useful for newbies to get an early source of fox materials. It is also possible to initiate combat with the badger while it is fighting the fox, if you start the fight early enough you and the fox together can quite easily kill it. ==Gallery== Badger Sett.jpg | Badger dungeon entrance. {{Game Development}}

sections.Strategy:
Badgers are slightly tougher than [[Fox]]es, between them and [[Wolverine]]s. The easiest method to kill one, is to use a sling on the badger while on the other side of a large cliff. Make sure the cliff is wide enough so that the badger will not pathfind around it. Then, after hitting the badger until it runs from low health, break combat with it. Let the badger run away, then find it again. Make sure the move speed is set to sprint, and finish the badger off with unarmed or a few more hits of the sling.

If you can take on a badger you can also take on an [[otter]], [[stoat]] and [[beaver]], and probably a [[mouflon]], but you'll want to strengthen up a bit before you take on bigger game like [[Aurochsen]] or [[Boar]]s due to high HP and armor.

If a badger and a fox fight you can help out the fox and kill the badger as the badger is a bit tougher, and then kill the wounded fox afterwards if you want.

Bat Wings - not to be confused with the [[Bat Wing]] cape - are gathered by butchering a [[Dead Bat]].

==How to Acquire==

Kill a [[Bat]] and butcher the corpse with a [[Sharp Tool]]. Bat wings do not need to be dried before use.

== Additional Uses==

Bat Wings are used to craft the [[Bat Wing]] cape, and is also a [[Curiosity]] which can be used in the [[Study Report]] to earn [[Learning Points]]. When placed on a herbalist table, they become dried bat wings which can be eaten to gain agility.

Bat wings do not count as an animal hide.

{{Game Development}}

Bear.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-10/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 60 | | armor = 65 | hp = 850 | fhp = ~500 | | loot = [[Fresh Bear Hide]] x2, [[Intestines]] x6, [[Entrails]] x4, [[Animal Fat]], [[Raw Bear]] x20, [[Bone Material]] x12, [[Bear Tooth]] x1-5 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Bears are generally one of the toughest animals you will see in the Hearthlands. They are big, bulky, strong, and faster than they look. They can be seen occasionally wandering in forests. Bears are territorial and are likely to attack you if you get too close. They can easily take down a weaker Hearthling in a few swipes of their mighty claws, so you should give them a wide berth. They won't attack other creatures but will get attacked by [[Wolf|Wolves]] and will generally win a fight against them. Bears have a decent chance to [[execute]] you if they KO you. More specifically they will often smack you once while you're down which will deal 100-150 damage and then leave you alone. If your CON is 100 or more you might survive. The pelt, teeth and meat have various unique recipes like the [[Bear Cape]], [[Bear Tooth Talisman]] and multiple sausages. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== They will attack you if you get close. Very strong with high damage, they can easily kill you if you aren't fit for the task. They are very fast and can only be barely outran by sprinting. If you are attacked by one you should look for a grassland or another area where you can sprint. Try to make it to a river or body of water, as they cannot swim. If you are in the middle of a forest there is little to be done. After receiving roughly 500 damage, bears will briefly flee from you, and then enter a rage mode. In this rage mode, the bear's eyes will light up with fire and the ferocity of the bear will be unmatched. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:6 ! Restorations:3  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Chomp}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Bear Hug}} {{:Shredding Paw}} {{:Dark Heart Move}} | {{:Unstoppable}} {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Bears have very high combat stats and easily shrug off most attacks. Their openings increase slowly so they generally focus on the offense, only restoring themselves when they get high. Due to their 65 armor they're comfortable standing and delivering even when against a blade. Most of the time, both your and the bear's strategy will be the same. Increase openings a ridiculous amount and decimate with a massive attack. At 60+ openings bears easily deal 100 damage per attack and throw out hits quickly. In Rage Mode they have a special attack, Bear Hug, which MAXES OUT ALL YOUR OPENINGS. As such they should only be fought in a group where you can shake off its attack onto a friend or with a kiter's advantage IE on horseback or near a cave you can duck into. If on horseback they may aggro and attack your horse while you ride it. Cleave is really your only option. Quick barrage or take aim until you have a lot of IP, at least 30-50, then continue quick barrages and use Opportunity Knocks to raise their red opening as high as possible. Optionally use Flex, Parry or something similar to increase their blue opening for extra damage. Then give it your all and go all out with Cleave until you've run out of IP or the bear reduced its openings, then kite it and reduce yours and rinse and repeat until dead. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Bear]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bear Tooth]] and [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *To get to you, bears will destroy any objects obstructing their path with lower than Soak 25, meaning only [[Palisade]]s and [[Brickwall]]s will stop their blood lust. {{Game Development}}

They'll occasionally spawn in some shallow water but can rarely be seen hanging out in the middle of forests too. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Like Swans they might attack you on very rare occasion if you get all up in their grill but generally they won't take to your presence. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! They're pretty weak but their attacks are fast and can hurt. If you can kill a badger, you'll have an even easier time with a beaver. Beaver Lodge.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xobst = 999 | yobst = 999 | | dng_animals = [[Beaver King]], [[Big Brown Beaver]], [[Elder Beaver]] }} [[Category:Dungeon]] [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] [[{{PAGENAME}}]]s are [[Dungeon]]s found within bodies of water. {{PAGENAME}}s are where beavers lodge, and that is to say vast, sprawling, underwater complexes of rooms, corridors, and great halls, laden with beavers and things beaver. They are the first instance of a new type of natural phenomenon which are generally called "Dungeons". =Getting through the lodge= Before entering the lodge, you should be aware that the difficulty level is random and once inside you won't be able to teleport to your hearthfire even if you get knocked out. If you get low on health, run for the exit.
Some rooms are flooded, so if you aren't confident in your swimming abilities you should bring a [[coracle]] to avoid drowning. Each room has an objective that you must complete to unlock the next room. These can include killing all beavers, killing x many beavers, or making sure a certain amount of beavers are dead at the same time before more spawn.
There is usually a main branch of rooms which leads to the king and side branches which lead to treasure chests. Chests contain a random assortment of loot including gems, curios, wild dreams, gildings, bone needles.
At the end of the main path you will find the beaver king. Beavers never stop spawning in the final room. Once the king is dead the lodge will start to shake and create a new exit to the surface nearby, sometimes if the exit is near a wall it will spawn in an adjacent room. Once you are outside the lodge will collapse and respawn somewhere else in the world.
Defeating [[Beaver King]] trigger [[Experience Points#Fall of The Beaver King|Fall of The Beaver King]] event and spawn [[Heart Container]] somewhere in final room. ==Beavers== BrownBeaver.png|frame|[[Big Brown Beaver]] - common and strong beaver in the Dungeon, slightly stronger than ordinary beaver. 400 Hp WhiteBeaver.png|frame|[[Elder Beaver]] - rare and strongest beaver in Dungeon. 1000 Hp BeaverKing.png|frame|[[Beaver King]] - a Dungeon Boss. 2500 Hp ==Doors== BeaverDoorLocked.png|frame|Locked door. BeaverDoorOpened.png|frame|Opened door. BeaverDoorBlocked.png|frame|Sometimes door can be blocked by dam. Dam can de destroyed with Destroy option. *Doors unlocked after you defeat all beavers in a room. ==Chests== BeaverChest.png|frame|A chest with loot. ==Other== BeaverLog.png|frame|A log of beaver salvage. Chopping it yields blue blocks which can be used in construction. =Notes= Indi.png|Inside a beaver lodge. Beav.jpg|Dead beavers inside a beaver lodge. Beav2.png|A player fighting with giant beavers in the lodge. They're bigger and stronger than what you can find in the wild. Bloc.png| A windmill with a roof made of [[Beaver Salvage]]. 1111111.jpg| A great hall with a roof made of [[Beaver Salvage]]. Knar.png|A knarr made with blocks of [[Beaver Salvage]]. {{Game Development}}

Beehive Dungeon.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xobst = 4.816 | yobst = 3.103 | | dng_animals = }} [[Category:Dungeon]] [[{{PAGENAME}}]]s are [[Dungeon]]s found sometimes when raiding ordinary [[Wild Beehive]]s. They are filled with tough creatures, treasure, and a boss at the end. You cannot hearth home while inside a Dungeon, '''even if you get KOed,''' so if you do, your only choice is to wait until you get up, and then sprint for the exit. All dungeon enemies ''cannot'' [[Execute]], but they can repeatedly KO you and kill you via a Concussion and have '''no armor.''' =How to Find= ''Occasionally when you raid [[Wild Beehive]]s, they may reveal themselves to be more than they first appeared.'' - Jorb There is a small chance (around 2%) for a wild beehive you raid to begin shaking, and after raiding it will grow on the ground becoming a {{PAGENAME}}. {{PAGENAME}}s are full of [[Large Bee]]s with varying degrees of strength, Bee-related loot, [[:Category:Forageable Plants|Forageable Plants]], [[Small Heart Container]]s, and rarely [[Seed of Tree or Bush|Seeds of Trees or Bushes]]. The {{PAGENAME}} is a sight to behold as the player is swept into a wholly unique, albeit small, world. These are the fourth and latest type of [[Dungeon]] that has been introduced to the game. =Getting Through the Beehive= The beehive is composed of hexagonal rooms of various sizes. The rooms branch out in different directions, with a flame room at the end of each branch. There are 5 types of rooms within the dungeon: Entrance: The entrance to the dungeon can never spawn enemies, so if you enter and there are enemies waiting for you, someone has already been there and lead them to the entrance. Eliminate: These rooms have all except the door you entered from locked. To unlock them, you need to wipe out all enemies that spawned in the room, at which point none more can spawn, and all doors will be unlocked. Charge: These rooms have a static, unbreakable stone totem with a glowing orange gauge in the middle and will spawn bees until the room is completed. The gauge charges as you kill bees, and once it reaches the top, the room will complete, all doors will unlock and bees will stop spawning. [[File:Flame Room.png|thumb|alt=Room with green flame|Room with green flame]] Flame: These rooms contain no enemies or loot chests, and have only one door. The only feature is a pillar in the center of the room with a flame burning in it. The flame may be in one of three states: * Extinguished - clicking the pillar produces the message "It looks like there used to be a flame burning here." * Green - clicking the pillar produces the message "There is a cool flame burning here." * Red - clicking the pillar may turn the flame green, or spawn a swarm of enemies. Flames need to be turned green in a specific order. When the correct red flame is clicked, the flame turns green and some non-green flames in the dungeon may change their state (ie. a red flame may become extinguished or vice versa). When the wrong one is clicked, a number of large bees spawn and immediately attack the player, and all flames reset to their initial state. The order for a given dungeon is fixed and doesn't change, but the only way to figure it out seems to be trial and error. Boss Room: This large room contains the dungeon boss and several other bees. The door to the room will be locked until all flame pillars in the dungeon have been turned green. ==The Boss== The Bee Queen is capable of dealing damage and speed similar to a boar. However, she has high health of 1000 or more, and is guarded by many of her subjects, especially Royal Guard bees. She also periodacally summons larvae to fight alongside her. Unfortunately for her, she is extremely slow, also similar to a boar, and can be easily outrun on Run speed. If you have at least 2 people, you can allow one person to get the attention of her minions and allow the other to fight her. She can be kited without issue, and has no armor, so all it takes is a good few cleaves to take her down. Due to her large size, she sometimes gets stuck in doorways. ==The Escape== Once the boss is defeated, anyone who dealt damage to her gains the [[Experience Points#Buzzkill|Buzzkill]] event that increases the speed of raiding wild beehive permanently for that character, and a [[Small Heart Container]] will spawn near the center of the boss room. Afterwards, the entire dungeon will begin shaking violently. Not to worry, an exit will open up in the boss room. Make sure you carry the queen's corpse out. After all hearthlings have exited, the dungeon will continue to visibly shake for a few minutes, then collapse into the ground, never to be seen again. ==The Spoils== Within the dungeon you can find high quality forageables and tree seeds, which can help to jumpstart your treeplanting and gardening industry. There is additionally a small chance you can find a lower tier [[Sugar diamond]]s. You can also find high quality [[Beeswax]], [[Bee Larvae]] which can be used for build [[Beehive]]s. The queen herself will give around 20 [[Bee Chitin]], 50 [[Bee Meat]], 15 entrails, and her signature [[Bee Crown]] and [[Bee Queen's Wings]], both of which have high gild chances. The wings in particular can actually be worn in the backpack slot, the only other equipment to do so other than of course backpacks being the [[Hussar's Wings]]. ==Strategies and Tips== '''Before entering the Hive by Right-clicking on it, the player must be aware that the difficulty level inside is random.''' The [[Bees]] inside are somewhat similar to [[Ant Hill Dungeon]] and fairly weak compared to the inhabitants of the [[Bat Cave|Bat Cave Dungeon]]s and [[Beaver Lodge|Beaver Lodge Dungeon]]s, thus making them easier to fight. Most wounds you receive from them can be healed with [[Yarrow]].
It is suggested to enter an {{PAGENAME}} with a [[Horse]] that is [[Rope|Leashed]]. Upon entering the dungeon, you will be able to have your hearthling Right-click on the [[Horse]], and choose from the flower-menu the option ``Giddyup!`` to mount it. There will be times where the player's [[Horse]] may become stuck within narrow confines of a dungeon, but this should be easily solved with some careful maneuvering. Some [[Combat moves|Combat Moves]] that will be efficient in such dungeons are [[Combat moves|Side Swipe]] and [[Combat moves|Storm of Swords]], though keep in mind that these two require [[Abbreviations|IP]] (Initiative Points). Moves that do not require [[Abbreviations|IP]] and are helpful in an {{PAGENAME}} are [[Combat moves|Quick Barrage]] and [[Combat moves|Full Circle]]. Chests contain a random assortment of loot including [[Sugar diamond]]s, [[Beebread]], [[Bee Meat]], [[A Wild Dream|Wild Dreams]] and [[Flower]]s. ==Bees== Below are images of the various types of enemies you will find inside an {{PAGENAME}}. ==Doors== Ordinary unlocked doors will unlock after you defeat all bees in a room. ==Chests== These are the various bee-themed containers found within an {{PAGENAME}} that contain loot for the player to collect. =Notes= *You can bring a [[Horse]] that is on a [[Rope|Leash]] inside a Dungeon. {{Game Development}}

Beetroot.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Windsown Weed]] | producedby = [[requires::Beetroot Crop]] | specific = Tuber | | energy = 100 | hunger = 2 | cha = 0.2 | per = 0.5 | | sat1 = Vegetables }} [[Category:Seeds]] Beetroots can be eaten raw, planted as a crop, or used as an ingredient for various cooking recipes. *Beetroots can be planted to produce 1-3 Beetroots and [[Beetroot Leaves]] *Beetroots fills 20 units of the [[hunger]] bar, gives 0.5 [[:Attributes#Perception|perception]] and 0.2 [[:Attributes#Charisma|charisma]] [[:Getting_Started#Increasing_Stats|FEPs]] at quality 10 ==Uses== *[[Steak & Tubers]] *[[Tuber Mash]] *[[Fat-Braised Veg]] *[[Red-Shred Salad]] *[[Sweet Beets]] ==How to Acquire== *found while drying [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Herbalist Table]] or [[Drying Frame]] *Harvesting a planted beetroot. ==How to Use== In order to plant {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. ==Notes== *While harvesting beetroots, you can very rarely acquire a [[Weird Beetroot]] [[curiosity]]. *Remember to pick [[Beetroot Leaves]] from the ground after you harvest. {{Game Development}}

'''Harvesting''' *RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest. *SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from. ==Growing== {{PAGENAME}} growing speed affected by [[Bee Skep]], [[Credos#Farmer|Farmer Credo]] and hidden ground spots. ===Stages=== {{PAGENAME}} have four stages of growth: [[File:Beetroot_Crop_Stage_1.png|100px]] Stage 1: The beetroot field just planted. *To plant beets you must first create a [[Terraforming|Plowed Field]]. 
*LEFT CLICK the crop itself in your inventory and then RIGHT CLICK on the plowed field you wish to plant them on. 

'''Harvesting'''
*RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest.
*SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from.

[[Category:Vegetables]]

[[Beetroot Leaves]] are a byproduct of harvesting the [[Beetroot]] crop, along with [[Beetroot]]s.

[[Beetroot Leaves]] are required for making a [[Stinging Salad]], and can be used as [[Salad Greens]] in recipes such as [[Chicken Salad]]. 

The [[Beetroot Leaves|Leaves]] can also be used as [[Swill|fodder]] in [[Food Trough]]s, [[Rabbit Hutch]]es and [[Chicken Coop]]s.

Eating the leaves on their own does not give you any [[FEP]]s, but will add to the player's [[FEP#The_Variety_Bonus|Variety Bonus]].

[[Category:Home Decor]]

A Blood Stern is a red flower growing in the forest, where they can be picked. 

You need at least 1250 PER*EXP to be able to start spotting them in the world. 

It is used in the making of [[Bandit's Mask]] and [[Mud Ointment]].

Has no real life equivalent but is implied to be spicy by its use in [[Hot Wings]].

==Notes==
Can be planted in a [[Garden Pot]] after completing level 1/5 [[Credos#Gardener|Gardener credo]].

Blueberries can be eaten by right clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 75% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Intelligence]] in [[FEP]]s.

Furthermore, you need blueberries as an ingredient for [[Blueberry Pie]] and [[Sacrebleu]].

==How to Acquire==

Blueberries grow wild on [[Forest Terrain]]. If you have the [[Foraging]] skill and happen across one, you can harvest it. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].

Blueberries are very common, a Perception*Exploration value of 5 is enough to find it.

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Blueberry Pie Dough}}

Blueberry Pie, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[food]] fills 200% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 4 [[INT]] + 3 [[PER]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the oven, then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as blueberry pies, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ashes]]. Time of making is about 20 minutes.

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

Boar.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-7/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 40 | | armor = 15 | hp = 450 | fhp = ~275 | | loot = [[Fresh Boarhide]], [[Entrails]] x2 [[Intestines]] x3, [[Animal Fat]], [[Raw Boar]] x8, [[Bone Material]] x4, [[Boar Tusk]] x 1-2 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Boars are a quite common sight in forests, and may be [[Taming|tamed]] to become [[Pig|domesticated pigs]]. Boars also stand out as being one of the tougher creatures due to their aggressive nature and the likelihood of encountering them on forest terrain. Fortunately, boars are slower than most other animals as their fastest movement speed is somewhat slower than a player's running speed, making it possible to outrun them given enough time and stamina. Boars have a decent chance to [[execute]] you when they KO you. Boars have a natural armor that absorbs all damage below 15. This armor cannot be broken and ignores armor piercing. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== There is a good chance that it will attack you if you get too close. Fortunately it is well known that boars are one of the few animals which run slower than a Hearthling's third speed. (Except in very rare cases where they may run fast enough to require you to sprint.) Use this to your advantage if you need to flee from them or when trying to hunt them. If they begin to run away from you, you can punch them from behind for maximum damage. For an easy kill against boars, sling rocks at them from a boat, and then when they start to run you can hop out and chase them down. === Strategy === Boars are a great benchmark for your combat prowess. By the time you can reliably kill boars without too much damage you know you're ready to pretty much kill anything save for Mammoth or Trolls with a bit of cheese, and with a great weapon and armor set you can stand and deliver to most animals you'll see. Boar fight with ferocity. They attack faster than you might expect and deal big opening and big damage. They prefer to spam Mule Kick to raise your yellow, then Bear Down to weaken you up and gain IP. Then they might Mule Kick more for reliable damage dealing or Bristle to keep themselves safe, and occasionally they're go all out and use Blood & Gore for a mighty whooping. '''Always remember Boars might decide to KILL and EAT you!''' Blood & Gore can catch you off guard if you aren't paying attention and send you on your ass from a 50 damage smack. You should be constantly kiting them. Be on horseback for a far easier time. Keep all openings low, they can exploit all of them. Since they have 15 armor attacks like Punch are useless. Quick Barrage should be spammed, but it won't deal good damage unless you have a good sword; it's more to gain IP. If you've got good stats but a weak weapon like a Tinker Axe, you should rely on creating big openings and wasting their IP so that they can't Bristle. Full Circle is good if you have a nice sword, but if you're using an Axe you should use Cleave or perhaps Raven's Bite, and only use them when you've made big openings. Using strong moves is pretty much the key to success. Just whittle them down. A huge issue with Boars when using anything other than a good sword is that they will occasionally decide they don't like the hand of cards they've been dealt and '''erase all their openings.''' They do this by suddenly and randomly spamming a Red and Yellow restoration that gives 1 IP 3, 6 or even 9 times, and then spamming Bristle, removing all openings. They generally do this once you've caused any opening to go beyond 30%. They might repeat this once, twice, and on rare occasions, infinitely until all openings are at 10 or lower. The only real counter play is to swiftly attack and take the damage they'll deal or heavily outstat them in Agility allowing you to deal out damage far faster than they can spam their moves. If you're at 20 or lower Agility they can use a move once per second and essentially remove all openings multiple times per minute leading to a horrifically drawn out fight. One good setup is to spam Quick Barrage to stack IP and then use Takedown or Storm of Swords once or twice, leaving them at ~50 Red and Yellow openings. Proceed to spam Full Circle until one of you are dead or they remove all their openings. Alternatively, f you have a really nice sword and good stats/armor you can just spam quick barrage/full circle or sideswipe without much thought. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:3 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Mule Kick}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Blood & Gore}} | {{:Bristle}} |} ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}}hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Boar]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Boar Tusk]] and [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *To get to you, boars will destroy any objects obstructing their path with lower than Soak 25, meaning only [[Palisade]]s and [[Brickwall]]s stop boars from chasing you. {{Game Development}}

Fortunately it is well known that boars are one of the few animals which run slower than a Hearthling's third speed. (Except in very rare cases where they may run fast enough to require you to sprint.) Use this to your advantage if you need to flee from them or when trying to hunt them. If they begin to run away from you, you can punch them from behind for maximum damage. For an easy kill against boars, sling rocks at them from a boat, and then when they start to run you can hop out and chase them down.

[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Boar]].

{{Game Development}}

Boar fight with ferocity. They attack faster than you might expect and deal big opening and big damage.

They prefer to spam Mule Kick to raise your yellow, then Bear Down to weaken you up and gain IP. Then they might Mule Kick more for reliable damage dealing or Bristle to keep themselves safe, and occasionally they're go all out and use Blood & Gore for a mighty whooping.

'''Always remember Boars might decide to KILL and EAT you!''' Blood & Gore can catch you off guard if you aren't paying attention and send you on your ass from a 50 damage smack.

You should be constantly kiting them. Be on horseback for a far easier time. Keep all openings low, they can exploit all of them. 

Since they have 15 armor attacks like Punch are useless. Quick Barrage should be spammed, but it won't deal good damage unless you have a good sword; it's more to gain IP. If you've got good stats but a weak weapon like a Tinker Axe, you should rely on creating big openings and wasting their IP so that they can't Bristle. Full Circle is good if you have a nice sword, but if you're using an Axe you should use Cleave or perhaps Raven's Bite, and only use them when you've made big openings. 

Using strong moves is pretty much the key to success. Just whittle them down.

A huge issue with Boars when using anything other than a good sword is that they will occasionally decide they don't like the hand of cards they've been dealt and '''erase all their openings.''' They do this by suddenly and randomly spamming a Red and Yellow restoration that gives 1 IP 3, 6 or even 9 times, and then spamming Bristle, removing all openings. The only real counter play is to swiftly attack and take the damage they'll deal or heavily outstat them in Agility allowing you to deal out damage far faster than they can spam their moves. If you're at 20 or lower Agility they can use a move once per second and essentially remove all openings multiple times per minute leading to a horrifically drawn out fight.

One good setup is to spam Quick Barrage to stack IP and then use Takedown or Storm of Swords once or twice, leaving them at ~50 Red and Yellow openings. Proceed to spam Full Circle until one of you are dead or they remove all their openings.

Board.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 4 | | skillreq = [[requires::Carpentry]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Log]] | producedby = [[requires::Bone Saw]] or [[requires::Metal Saw]] | specific = Sculptable Material }} The quality of the boards depends on the quality of the [[Trees|tree]], the quality of your [[Metal Saw]] or [[Bone Saw]], and your [[Abilities#Carpentry|Carpentry]] skill level. ==How to Acquire== Right click on a [[Log]] while having either a [[Metal Saw]] or [[Bone Saw]] equipped and the option "Make boards" appears. A metal saw can make more boards out of one log than a bone saw. However, it is often easier to get a high quality bone saw than it is to get an equal quality metal saw, so sometimes a bone saw is still desirable when you want quality Boards. ==Quality== Your Carpentry skill acts as a softcap for board quality. The formula for board quality is as follows: \operatorname{_{q}Board} = \sqrt{_{q}Saw * _{q}Wood} If (Carpentry < board quality) then \operatorname{_{FinalQ}Board} = \frac{_{q}Board + Carpentry}{2} Example (q10 Tree, q50 saw, 3 Carpentry) board ql = sqrt(50 * 10) board ql = 22.36 board ql = (22.36 + 3) / 2 board ql = 25.36 / 2 board ql = 12.68 board ql = 12 ==Variations== Board-acacia.png | [[Acacia Tree|Acacia]] Board-alder.png | [[Alder Tree|Alder]] Board-almond.png | [[Almond Tree|Almond]] Board-apple.png | [[Apple Tree|Apple]] Board-ash.png | [[Ash Tree|Ash]] Board-aspen.png | [[Aspen Tree|Aspen]] Board-baywillow.png | [[Bay Willow Tree|Bay Willow]] Board-beech.png | [[Beech Tree|Beech]] Board-birch.png | [[Birch Tree|Birch]] Board-birdcherry.png | [[Birdcherry Tree|Birdcherry]] Board-blackpine.png | [[Black Pine Tree|Black Pine]] Board-blackpoplar.png | [[Black Poplar Tree|Black Poplar]] Board-heath.png | [[Tree Heath Tree|Briarwood

sections.Quality:
Your Carpentry skill acts as a softcap for board quality. The formula for board quality is as follows:

<math>\operatorname{_{q}Board} = \sqrt{_{q}Saw * _{q}Wood}</math>

If (Carpentry < board quality) then <math>\operatorname{_{FinalQ}Board} = \frac{_{q}Board + Carpentry}{2}</math>


Example (q10 Tree, q50 saw, 3 Carpentry)

board ql = sqrt(50 * 10)

board ql = 22.36

board ql = (22.36 + 3) / 2

board ql = 25.36 / 2

board ql = 12.68

'''Boiled Odds''' is a [[Food]].

==How to Acquire==

You need a cauldron or clay cauldron with at least 0.3L (verify) of boiling water to make a Boiled Odds. The resulting item quality is influenced by the quality of the water and the ingredients, and is softcapped by cooking and perception. Firewood does not play a role in the quality formula.

{{Game Development}}

Boiled Pepper Drupe.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Farming]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Peppercorn]]
| producedby = [[requires::Cauldron]](with 0.5 liters)
}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|B$oil Pepper Drupes}}

Boiled Pepper Drupe is a food ingredient created by boiling [[Peppercorn]]. The boiled drupes must be then dried into [[Dried Pepper Drupes]] on an [[Herbalist Table]] and either ground into [[Black Pepper]] or put in a [[Peppermill]] before they can used. The quality of the boiled pepper drupes is [[softcap]]ped by the [[Perception]] and [[Cooking]] skills. The quality of the cauldron and water used also determines the final outcome.

==How to Acquire==

You need a cauldron with at least 0.5 liters of hot water to make a boiled pepper drupe.

==Notes==

Boiled Pepper Drupes disappear when dropped.

==Formulas==

<!-- Intentional 3 line spacing between formulas. -->
* Base quality: <math>\frac{_{q}Peppercorn*3 + (_{q}Cauldron + _{q}Water)/2}{4}</math>

* [[softcap]]ped by: <math>\sqrt[2]{Perception*Cooking}</math>

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Seafood]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|S$eafood|Bo$iled River Pearl Mussel}}

A boiled [[River Pearl Mussel]]. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] fills 1% of the [[hunger]] bar, and gives 2 [[Dexterity]] and 1 [[Psyche]] [[:Getting_Started#Increasing_Stats|FEPs]].

==How to Acquire==

You need a [[Clay Cauldron|clay]] or [[Metal Cauldron|metal cauldron]] with at least 1 liters of hot water to make a boiled mussel. The resulting item quality is influenced by the quality of the water and the mussel, and is [[softcap]]ped by cooking and perception. Firewood does not play a role in the quality formula.

==Notes==

Every time you boil a mussel, there is a chance that you will obtain a [[River Pearl]] too.

== Quality ==

The formula that seems to work is:

Boiled River Pearl Mussel Q = <math>\frac{_{q}RiverPearlMussel * 6 + _{q}Cauldron + _{q}Water}{8}</math>

Softcapped by <math>\sqrt{{Cooking}*{Perception}}</math>.

{{Specific-property-text|i2=3}}
... '''Actually''' Bone Material is a general in-game-object. (''same-name conflict'')

Bone Material is required by several things, such as [[Bone Arrow]]s, [[Bone Hook]]s and [[Bone Saw]]s.

==How to Acquire==

All animals contain Bone Material. Killing and butchering an animal will result in a quantity of bone material dependent on the type of animal see [[Creatures]] for more information.

Do note that animal specific materials such as [[Moose Antlers]], [[Deer Antlers]], [[Boar Tusk]]s, [[Bear Tooth|Bear Teeth]], [[Crab Shell]]s, [[Bog Turtle Shell]]s and [[Wishbone]]s also count as Bone Material, so be careful when crafting with these items in your inventory

Boreworm.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature
| xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}}
| yobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}}
|
| baseq = 20
|
| armor = 31
| hp    = 1200
| fhp   = 800
|
| loot  = [[Fresh Boreworm Hide]], [[Intestines]] x5,[[Entrails]] x3, [[Boreworm Meat]] x4, [[Boreworm Beak]] x1
}}

[[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]]

''"Added "Boreworms", creature. Not to bore you with the details, but these tunnelling nematodes now inhabit the lower reaches of the cavernous underworlds. They can pop up, and down, and quite generally cause more than a bit of a nuisance to the poor, unsuspecting cave dweller who chances upon them. Their hides take a fair bit of time to dry, but can be used as leather when dry, without further processing required." ~ Jorb''


Boreworms are rare underground creatures, that can be spawned naturally or from [[Loose Rock]] when destroying it or mine around 7 tiles it.

They are known to hide alone or in a group of 2-3 worms by burrowing in the underground tiles, and as a player walks by them, they will unburrow all at once and immediately attack him.

==Combat==

===Behaviour===

Aggressive, instantly attack upon being mined out. 

Can instantly interrupt combat interaction by burrowing.

Once burrowed, they wait between 3-20 seconds before jumping out and either waiting idle or attacking the player again.

====Combat Moves====

{| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"
! Attacks<small>:2</small>
! Restorations<small>:2</small> 
|- style="vertical-align: top;"
| {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Chomp}}
| {{:Bristle}} {{:Careful Approach}}
|}

===Strategy===

They do not have any special attacks and their only advantage is their high Hitpoints value. They pose no threat to a player on horse, with at least basic combat knowledge and relatively low stats (~100 FEPs and unarmed or melee combat)

Keep in mind, that Horses are faster than Boreworms.
==Notes==
*[[Boreworm Hide|Dried Boreworm Hide]] acts like a single piece of leather for any recipe requiring it.
*During combat, Boreworms tend to burrow and break aggro. It is not uncommon for them, to jump out and not aggro the player again, causing them to stand still and ignore the player they have fought just a moment ago. It is unknown if this is a bug.

{{Game Development}}

Branch.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = none
| objectsreq = None
| producedby = [[requires::Tree]] (must be sufficiently grown)
| specific   = Wicker
}}

==How to Acquire==
Right click on a sufficiently grown tree and select the appropriate option.

You can also obtain branches by getting a [[Block of Wood|block of wood]], right clicking and choosing split, you will then get 5 branches.

Qualities of branches obtained from [[Block of Wood|block of wood]] depend both on quality of [[Stone Axe|axe]] and quality of [[Log|log]] using formula: <math>\sqrt[2]{Axe*Block}</math>


To get high quality branches you need to grow high quality tree, doing so by using a good quality [[treeplanter's pot]], good quality soil, water, and a seed used to sow a tree, repeat this with the tree you grew to get higher quality branches. Keep in mind that most trees have 7 branches.

{{Game Development}}

{{Infobox metaobj
| skillreq   = [[requires::Winemaking]]<!-- Might seems logical but is probably "None". As its the still that's making the brandy. A hearthling just has to fill the still and light it. No skill required for that I figure. -->
| objectsreq = [[requires::Wine]]
| producedby = [[requires::Still]]
}}

[[Category:Alcohol]]
[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

Brandy is an alcoholic drink acquired by distilling [[Wine]] in a [[still]]. 

Brandy buffs the effects of [[Food Satiations|Cheese]] and [[Food Satiations|Offal]] by 0.5%, as well as [[Food Satiations|Sweets & Desserts]] by 1.0% per gulp at [[Quality|Q10]]. 

Each drink will also add a negative satiation against itself, thus granting diminishing return on the buff.

== How to Acquire ==
Build a Still and fill it with [[wine]] and fuel. The [[Wine]] turns to {{PAGENAME}} as its cooking slowly filling up the small container. In order to halt the {{PAGENAME}}'s further progression into [[Vinegar]], one must store it in a barrel or a [[Wine Bottle]].

== How to use ==
There is no preferred vessel for {{PAGENAME}} yet.

== Drinking ==

== Quality ==
The quality of the Brandy is [[Softcap]]ed by the quality of the Still.
The used fuel quality has no effect.

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Bread Dough}}

Bread is a [[Food|food]] and, more specifically, a [[:Category:Baked Goods|Baked Good]], meaning you will need the [[Baking]] skill and access to an [[Oven]] to cook. Once baked, it can be eaten by right-clicking and selecting the 'Eat' option. Each provides 500% of [[Energy]].

==How to Acquire==

Gather [[Barley Flour]] or [[Wheat Flour]] and [[Water]] to make [[Bread Dough]]. Place the dough into an [[Oven]]. [[Fuel]] the Oven with 4 [[Branch]]es and light it. The Bread will be finished in 4 [[tick]]s, or approximately 16 minutes and 40 seconds.

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]

Brown Kelps can be found on a beach or in the shallow and deep near the ocean.

==How to Use==

Brown Kelp are used as [[Thatching Material]] for buildings.

They can also be used as fodder in [[Food Trough]]s and in [[Chicken Coop]]s.

==Notes==

* Spawning as clusters.
* Can be stored in a [[Stockpile]] of 35 pcs.

Bucket.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 2 | yitem = 2 | | skillreq = [[requires::Carpentry]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Board]] x2 | producedby = Nothing | | liquid_size = 10 | | slot = 6L or 6R }} {{GM|$Craft|$Clothes & Equipment|$Vessels & Liquids|$Bucket}} A Bucket is a container that can be used to transport and store any liquid 10 liters at a time, such as [[Water|water]], [[Honey|honey]], [[Milk|milk]], [[Wort|wort]], [[Grape Juice|grapejuice]], [[vinegar]], [[Wine|wine]], [[Tea|tea]], [[Beer|beer]] and [[Rennet|rennet]]. The bucket can also hold up to 10kg of any powder, including [[flour]], [[ashes]] and [[cavedust]]. The bucket may also hold up to 1000 of any type of seed. If equipped, the bucket will automatically collect seeds from the crops you harvest, as long as they are of matching quality. You can manually fill the bucket with seeds of varying quality though this will have an impact on all of the seeds in the bucket. It should also be noted that you cannot use a bucket at the same time you are using a scythe. To fill a bucket, left click on the bucket in your inventory or equipment screen, then right click on an object to fill the bucket. Only empty buckets can be carried in the inventory; filled bucked must be equipped in the 5L or 5R slot. It takes 10 full buckets to fill a [[barrel]]. ==Notes== * For most stuff that can be put in a barrel or bucket, there is an Empty option. This option is not available for, for example, ashes, black pepper, cave dust, Pipestuff. * When a hand-held bucket is needed for crafting. ==How to Acquire==

{{PAGENAME}} is acquired from butchering a [[Cleaned Bullfinch]].

==Quality==

*The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of animal corpse and [[softcap]]ped by your [[survival]] and [[sharp tool]] [[quality]].
**If (ToolQ < MeatQ ) then <math>MeatQ = (MeatQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
**If (Survival < CorpseQ) then <math>RawMeatQ = (MeatQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
*Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

Bushcraft Fishingpole.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| caption    = No line/hook » No hook » Fully equipped
|
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 2
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Fishing]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Tree Bough]] x1; [[requires::Block of Wood]] x1, [[requires::String]] x2; [[requires::Fishline]], [[requires::Fish Hook]], [[requires::Bait]]
| producedby = Hand
|
| slot       = 6L or 6R
}}

[[Category:Fishing Equipment]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Clothes & Equipment|Hook, $Line & Sinker|Bushcra$ft Fishingpole}}

The {{PAGENAME}} is, as the name suggests, a tool used to catch fish, one of the staple food source early on for beginners. It must be equipped, occupies one hand, and is used by right-clicking an area where fish are jumping. <!-- This usually will necessitate being in a boat, though in some instances it is possible to fish from shallow water on shore. --> For more information check the [[Fishing]] page.

==How to Acquire==

Create a pole with the crafting recipe. (This requires you to have gathered and discovered a [[Tree Bough]], a [[Block of Wood]], and [[String]]. String is most easily found by foraging spindly taproot or stinging nettle. See [[Foraging]].)

Now, you need to add a line, a hook, and some kind of bait before you can use the fishing pole. You do so by crafting a [[Fishline]], a [[Fishing#Fish Hook]], and gathering [[Bait]], independently.


'''[[Hook]]'''

The most accessible hook is the [[Bone Hook|bone hook]], which is made from [[Bone Material|bones]], easily obtained from animals, particularly [[Chicken|chickens]] and [[Squirrel|squirrels]]. The [[Metal Hook|metal hook]] requires a [[Nugget of Common Metal]].

'''[[Fishline]]'''

The easiest line is [[Bushcraft Fishline]], crafted from any two pieces of [[String]]. The easiest early game strings are [[Stinging Nettle]], [[Cattail Fibres]], [[Bark Cordage]] and [[Spindly Taproot]]. Those strings (except the nettle) are also used in [[Shoreline Fishline]] and [[Woodsman's Fishline]], so check the [[Fishline]] category for more info.

'''[[Bait]]'''

Anything that seems like it would be good fishing bait, odds are it can be used as bait. You can use the [[Entrails|entrails]] from that rabbit or chicken you killed for the hook earlier for an easy choice. Others include [[ants]], [[Leech|bloated leeches]], [[Dragonfly|dragonflies]], [[Earthworm|earthworms]], [[Grub|grubs]], [[Ladybug|ladybugs]], [[Silkmoth|silkmoths]], ... Bait will be lost whenever you catch a fish, but will be replaced automatically from bait in your inventory while fishing, so make sure you don't have anything you want to lose in there.


To equip the fishing pole with fishing line and other necessities, left click on the item to select it and then right click it onto the fishing pole. If you want to swap something out, you can take it apart again by right clicking on the fishing pole and selecting Unequip, which will return all the parts to your inventory.


<!--
==Quoting Loftar==

"The lure, hook and line each have a 1% chance of breaking each time you fish. (Except, at least for now, when using baits instead of lures, in which case only the bait is lost.)"
-->

Butcher's Surprise.title: Butcher's Surprise

Butter.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Animal Husbandry]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Milk]] (1.0 liters)
| producedby = [[requires::Churn]]
| specific   = Solid Fat
}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|Ch$urn Butter}}
[[{{PAGENAME}}]] is an ingredient for a variety of baked goods.

==How to Acquire==

Have at least 1 liter of [[milk]] in your inventory, right-click on a [[churn]], and execute the recipe.

==Quality==


Butter quality appears to follow the formula: <math>\frac{_{q}Churn + (_{q}Milk * 3)}{4}</math> and then softcapped by crafter's [[Cooking]] and [[Perception]].

Where <math>\operatorname{_{q}Churn} = \frac{_{q}AvgBoard + _{q}AvgBlock + _{q}AvgBough}{3}</math>

==Food Input Types==

If you churn mixed milk that has even 0.01 liters added from other type of milk, resulting "generic" milk turns into equally "generic" butter with no special Food Input type. This can be desirable, at least/specifically in the case of [[Butter-steamed Cavebulb]] that gives pure PSY [[FEP]]s when made from generic/mixed milk.

[[Category:Baked Goods]]
[[Category:Foods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Butter Scones}}

Butter Scones, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[food]] fills 500% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 3 [[DEX]] + 2 [[INT]] in [[FEP]]s.

Butter Scones made from wheat flour add a lot of CON FEPs, butter scones made with barley flour add a decent amount of STR FEPs.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the oven, then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as butter scones, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ashes]]. Time of making is 16 minutes 40 seconds.

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Garden Greens|Butter-steamed $Cavebulb}}


Butter-steamed Cavebulb is a food that grants mostly [[Psyche]] [[FEP]]s, and as such is highly sought after by high end crafters.

==How to Acquire==

You need a cauldron to make a butter-steamed cavebulb. Add water & fuel to the cauldron, light it and execute the recipe.

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

Cachalot.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(20/11)-(-20/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(97/11)-(-97/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 120 | | armor = 150 | hp = 50000 | fhp = Varies | | loot = [[Cachalot Blowhole]],
Cachalot Chunk x16:
[[Raw Cachalot]] x8 (128 total), [[Animal Fat]] x4 (64 total),
Cachalot Heart:
[[Raw Cachalot]] x16 (25% higher q),
Cachalot Skull:
[[Hvalraf]] x20-25, [[Cachalot Tooth]] x2-10,
Cachalot Carcass:
[[Whale Bone Material]] x40 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Cachalot are large toothed whales living in [[Deep Water|Oceans]]. They're very fast, strong and durable high-end creatures which parts give access to following recipes: [[Cachalot Charm]], [[Whaler's Jacket]], [[Whalebone Clasps]] and [[Whalebone Scrimshaw]]. To butcher a creature, you must first tow it to the [[Knarr]] or [[Snekkja]] and take it to the shore, secondly drag on the ground with the help of a horse. ==Spawn== There is only 3{{verify}} whales can be alive in the world, spawning mechanic is similar to dungeons - whales will not despawn naturally, and after one is killed, next one will spawn in next 8 RL hours (1 in-game day). ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Usually not aggressive, but will attack anybody in the area if already in combat. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:0 ! Restorations:0  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | | |} ===Strategy=== Beware, Cachalots are very strong creatures, capable of outrunning and breaking a [[Knarr]] or [[Snekkja]]. ==In-Game Example(s)== ==Notes== *Dead Cachalot can be hooked to knarr and lifted on сoast. Then dragged on land by the horse. *Butchered in several pieces that can be lifted. *The [[Cachalot Blowhole]] is softcapped by the quality of the cutting tool first used to butcher the cachelot. *The Cachalot chunks are only softcapped by tool when butchered directly, not when the whale is butchered. *Chunks and Skull not decay naturally and can be used as decoration forever. *Neither the heart or skull can be stored in a vehicle's cargo {{Game Development}}

*Neither the heart or skull can be stored in a vehicle's cargo

[[Category:Flowers]]
[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]

Camomile has healing properties when eaten, and can heal a few, scattered points of certain wounds.

Camomiles can be eaten by right clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 250% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1.5 [[Will]]+2 in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Camomile is a healing herb which can be found in [[Flower Meadow]] and rarely on swamp biomes.
Hardcapped by [[Survival]].

'''Note''': grows only in spring.

==Use==

Can be placed into a herbalist table to dry them in order to create [[Camomile Compress]] or eaten raw to instantly heal light wounds.

==Gallery==

Candleberry.rawWikitext: {{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Foraging]] | terrain = [[terrain::Bog]], [[terrain::Fen]], [[terrain::Swamp]] | specific = Berry | | perexp = 160 | | energy = 75 | hunger = 1 | cha = 3 | | sat1 = Berries }} [[Category:Forageable Plants]] [[Category:Foraged Foods]] ==How to Acquire== Candleberries can be found in any of the [[Swamp|swamps]]. If you happen across one, and have the foraging skill, you can harvest it. There is no known way to purposely produce Candleberries, so they can only be found by chance and by increasing exploration and perception skills. The lowest reported [[Perception]] * [[Exploration]] score to spot Candleberries has been 160 (16 EXP, 10 PER). ==How to Use== The main product that utilizes Candleberries is [[Candleberry Wax]]. This is the main source of [[Wax]] in the early game, before farms and [[Bee Skep]]s become abundant. ==Other Uses== Candleberries are edible and can be used in recipes that require berry or fruit. Aside from using them accidentally (or in a niche type of food, depending on food system), this is presumably most relevant for Forager [[Credo]], that can give a quest to eat one. Warning: Candleberries can be used as fodder for Chicken Coops and Food Troughs! Be careful if using shift-right click to add fodder. ==Gallery== Candleberry_ingame.png | Candleberry ingame

| specific   = Tuber
|

| hunger     = 1.5
| per        = 1
|
| sat1       = Vegetables
}}

[[Category:Seeds]]
[[Category:Vegetables]]

Carrots can be eaten raw, planted as a crop, or used as an ingredient for various [[Cooking]] and [[Baking]] recipes. This [[food]] fills 150% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Perception]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Harvest a [[Carrot Crop]] at the third or fifth stage of growth. Harvesting at the third stage provides exactly one carrot. Harvesting at the final stage provides two or more carrots based on character skills.

==How to Use==

To plant a carrot or carrot seed, left-click it, then right-click a plowed field.

==Notes==

[[Carrot]]s have two stages of harvesting. Their fourth stage results in seeds when harvested, and their fifth and final stage result in 1-3 Carrots, which can also be planted. If using the [//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?t=40400 Amber client] with crop stage indicators enabled (ctrl + p), the forth stage is denoted by a yellow dot over the crop, while the fifth stage is indicated by a red dot.

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Carrot Cake}}

Carrot Cake, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[food]] fills 500% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 7 [[Perception]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as carrot cakes, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will either turn into [[ashes]] or be unfinished.

The carrot cake will be ready in approximately 16 minutes 40 seconds.

Rarely spawn when picking [[Lamp Stalk]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Very aggressive in small radius. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:6 ! Restorations:5  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Bear Down}} {{:Chomp}} {{:Dark Heart Move}} {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Shredding Paw}} {{:Tail Splash}} | {{:Bristle}} {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Dive & Hide}} {{:Rampant Rage}} {{:Unstoppable}} |} ===Strategy=== Easy kill with any ranged weapon from ground, they can't reach you. Carefull for their [[Tail Splash]] attack, it creates large blue openings and can be used up to five tiles away. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be scaled for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Scales]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Scaled {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Cave Angler Light]], [[Fishy Eyeball]] and [[Filet of Cave Angler]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *[[Lamp Stalk]]s rarely reveal themselves to be [[Cave Angler]]s (even in shallow water). *Cave Anglers move faster than a [[Rowboat]]. *Can't leave water terrain. *Not affected by any [[Credos]]. {{Game Development}}

[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
sections.Notes:
*[[Lamp Stalk]]s rarely reveal themselves to be [[Cave Angler]]s (even in shallow water).
*Cave Anglers move faster than a [[Rowboat]].
*Can't leave water terrain.
*Not affected by any [[Credos]].

{{Game Development}}
sections.Strategy:
Easy kill with any ranged weapon from ground, they can't reach you.

Carefull for their [[Tail Splash]] attack, it creates large blue openings and can be used up to five tiles away.

Cave Louse.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(3/11)-(-3/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(7/11)-(-7/11)round6}} | | baseq = 40 | | armor = 50 | hp = 1000 | fhp = 300 | | loot = [[Cave Louse Chitin]] x2-5, [[Cave Louse Leg]] x0-5, [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x3, [[Cave Louse Meat]] x4 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Cave lice are large and tough creatures lurking in caves. They normally spawn in groups of around 5 to 12, though group sizes may be as low as 1 depending on how tight the space around the spawn area is. ==Combat== Cave Louse are slow but have very strength, agility, and HP. They hit for 50 damage with only 30% openings and like to spam Fell Scratch and Bear Down to gain IP, and then use Bristle. Due to their numbers, massive HP and high damage fighting them should be avoided at all costs as their only loot is chitin, which has no use other than [[Scarab Talisman]], and high quality chitin is easily acquired from ant or bee dungeons. ===Behaviour=== Cave lice are rarely aggressive, though initiating an attack on a louse will also get the attention of other nearby lice. Due to their numbers, a group of them can knock a player out very quickly. Lice start to flee at 300 HP, but may return to combat immediately if other lice continue to be attacked. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Chomp}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Sudden Charge}} {{:Blood & Gore}} | {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Use extreme caution when fighting a group of lice. It is recommended to fight them using a horse. They can destroy [[Mine Support|mine supports]] in their path, possibly triggering a [[Mining#Safety_and_Cave-ins|cave-in]]. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be broken for [[Cave Louse Chitin]] and [[Cave Louse Leg]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Cave Louse Meat]].
==Notes== *To get to their target, lice will destroy any objects obstructing their path with lower than 25 [[Glossary#Soak|soak]]. {{Game Development}}

==How to Acquire==

The [[Cave Louse Leg]] can be obtained by Right-clicking the [[Cave Louse]] corpse and selecting the ''Break'' option from the flower menu. You will obtain between 0 to 5 [[Cave Louse Leg|Legs]].

==Quality==

The [[Quality]] of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by the [[Quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|Softcapped]] by your [[Sharp Tool]]'s [[Quality]] and [[Survival]].

#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>LegQ= (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>LegQ= (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool [[Quality]] softcap is applied before the [[Survival]] softcap.

This [[:Category:Foods|food]] fills 175% of your [[energy]] bar, and gives hard to get [[Attributes#Psyche|Psyche]] [[FEP]]s.

Cavebulbs are used in [[Butter-steamed Cavebulb]] and are highly sought by players, thus they can be valuable in terms of trading.

==How to Acquire==

Cavebulbs are found in [[Cave|caves]], with a fairly high [[Foraging#Exploration|exploration]] and [[Attributes#Perception|perception]] requirement.

Get your Perception * Exploration over 525 and you will begin to see them.

==Notes==

Can be planted in a [[Garden Pot]] after completing level 2/5 [[Credos#Gardener|Gardener credo]].

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]
[[Category:Mushrooms]]

A type of edible mushroom. Chantrelles can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 75% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Dexterity]] in [[FEP]]s.

== How to Acquire ==

Chantrelles can be found in [[Forest Terrain|forests]]. If you happen across one, and have the foraging skill, you can harvest it. You need at least 52 [[Perception]] * [[Exploration]] score to spot Chantrelles. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].

==Gallery==

<gallery>
Chantrelles_ingame.png | Chantrelles ingame.
Chantrelles_in_Garden_Pot.png | Chantrelles in Garden Pot.
</gallery>

==Pronunciation==

"Shan-ter-elle"

The "Ch" is soft, and the "an" can be pronounced like "on" or "an", depending on accent.

NOT pronounced "shan-tra-lay".

[[Category:Guide]]
== Cheesemaking ==
Fill a [[Curding Tub|curding tub]] with [[milk|cowsmilk]], [[milk|goatsmilk]] or [[milk|sheepsmilk]] (don't mix the milks!) and [[rennet]]. As long as the tub has at least 1.0L of milk and 0.02L of rennet, it'll produce one [[Cheese Curd]] every 36 minutes (real-time). Curd can be either eaten for 1 strength FEP or used to fill a [[Cheese Tray|cheese tray]]. Cheese trays, filled with 4 curds and placed on a [[Cheese Rack|cheese rack]], will slowly turn into cheese. Stage 1 cheese can be further aged into stage 2 cheese, and so forth (refer to flow chart below). The time of each stage is dependent upon the type of cheese being made.

=== Location ===
Which of the four locations the rack resides in determines which type of cheese is produced:
* '''Cabin''' or '''inside''' means inside a [[:Category:Housing|house]] on any floor (except '''Cellar''')
* '''Cellar''' means inside a [[Cellar]] of any building.
* '''Outside''' refers to outside area on the world surface.
* '''Mine''' refers to outside area underground in either natural [[cave|caves]] or [[mine|mines]].

Only latest inner location affects a product. E.g. if cheese rack is placed inside ''cellar'' of a log ''cabin'' inside some ''mine'' then its current location is determined as ''cellar''

=== Types ===
*[[Cow Cheese|Cow Cheeses]]
*[[Sheep Cheese|Sheep Cheeses]]
*[[Goat Cheese|Goat Cheeses]]
Below you can find cheese stage charts with locations and estimate time required to obtain next stage and product's stats.
All other sequences than mentioned in the schema will return [[Generic Gouda]] in {{time|G&Rhm|9|0|0}}. Please see specific cheese page for detailed info (precise timing etc.)

==FEP information==
See [[:Category:Cheeses|Cheese category]] page for per-cheese FEP table.

==Cow cheese tree==
{{shadowbox|img2|CheeseTree.png|caption=Cheese Tree}}

==Sheep cheese tree==
{{shadowbox|img2|5D7lp0J.png|caption=Cheese Tree}}

==Goat cheese tree==
{{shadowbox|img2|Goatcheese_Table.png|caption=Cheese Tree}}

==Notes==
*Mixing goat, sheep, aurochs and cow milk will make all milk for that curding batch only make [[Generic Gouda]].
*Wild Aurochs milk will only create [[Generic Gouda]].
*Slicing cheese from a cheese tray has a small chance of producing [[The Perfect Hole]].
*Quality of cheese is [[Softcap|softcapped]] by cheese tray for every stage.
*As of [//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?f=39&t=71694 Raw Trucker]: "The quality of cheese racks no longer matters for cheese quality. Tray quality still matters. You. Are. Welcome." - Jorb

{{Game Development}}

| wil2       = 4
|
| sat1       = Cheese
| sat2       = Sweets & Desserts
}}

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Cheesecake}}

{{PAGENAME}}, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[Food]] fills 500% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 3 +2[[WIL]], 2 [[CHA]] and 3 [[PER]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ash]].

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

The type of curd used in the recipe does NOT affect the resulting FEPs. However, the type of flour does. Barley flour removes -0.2 CHA FEPs, but adds 0.7 STR FEPs. Wheat flour removes -0.6 WIL +2 FEPs, but adds 1.8 PER FEPs. Though wheat flour adds more total FEPs than barley, it removes valuable about 20% of +2 WIL FEPs from the resulting cake.

{{Game Development}}

Chicken Egg.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = none
| objectsreq = [[requires::Chicken]]
| producedby = [[requires::Chicken Coop]]
| specific   = Egg
}}

An egg laid by a [[chicken]]. Required by several cooking recipes, can be eaten [[Fried Egg|fried]] or [[Boiled Egg|boiled]].

==How to Acquire==
Chicken eggs are laid by hens in a [[Chicken Coop|chicken coop]]. It takes roughly 24 hours for a hen to lay an egg. There needs to be at least one empty space below the hen, otherwise the egg won't appear.

If there is a [[cock]] in the coop with the hen, the produced egg will be fertile and will hatch into a [[chick]]. Fertile eggs need to be brooded by a hen in order to hatch. To be brooded, the egg needs to be in a space anywhere below the hen. A fertilized egg can be moved, but if it's been without a brooding hen for too long, the hatching process will stop. This will "kill" the egg, effectively turning it into unfertilized.

Fertilized eggs show a timer counting up to 100%, this indicates when a [[chick]] is going to hatch. It takes {{time|G&Rhm|06|0|0}}, or about 2 days (real time), for a [[chick]] to hatch from an egg. (be aware that the timer takes some time to show up on freshly laid fertilized eggs)

==Quality==
New eggs that are layed will have a quality of the average qualities of the hen and the cock, ±5.

==Trivia==
Because removing a fertilized egg doesn't immediately stop its hatching timer, chicks sometimes hatch inside a cauldron while boiling eggs.

==How to Acquire==

{{PAGENAME}} is acquired from butchering a [[Cleaned Chicken]].

==Quality==


The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

==Variable Ingredients==

As a raw meat/poultry ingredient, {{PAGENAME}} can be used in a variety of recipes. The table below shows the amount of FEPs added/removed from the original recipe's FEPs. Use it when considering which meat makes the best use of this ingredient for the stat you want to raise. 

Make sure to consider individual meat types can be used in specific recipes that other meat types cannot ({{PAGENAME}} can be used to make [[Chicken Chorizo]] on top of the below recipes, for instance). 

Also take note other required ingredients and possible spices in recipes can change the final FEP values (example: [[Onion Skewer]] will also consider what type of onion you're using).

Clover.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Foraging]] | objectsreq = None | terrain = [[terrain::Bounty Acre]], [[terrain::Flower Meadow]], [[terrain::Hard Steppe]], [[terrain::Highground]], [[terrain::Leaf Patch]], [[terrain::Moor]], [[terrain::Red Plain]] | specific = Flower | | perexp = 130 }} [[Category:Flowers]] [[Category:Forageable Plants]] [[Category:Home Decor]] Can be fed to a [[Aurochs]] or [[Mouflon]] or [[Wildgoat]] to attain [[Milk]] or [[Wool]] or [[Mohair]] without having to domesticate the beast. To milk an Aurochs, feed it a clover and then pick up a bucket and right-click in on the Aurochs. Each clover gives you 2 liters of milk. To sheer a Mouflon, feed it a clover and then right click on it and select 'sheer'. Each clover gives you 1 wool. You have to equip a sharp tool to be able to sheer ([[Stone Axe]] works fine). Wildgoats work the same as Mouflon, '''except they are aggressive'''. Wildhorse do not provide material resources, but can be temporarily ridden until they run out of Pony Power (dismount at 5% to be safe, at 0% they throw you off their backs, dealing SHP damage.) To feed an animal a clover, for temporary usage and/or [[Taming]]: # Dismount any horse or vehicle, as they will block the animal from reaching you. # Grab up a clover from your inventory and right-click it onto the animal. # Wait for the animal to come to you. This may include the animal jumping about and incrementally approaching you. Don't move or click anywhere, or the process will be cancelled. # Once the animal gets all the way to you, they will either take the clover or not. # Once they have eaten the clover, they will slowly walk away, that's the time to try and leash them or 'work' on them with sheers, etc. # Grab the rope from your inventory and right click the animal # The only visual indicator of the leash success is the slight change to the image of the rope itself. The rope must be placed back into your inventory. # The animal will follow you as best as it can. If it's blocked from following you, eventually the leash will break. ==How to Acquire== Clovers can be found on [[Grassland|Grassland Terrains]]. Get your [[Perception|(Perception]] x [[Exploration|Exploration)]] over 130 and you will start seeing some. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]]. ==Quality== The quality of the Clover is is [[Hardcap|hardcapped]] by [[Survival]]. The quality of the product obtained is equal to the quality of the clover used to feed the animal [[Softcap|softcapped]] by \sqrt{Charisma*Survival} Shearing a [[Mouflon]] will further softcap it by \sqrt{Dexterity*Sewing}
Milking an [[Aurochs]] will further softcap it by \sqrt{Perception*Cooking}
==Gallery== Clover_ingame.png | Clover ingame. Clover - (on wall).png | A clover used as a wall decoration. Clover_in_Garden_Pot.png | Clover in Garden Pot. {{Game Development}}
