Cock.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature
| xitem = 2
| yitem = 2
| xobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}}
| yobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}}
|
| baseq = 10
|
| hp = 10
| fhp = 10
|
| loot = [[Dead Chicken]]: [[Chicken Feather]] x3, [[Raw Chicken Meat]], [[Entrails]], [[Bone Material]] or [[Wishbone]](Rarely)
}}

[[Category:Domesticated]]
[[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]]

Cocks are the male equivalent of [[Hen]]s, and are required for producing fertilized [[Chicken Egg]]s.

==How to Acquire==

There are two possibilities:

*Wild flocks of Chicken may randomly spawn on [[Grassland]], and [[Moor]]. Right-clicking a Cock will cause your character to chase it. Once caught, it will appear in your inventory.

*Build a [[Chicken Coop]] for breeding Chickens and Cocks. When a [[Chick]] is placed inside a Chicken Coop with sufficient fodder, it will turn into a [[Hen]] or Cock.

==How to Use==

*[[Animal Husbandry]] enables you to breed Chickens. A [[Chicken Coop]], a Cock and a [[Hen]] are required to produce fertile [[Chicken Egg]]s, that will hatch into [[Chick]]s.

*A Cock can be slaughtered via the "Wring Its Neck" option which will convert it into a [[Dead Chicken|Dead Cock]].

*If killed via combat, it will leave a [[Dead Chicken|Dead Cock]] as its corpse.

*Cocks require food, and if left in your inventory/storage will die, leaving a low quality [[Dead Chicken|Dead Cock]].  This will occur within 24 hours

==Notes==

*If you do not have a [[Chicken Coop]] or fodder, you can keep your chickens alive longer than 3 days by releasing them and catching them again.

==Gallery==

<gallery widths=160px heights=120px>
Cockingame.png
</gallery>

sections.Quality:
* [[{{PAGENAME}}]] Quality = <math>\frac{_{q}Bread+ _{q}Vegetables+ _{q}Fruit}{3} </math> and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[3]{Intelligence*Perception*Lore}</math>.


* '''Stats''' = <math>_{q10}Stats*\sqrt{\frac{_{q}Item}{10}}</math>.
sections.Notes:
* Due to the games preference to select fruit before berry. It can/will skip additional fruit-only items after already selected two items that are both fruit and berry.
* - The games items scanning order is <span title="Last In First Out">[//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LIFO LIFO]<span> related. ie: Last placed is first scanned.
* Although non-direct-edibles can be used for crafting cornucopia's (''or other foods''). They are ignored in the final product (''kinda acting as blank crafting fillers'').
sections.Trivia:
From Wikipedia:

''In classical antiquity, the cornucopia, from Latin cornu (horn) and copiae (abundance)), also called the horn of plenty, was a symbol of abundance and nourishment, commonly a large horn-shaped container overflowing with produce, flowers, or nuts.''

''Baskets or panniers of this form were traditionally used in western Asia and Europe to hold and carry newly harvested food products. The horn-shaped basket would be worn on the back or slung around the torso, leaving the harvester's hands free for picking.''

"Symbol of abundance and nourishment" seems to contrast the game's version quite hard, which is quite easy to make and extremely bad.

{{Game Development}}

{{GM|$Craft|C$uriosities|C$ornucopia}}

A '''Cornucopia''' is a combination item that can be eaten, studied as a curiosity, or even placed on a table as a [[Symbel]] bonus. 

It is exceptionally awful in all those regards.

It requires bread, berries, and fruits to make.

==Quality==

* [[{{PAGENAME}}]] Quality = <math>\frac{_{q}Bread+ _{q}Vegetables+ _{q}Fruit}{3} </math> and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[3]{Intelligence*Perception*Lore}</math>.


* '''Stats''' = <math>_{q10}Stats*\sqrt{\frac{_{q}Item}{10}}</math>.

== Notes ==
* Due to the games preference to select fruit before berry. It can/will skip additional fruit-only items after already selected two items that are both fruit and berry.
* - The games items scanning order is <span title="Last In First Out">[//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LIFO LIFO]<span> related. ie: Last placed is first scanned.
* Although non-direct-edibles can be used for crafting cornucopia's (''or other foods''). They are ignored in the final product (''kinda acting as blank crafting fillers'').

==Trivia==

From Wikipedia:

''In classical antiquity, the cornucopia, from Latin cornu (horn) and copiae (abundance)), also called the horn of plenty, was a symbol of abundance and nourishment, commonly a large horn-shaped container overflowing with produce, flowers, or nuts.''

''Baskets or panniers of this form were traditionally used in western Asia and Europe to hold and carry newly harvested food products. The horn-shaped basket would be worn on the back or slung around the torso, leaving the harvester's hands free for picking.''

"Symbol of abundance and nourishment" seems to contrast the game's version quite hard, which is quite easy to make and extremely bad.

{{Game Development}}

{{infobox creature
| xitem    = 2
| yitem    = 1
| xobst    = {{#expr:(1/11)-(0/11)round6}}
| yobst    = {{#expr:(1/11)-(0/11)round6}}

}}

[[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]]

A Crab is a beach-dwelling crustacean that can be cracked for [[Crab Shell]]s, [[Crab Meat]], [[Crab Roe]] and [[Crab Claw]]s. It takes up a 2x1 space in the inventory when alive. They are found on [[Beach]] terrain.

Both picking up and cracking Crabs has a large chance to give you the [[Wounds|Crab Carresed]] wound, causing 3-6 HHP damage per wound.

==How To Acquire==

Crabs can be found commonly on beaches. They do not engage in combat and do not flee. Unlike [[Hedgehog]]s, it is not possible to engage crabs in combat to avoid the damage from picking them up, as there is no Dead Crab item (You simply crack them, which serves as killing and butchering them.) and killing them through combat/trampling with a horse simply deletes them from the game world entirely.

==Notes==

Wearing gloves, such as [[Poor Man's Gloves]], reduce the damage, and chance at taking damage dealt by crabs.

==Crabs In-Game==

[[File:Crab in_game.png]]

==Quick Facts==

* Desperate Hearthling who has lost their will to live can build up Crab Caressed wound incredibly fast by repeatedly picking and releasing single crab.

{{Game Development}}

Crab Roe is an edible item found sometimes when cracking open [[Crab]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Crack open a [[Crab]] for a chance to get Crab Roe.

==Quality==

Crab Roe quality is based on the crab that was cracked open, [[softcap|softcapped]] by the player's [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]] ability.

Dandelion is a [[Curiosity]] which can be used in the [[Study Report]] to earn [[Learning Points]].

A staple of most newbies' LP gains.

==How to Acquire==

They are commonly found in every [[grassland]] biome, and more rarely in specific types of [[forest]]. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].

[[Hardcap]]ped by [[Survival]].

Dead Chicken.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 2
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Cock]], [[requires::Hen]]
| producedby = Nothing
}}

Right-click and select 'Pluck' to receive three [[Feather]]s and a [[Plucked Chicken]].

==How to Acquire==
To get a dead chicken all you have to do is right click on a Chicken in your inventory and select "Wring Neck"; or you can simply kill a live chicken, which is running around on [[Grassland]] or [[Moor]].

==Quality==
* Products quality result from quality of the [[Dead Chicken]] and it's [[Softcap]]ped by highest [[Farming]] or [[Survival]].
* Further butchering of the products will have them successively [[Softcap]]ped more if the skill level is below the quality of the products. 
* The Sharp Tool used has no effect.

{{PAGENAME}} can be gathered from a Dog Rose Bush. They can be eaten or planted to grow more Dog Rose Bushes.

Plant them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] with 4 [[Soil]] and 1 liter of [[Water]], then placed them on an [[Herbalist Table]] to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

{{PAGENAME}} are a simple [[Agility]] food produced by drying [[Bat Wings]].

==How to Acquire==

Place [[Bat Wings]] on an [[Herbalist Table]]. They will turn into [[Dried Batwings]] in 3 game days or 24 hours realtime.

==Quality==

The quality is the average between the Herbalist Table and Wings.


<math>Dried Batwings= \frac{TableQ + Bat Wings}{2}</math>

{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Plant Lore]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Morels]]
| producedby = [[requires::Herbalist Table]]
| specific   = Any Mushroom
|
| energy     = 200
| hunger     = 1
| str        = 1.5
|
| sat1       = Mushrooms
}}

{{PAGENAME}} are a simple [[Strength]] food produced by drying [[Morels]].

==How to Acquire==

Place [[Morels]] on an [[Herbalist Table]]. They will turn into [[Dried Morels]] in 3 game days or 24 hours realtime.

==Quality==

The quality is the average between the Herbalist Table and Morels.


<math>Dried Morels= \frac{TableQ + Morels}{2}</math>

| xitem      = 1

| objectsreq = [[requires::Boiled Pepper Drupe]]

}}

An ingredient required to make [[Black Pepper]]. Created by drying [[Boiled Pepper Drupe]]s on an [[Herbalist Table]].

==Usage==
You can grind 5 {{PAGENAME}}s into (0.03-0.05)kg Black Pepper at a [[Quern]]

==How to Acquire==
Place the boiled drupes on the herbalist table and wait 5 in-game days (roughly 37 real-time hours).

{{Game Development}}

Dried Pepper Drupe.title: Dried Pepper Drupe

sections.How to Acquire: Obtain 2 [[Tree Bough]]s, 5 [[Branch]]es and 2 pieces of [[String]], then build the frame.
sections.How to Use:
Right-click the built frame, place item into it, then wait for them to dry.

A [[Wheelbarrow]] can also be used to place fresh hides on drying frames. (''but not to pickup dried hides'')
sections.Notes: * (Terrain) A drying frame can't be build on Ice.
sections.Quality:
* Drying Frame Build Quality: <math>\frac{_{q}Bough+ _{q}Branch+ _{q}String}{3}</math>

* Quality of Drying Frame affect speed of drying [[Prepared Animal Hide|Hides]], [[:Category:Dried Filet of Fish|Dried Filet of Fish]] and [[Wild Windsown Weed|WWW]].

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center"
!Quality
!Speed %
|-
|10
|100
|-
|80
|200
|-
|270
|300
|}
* Speed % = <math>100*\sqrt[3]{\frac{_{q}Drying Frame}{10}} </math>
sections.What is it:
A Drying Frame is used to dry fresh [[:Category:Fresh Animal Hide|hides]] from animals. Dried hides are further [[Tanning Tub|tanned]] to produce [[Leather|leather]] or crafted into an item.
The drying frame can also be used to dry [[Wild Windsown Weed]]s (WWWs), but it takes significantly more time than on a [[Herbalist Table]]. You will obtain 5 seeds of crops that have seeds, or one unit of crops that don't (such as [[Beetroot]]s or [[Yellow Onion]]s)

Note: A drying frame is destroyed within two decay hits and cannot be built inside. It is better build on a claim to prevent decay. When a drying rack turns red, it is in risk of being destroyed and should be repaired with a single piece of string.

Drying rack quality does not affect the quality of skins or WWWs dried upon it, but it does affect their speed of drying.

Drying Frame.title: Drying Frame

Dusk Fern.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Foraging]]
| producedby = Hand
| terrain    = [[terrain::Cave]]
| specific   = Leaf, Salad Greens
|
| perexp     = 150
|
| energy     = 75
| hunger     = 1
| wil        = 1
}}

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]

'''Dusk Fern''' is [[Foraging|foraged]] plant. Assuming base 10 [[Perception]], you need 15 [[Exploration]] for a chance to see them.

==How to use==

While edible raw, Dusk Ferns are most useful as crafting material, counting both as [[Leaf]] and [[Salad Greens]]. Without access to crops like Lettuce and Beetroot, Dusk Ferns are one of the best options.

==Where to find==

They spawn in the first cave layer, but only near natural entrances. 

Mountains have an abundance of natural entrances and can therefore host a decent-sized tunnel network where Dusk Ferns spawn all over the place.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
Dusk Fern_ingame.png | Dusk Fern in-game.
</gallery>

{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Cleaned Eagle Owl]]
| producedby = [[requires::Sharp tool]]
| specific   = Poultry, Raw Meat
}}

==How to Acquire==

{{PAGENAME}} is acquired from butchering a [[Cleaned Eagle Owl]].

==Quality==

The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

sections.Notes:
*The game doesn't explicitly differentiate between "Any Mushroom" and "Edible Mushroom". The only in-game way is to open crafting window with a recipe requiring edible mushroom(s) and see if the recipe highlights the mushroom being tested.
*[[Cave Lantern]] is inedible when raw, but can be used in all "edible mushroom" -recipes.
*[[Stalagoom]] is inedible except for characters with Cave hermit [[Credo]]. Few recipes specifically asking for Stalagoom are available to craft normally.

Egg Cake.rawWikitext: {{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Baking]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Unbaked Egg Cake]] | producedby = [[requires::Oven]] | | energy = 600 | hunger = 2 | agi = 3 | int = 5 | | sat1 = Fowl & Poultry | sat2 = Bread }} [[Category:Baked Goods]] [[Category:Foods]] {{GM|copy=Unbaked Egg Cake}} Egg Cake, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[food]] fills 600% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 5 [[INT]] + 3 [[AGI]] in [[FEP]]s. ==How to Acquire== Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the oven, then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as egg cakes, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ashes]]. Time of making is 16 minutes 40 seconds. ==Variable Ingredients== Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type. {| class="wikitable" |+Flour/Milk |- !Type !colspan="2"|FEP Variation |- |- |- |rowspan="2"|Aurochs Milk/Barley | + 0.7 | [[STR]] |- | - 0.2 | [[AGI]] |- |rowspan="2"|Aurochs Milk/Wheat | - 0.8 | [[INT]] |- | + 2.4 | [[CON]] |- |rowspan="3"|Sheepsmilk/Barley | - 0.2 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1 | [[INT]] |- | + 1.65 | [[DEX]] |- |rowspan="4"|Sheepsmilk/Wheat | - 0.5 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1.6 | [[INT]] |- | + 1.8 | [[CON]] |- | + 2 | [[CHA]] |- |rowspan="3"|Goatsmilk/Barley | - 0.2 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1 | [[INT]] |- | + 1.65 | [[WIL]] |- |rowspan="4"|Goatsmilk/Wheat | - 0.5 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1.6 | [[INT]] |- | + 1.8 | [[CON]] |- | + 2.1 | [[DEX]] |- |rowspan="3"|Cowmilk/Barley | + 1.65 | [[STR]] |- | - 0.2 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1 | [[INT]] |- |rowspan="3"|Cowmilk/Wheat | - 0.5 | [[AGI]] |- | - 1.6 | [[INT]] |- | + 3.9 | [[CON]] |- |} ==Gallery== 3deggcake.png | {{PAGENAME}} (in{{Nbh}}game). {{Game Development}}

| per        = 0.1
|
| sat1       = Berries
}}

{{PAGENAME}} can be gathered from an [[Elderberry Bush]]. They can be eaten or planted to grow more Elderberry Bushes.

Plant them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] with 4 [[Soil]] and 1 liter of [[Water]], then placed them on an [[Herbalist Table]] to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

A delicious [[Sausages|sausage]] made from deer meat and moose meat. All sausages require [[Sausage Making]] and access to a [[Meatgrinder]] to cook.

==How to Acquire==

*Gather the 3 ingredients into your inventory: 1 [[Raw Venison]], 1 [[Moose Meat]] and 1 [[Intestines]]
*Right click a [[Meatgrinder]] to move into position and ready it for use
*Select the recipe and craft

Entrails.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = None
(''[[Hunting]]'') | objectsreq = [[requires::Sharp Tool]] | producedby = Animal Corpses | specific = Bait }} Entrails are used in a few crafting recipes, and also as bait. ==How to Acquire== Entrails, like [[intestines]], are obtained by butchering large animals. They are also acquired from cleaning (gutting) small animal carcasses. The quality of entrails received is based on the level as well as the type of animal, the quantity is based solely on the type of animal. In early game, easy source of entrails are flocks of [[Chicken]]. They don't fight back, meaning that the hard part is finding them on grasslands of the Hearthland and chasing them down once they begin fleeing. Chickens also provide [[Feather]]s and [[Bone Material|Bones]]. {{SharedData Quality BP}}

sections.How to Acquire:
Entrails, like [[intestines]], are obtained by butchering large animals. They are also acquired from cleaning (gutting) small animal carcasses.

The quality of entrails received is based on the level as well as the type of animal, the quantity is based solely on the type of animal. <!-- From lowest to highest are Aurochs/Mouflon, Fox, Boar, Deer, Bear. -->

In early game, easy source of entrails are flocks of [[Chicken]]. They don't fight back, meaning that the hard part is finding them on grasslands of the Hearthland and chasing them down once they begin fleeing. Chickens also provide [[Feather]]s and [[Bone Material|Bones]].


{{SharedData Quality BP}}

Filet of Cave Angler.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| image_basemeat = fish
|
| xitem          = 1
| yitem          = 1
|
| skillreq       = [[Hunting]]
| objectsreq     = [[requires::Cave Angler|Cave Angler Corpse]]
| specific       = Filet of Any Fish
}}

[[Category:Raw Fish]]

Filet of Cave Angler is the primary product of the corpse of a Cave Angler after being butchered. It can be roasted over a fire to produce [[Roast Filet of Cave Angler]], or used in fish recipes.

==How to Acquire==

Butcher a [[Cave Angler]].

==How to Use==

You need to lit a [[fire]] in order to roast a filet of raw fish.
{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Roast Meat}}

The quality of the roasted fish depends on your [[Perception]] and [[Survival]].

==Quality==

*The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of animal corpse and [[softcap]]ped by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
*Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

{{Game Development}}

| producedby = Hand
|
| energy     = unknown
| hunger     = unknown
|
| sat1       = unknown
}}

{{PAGENAME}} is an edible<!--assuming its edible--> item found sometimes when butchering a [[fish]].

==How to Acquire==

Butchering Sturgeons.

==Quality==

{{PAGENAME}} quality is based on the Sturgeon that was butchered, [[softcap]]ped by the player's [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]] ability. (''and probably also [[hardcap]]ped by used sharp-tool'')

{{Game Development}}

| cha2       = 5
|
| sat1       = Fish
}}

[[Category:Seafood]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|S$eafood|Fish in $Tears}}

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and craft using a boiling cauldron.

Both the type of fish and onion used will apply variable [[FEP]]s. Solid fat and "generic" [[butter]] do not get listed as an ingredients.

{{Game Development}}

Fishballs.title: Fishballs

Fishpie.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Baking]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Unbaked Fishpie]]
| producedby = [[requires::Oven]]
|
| energy     = 700
| hunger     = 2.5
| int        = 5
| per        = 3
|
| sat1       = Fish
| sat2       = Bread
}}

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Fishpie}}

{{PAGENAME}}, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[Food]] fills 700% of the [[energy]] bar and gives a variable amount of FEP depending on what ingredients you use.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into an [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as {{PAGENAME}}, be certain they have the same bake time or they will turn into [[ash]].

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Fishwrap}}

A food that can grant a variable amount of FEP depending on what ingredients you use.

==How to Acquire==

Place [[Unbaked Fishwrap]] inside of a fireplace. With full heat, the Fishwrap will be fully cooked after 18 minutes.


Alternatively, [[Kiln]] or [[Oven]] with 4 branches can be used.

==Quality==

The quality of the [[Unbaked Fishwrap]] is [[Softcap]]ped by your [[Survival#Survival|survival]], [[Perception#Perception|perception]], and [[Lore#Lore|lore]]

Fox.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(3/11)-(-3/11)round6}} | | baseq = 25 | | hp = 110 | fhp = 75 | | loot = [[Raw Fox Hide]], [[Entrails]], [[Intestines]], [[Raw Fox]] x3, [[Bone Material]] x2, [[Fresh Sly Ear of the Fox]] x(0-1) }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Foxes are not aggressive to players, but will attack any small game, such as [[Squirrel]]s, [[Rabbit]]s, and [[Chicken]]s. A player can pick up the corpse of the small creature afterwards. When Skinning them you have a chance to gain a [[Fresh Sly Ear of the Fox]]. =Combat= ==Behaviour== Passive until you attack them. Commonly chases and kills small animals, leaving them available to be picked up by players. They do however have a chance to attack you for stealing their prey while they are chasing them! ==Combat Moves== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! '''Attacks''' ! '''Restorations''' |- | {{:Fell Scratch}}{{:Chomp}} | {{:Bristle}} {{:Tail Spin}} |} ==Strategy== Fox hunting is a great goal for new players. You should try to get all your Combat skills (Agility, UA and MC) to about 15-25. There's not much to Foxes. Simply spam Punch while keeping your openings low. If you have it, you can also use Quick Barrage or Sideswipe/Uppercut. They won't attack nor defend often, so just go ham. They'll flee at sprint speed though, so you need to be on grassland or horseback. Good luck! =In-Game Example(s)= [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Raw {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be cleaned for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and then either butchered to gain [[Raw Fox]] or be [[Spitroast Fox|spitroasted]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
=Notes= *If a [[Badger]] and a [[Fox]] are in close proximity, they will initiate combat and begin to fight one another. Invariably, the Badger will kill the fox. This can be useful for newbies to get an early source of fox materials. It is also possible to initiate combat with the badger while it is fighting the fox, giving you the opportunity to kill it without taking any damage. {{Game Development}}

Fox Hole.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xobst   = 2.781
| yobst   = 1.97
|
| animals=
}}

[[Category:Dungeon]]

=How to Find=

Fox Holes are little mini "dungeons" that spawn every here and there. Significantly more mundane and common than the major Dungeons. Lasts about a 1 real-time week then despawn even if not raided.
{{PAGENAME}}s are full of common [[Fox]]es, [[Creatures| Dead small creatures]], [[:Category:Forageable Plants|Forageable Plants]], [[Seed of Tree or Bush|Seeds of Trees or Bushes]], and rarely [[Foxfire]].

=Getting Through=

The Hole is compiled like a large burrow, with 2-4 rooms.

Entrance: Unlike big dungeons entrance to this dungeon can spawn enemies.

Eliminate: These rooms have all doors unlocked. But spawn enemies you need to wipe out.

Treasure Room: These room contain 1-3 chests.

==Notes==
*Unlike big dungeons you can hearth home while inside a Dungeon.
{{Game Development}}

==Discovery conundrum: Frying Pan==
Recipes that require a Frying Pan as a tool require a [[Discovery]] of not just the usual ingredient requirements, but the tool itself. If you're selling/gifting a Frying Pan, it's a good idea to include a Material Account that describes the Frying Pan.

This food is often used as an energy refiller since it grants 500% energy in exchange for only 1 hunger, making it once of the highest energy/hunger foods that are also easy to make in bulk (as long as you have a supply of snow)

If you live far from a mountain remember you can mine [[Ice Block]]s to save snow from wintertime for the rest of the year.

Softcapped by [[Perception]] and [[Cooking]].

==How to Acquire==

Execute the recipe while having [[requires::Category:Fruit|Fruit]] or [[requires::Category:Berries|Berry]], [[Snow]] x2, [[Honey]], and [[Water]].

Exceptions: Inedible fruits or berries, including [[Spindleberries]] and [[Crabapple]] cannot be used to craft this recipe.

==Variable Ingredients==

Gelatin.rawWikitext: {{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Cooking]], ([[Hunting]]) | objectsreq = [[requires::Prepared Animal Hide]] x5 | producedby = [[requires::Cauldron]](with 2.5 liters) | specific = Gellant }} {{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|Ge$latin}} Creates 1.5 liter of {{PAGENAME}}. ==Common issues with Discovery== If a Barrel or a Bucket is used to receive the {{PAGENAME}}, it may not trigger its discovery when crafting. However anybody can discover {{PAGENAME}} by simply putting some in a Barrel and using the "Take" button. This applies to all things that can both be stored in a barrel and exist as an inventory item (flours, seeds, pigments.) {{Game Development}}

sections.Common issues with Discovery:
If a Barrel or a Bucket is used to receive the {{PAGENAME}}, it may not trigger its discovery when crafting. However anybody can discover {{PAGENAME}} by simply putting some in a Barrel and using the "Take" button. This applies to all things that can both be stored in a barrel and exist as an inventory item (flours, seeds, pigments.)

{{Game Development}}

| con2       = 3
|
| sat1       = Cheese
}}

[[Category:Cheeses]]

{{PAGENAME}} is a [[Cheese]]. Each tray produces 2 slices of cheese.

==How to Acquire==

Most cheeses, if left in the wrong area at the wrong stage, will turn into {{PAGENAME}} over time.

==Quality==

Cheese quality is equal to curd's quality when quality of a cheese tray is not lower than curd's. Otherwise if a tray has quality lower than a curd, a cheese will be [[softcap]]ped by a tray every stage.

Giant Pumpkin.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 3
| yitem      = 3
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Farming]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Seeds of Pumpkin]]
| producedby = [[requires::Pumpkin Crop]]
}}

[[Category:Vegetables]]

A {{PAGENAME}} requires a 3 x 3 empty space in your inventory in order to pick it up. If you slice a pumpkin you get 8 [[Pumpkin Flesh|Pumpkin Flesh]] and 40 [[Pumpkin Seeds|Pumpkin Seeds]] of the same quality of the pumpkin.

==How to Acquire==
You must plant 5 pumpkin seed, wait for the pumpkin to grow to full size and harvest it (4 growing stages). Pumpkins take 11 days to grow without a beehive.

With the Pumpkin in your inventory you can right click the pumpkin and select "Slice". This choice will yield 40 [[Pumpkin Seeds]] and 8 Slices of [[Pumpkin Flesh]] which can be eaten raw or baked into [[Pumpkin Pie]] or [[Pumpkin Bread]].

==In Game==
[[File:Pumpkin_on_ground.png]]

sections.How to Acquire:
You must plant 5 pumpkin seed, wait for the pumpkin to grow to full size and harvest it (4 growing stages). Pumpkins take 11 days to grow without a beehive.

<!-- {{GM|copy=Gingerbread Dough}} (not sure to add, also involves cookie cutter+cutting -->

==How to Acquire==

#Gather the [[Gingerbread Dough]] ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough.
#Use a Cookie cutter to cut cookies out of the dough.
#Place the unbaked cookies in the oven, then fuel the oven with 2 [[branch]]es and light it.

==Quality==
Unbaked Cookie Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Dough*2 + _{q}Cutter}{3}</math>

==Notes==

*Very rarely, after eating a Gingerbread Heart one may hear a child's scream.

{{Game Development}}

Grapejuice.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| skillreq   = none
| objectsreq = [[requires::Grapes]]
| producedby = [[requires::Extraction Press]]
}}

[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

==How to Acquire==
Put [[Grapes]] into an [[Extraction Press]], right-click it and press the Press button. This will put grapejuice in the press (shown as a bar); two full pressings will fill the press with juice.

With an empty bucket selected or an empty barrel (or a barrel partially filled with grapejuice), right-click the press to fill the bucket or barrel with juice.

==Use==
Grapejuice is placed in [[demijohn]]s to ferment into [[Wine]]. Grapejuice will not ferment if placed in a [[barrel]]. If left in the press or any container (other than the demijohn) for 5 hours, it will turn into [[Stale Grapejuice]] which eventually turns into [[Vinegar]].

If you add more grapejuice later, the fermentation doesn't reset but averages.

Source:
:''Loftar wrote:'' Nope, it's not reset; the time it will take for the fermentation to finish is the average of the time for the fresh and the half-fermented grape-juice to complete, weighted by the amount of each. In other words, if you have a barrel with Ae liters of grape-juice which will take De units of time to ferment, and put in An liters of fresh grape-juice which will take Dn units of time to ferment, the time it will take for the mixture to complete will be ((De * Ae) + (Dn * An)) / (Ae + An).

==Quality==
The quality of the grapejuice depends on the quality of the extraction press and the averaged qualities of the pressed grapes.

<math>\operatorname{Q_{grapejuice}} = \frac{ 3AvgQ_{grapes} + Q_{extraction\_press} }{4}</math>

== Notes ==

* You need 250 grapes to fill a 50 L demijohn with grapejuice.

Grapes.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Farming]], [[requires::Winemaking]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Seeds of Grape]] x5
| producedby = [[requires::Grape Crop]]
| specific   = Fruit
|
| energy     = 125
| hunger     = 1
| cha        = 1
|
| sat1       = Fruit
}}

Grapes are a crop that can be eaten, or used to make [[Raisins]] and [[Grapejuice]], which in turn can be used to make [[Wine]] and [[Vinegar]].

This [[:Category:Foods|food]] fills 125% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Attributes#Charisma|CHA]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Harvest grape from a [[trellis]], which will produce up to 3 grapes based on your [[skills]].

[[File:Legacy-GrapeVine.png]]

==Uses==

*Grapes used in an [[Extraction Press]] make [[Grapejuice]], leaving behind 5 seeds of grape. When placed into a [[Demijohn]], grapejuice will turn into wine after one week. If the grapejuice is not transferred to a demijohn or [[Barrel]] within 5 hours, it will turn into [[Stale Grapejuice]]. Both stale grapejuice and wine left in any container other than a barrel turn into [[Vinegar]] after a week.
*Grapes may also be placed on a [[Herbalist Table|herbalist table]] to produce [[raisins]]. However, this causes the loss of the grapes' seeds.

==Notes==

*Seeds of Grape may be acquired from [[Wild Windsown Weed]]s or as a by-product of eating grapes.
*Once fully grown, a grape plant's quality will not change. Replant from harvested seeds to roll the dice on Quality change like any other crop. ([//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?p=751092#p751092 ref])

{{Game Development}}

Grey Seal.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(6/11)-(-6/11)round6}} | | baseq = 35 | | hp = ~320 | fhp = ~240 | | loot = [[Fresh Grey Seal Hide]] x1-2, [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x2, [[Animal Fat]] x1-3, [[Raw Grey Seal]] x4, [[Bone Material]] x3, [[Flipper Bones]] x1-3 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] ''"Seal" redirects here. For the Curiosity, see [[Great Wax Seal]].'' '''Seals''' are considered one of the fastest animals in the game. Seals are able to inflict [[Seal Finger|Seal Finger]] [[wounds]] with their attacks, additionally these can also be obtained from butchering their corpses. The chance for wound from butchering is reduced if wearing [[Poor Man's Gloves]] or [[Plate Gauntlets]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you aggro them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:2 ! Restorations:2  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Chomp}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Seals are not tough opponents, their only advantage is their speed, which will be drastically reduced when taken on land. Even a [[Hearthling]] without a horse can deal with a Seal outside of water While on Horse, Seals pose no threat to any player with at least basic knowledge of combat and relatively low stats. If fought from a ship, and the retreating Seal is faster than you, break aggro and quickly initiate combat with them again, doing so will cause the Seal to return to combat, just to receive a finish blow from you. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Grey Seal]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Flipper Bones]] and [[Bone Material]]. ==Notes== {{Game Development}}

Grubs are tiny insects that can be found generally crawling around in forests and occasionally in [[Old Stump|old tree stump]]s and [[Leaf Pile|leaf piles]].

==How to Acquire==

Grubs are acquired by simply right clicking them. The character will then run to it, trying to catch it. They are slow, but can crawl under bushes and trees making them a bit annoying to catch. Despite this, they are still very easy to catch.

==Notes==

* Grubs spawned in forest have a quality related to ...
* Grubs spawned in old tree stumps have the quality of the old tree stump.

Hartshorn.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]], [[Cauldron]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Red Deer Antlers]], [[requires::Roe Deer Antlers]]
}}

[[Category:Powders]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

Hartshorn is the result of boiling [[Red Deer Antlers]] or [[Roe Deer Antlers]] in a [[Cauldron]]. When placed in a boiling cauldron, the antlers will eventually convert into a random amount of Hartshorn between 0.01kg, and 0.10kg with [[Roe Deer Antlers]] having a significantly lower yield compared to [[Red Deer Antlers]].

While the horns must be boiled for roughly two hours, the process can be interrupted and started intermittently at no penalty, as progress will never reset.

== Trivia ==

From Wikipedia:

"Hartshorn" is a red deer antler. The in game item refers to salt of hartshorn, which was ammonium carbonate. It was obtained by "dry distillation of oil of hartshorn", which was derived of "destructive distillation of male red deer horns or bones." Ammonium carbonate, or salt of hartshorn, itself acted very similar to baking soda and so was used as a leavening agent for breads and cookies. 

Salt of hartshorn was also used medicinally as a smelling salt and "used to treat insect bites, sunstroke, stye, and snakebites."

Hartshorn jelly, a decoction of the burnt antlers, was dissolved in water to help diarrhea. The coals of the burnt stuff was used as an absorbent, as well as in the treatment of dysentery. 

Salt of hartshorn when used in baking "releases ammonia and carbon dioxide gases, but no water, allowing you to make intricate designs in cookies and such. This also made baked goods cook more thoroughly and thinner cookies and biscuits let out the pungent aroma just fine."

The problem with hartshorn as a baking soda was that "ammonia released during the baking process reacts with glucose and fructose to form intermediate molecules that in turn, react with asparagine (an amino acid found in nuts, seeds, and whole grains) to form acrylamide, a carcinogen."

Hen.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature
| xitem = 2
| yitem = 2
| xobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}}
| yobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}}
|
| baseq = 10
|
| hp    = 10
| fhp   = 10
|
| loot  = [[Dead Chicken]]: [[Chicken Feather]] x3, [[Raw Chicken Meat]], [[Entrails]], [[Bone Material]] or [[Wishbone]](Rarely)
}}

[[Category:Domesticated]]
[[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]]

==How to Acquire/Use (Domestic)==

*Build a [[chicken coop]] for breeding hens and cocks. When a [[chick]] is placed inside a chicken coop with sufficient fodder, it will turn into a Hen or [[Cock]].

*[[Animal Husbandry]] enables you to breed chickens. A chicken coop, a cock and a hen are required to produce fertile [[Chicken Egg]], that will hatch into chicks.

*Hens require food to produce eggs.
*Hens hold outside claimed areas require food to not degrade and die. Inside claimed area's they will go dormant.

*A hen can be slaughtered via the "Wring Its Neck" option to convert it into a [[Dead Chicken|Dead Hen]].

==How to Acquire/Use (Wild)==

* You need [[Hunting]] skill to pick up wild chickens.

*For the longest time, Chickens stood placid while you picked them up one by one. However after {{Patch|Chicken Run}} the entire flock will start fleeing from you when you come close enough, meaning you can usually only catch one or two by sprinting.

*You can try spooking the flock into a direction of a [[Fox]] in hopes that it will start chasing and killing the chickens (they will stay on the ground to be picked up. '''Caution:''' Fox can aggro you for stealing it's prey. [[Badger]] and [[Wolverine]] can also aggro and kill chickens {{verify|Do Badger/Wolverine aggro/kill chickens}} but they can also aggro you!

*You can use a [[Clover]] to temporarily tame a [[Wildhorse]] to ride and easily trample chickens.

*If killed via combat, it will leave a [[Dead Chicken|Dead Hen]] as its corpse. This means using a [[sling]] might be viable way to kill some wild chickens.

*A hen can be slaughtered via the "Wring Its Neck" option to convert it into a [[Dead Chicken|Dead Hen]].

==Notes==

*Hunting wild chickens is viable source of [[Feather]]s, [[Entrails]], and [[Bone]]s in the early game. Pick them up, wring their necks, return to your camp/base when inventory is full and pluck/clean them there. All products except meat can be [[stockpile]]d for easy storage.

==Gallery==

<gallery widths=160px heights=120px>
Heningame.png
</gallery>

Herbalist Table.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xobst      = 1.082
| yobst      = 0.63
| xinv       = 4
| yinv       = 4
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Plant Lore]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Block of Wood]] x4, [[requires::Board]] x4, [[requires::Finer Plant Fibre]] x8
| repwith    = [[Finer Plant Fibre]]
|
| lift       = Yes
| sthp       = 100
| soak       = 0
}}

{{GM|$Build|$Production & Processing|$Herbalist Table}}

'''Note some values below have NOT been checked for 2.0 - if you can confirm numbers please remove the bold marking'''

Used with a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] and any one of [[Apple Core]], [[Fir Cone]], [[Mulberry]], nut, pine cone or any catkin to create a new [[Tree]] of that type. Also used to dry [[Wild Windsown Weed]]s to random seeds, to hatch [[silkworm|Silkworms]], to cure [[Fresh Hemp Bud|Hemp Buds]], to dry [[tobacco|Tobacco]] leaves, to dry [[raisins|Raisins]] from [[grapes|Grapes]], to dry and ferment [[tea|Tea]] leaves, to dry [[Boiled Pepper Drupes]], and to germinate [[Barley Seeds]] & [[Wheat Seeds]] for [[beer|beermaking]].

==How to Acquire==

Building an Herbalist Table requires plant fibres, which may be difficult to find for new players.

1. Ask somebody nicely for plant fibres.

2. If nobody's willing to donate or trade, and you can't acquire them by less legal means, then harvest your own fibres from [[Hemp Crop]] or [[Flax Crop]]. To get those, dry [[Wild Windsown Weed|wild windsown weeds]] (WWW) on a [[Drying Frame|drying frame]], and with a random chance you get a flax or hemp seed. Plant seeds into plowed land, wait, harvest, gather the fibres, repeat. While drying WWWs on the Herbalist Table is much faster than on a frame '''(Real time: 2.6h instead of 16h)''', you can start farming without a table and build one after you get hemp or flax.

==How to use==

Place any of the precursors of items mentioned above to the Herbalist Table and wait. [[Trees]] take 1 hour and 15 minutes to sprout from [[Treeplanter's Pot|treeplanter's pots]]; [[Silkworm Egg]]s take 8 hours to hatch; [[Fresh Tea Leaves]] take 40 minutes to dry into [[Green Tea Leaves|green tea leaves]]; those ferment into [[Black Tea Leaves]] in 16 hours if left on the table. However the quality of black tea leaves will be closer to the quality of the table than the green tea ones, so be careful if your Herbalist Table quality is less than quality of your Fresh Tea Leaves. However making black tea is beneficial if your Herbalist Table q > Fresh Tea Leaves q)

You can also dry [[Wild Windsown Weed|WWWs]] on the table.


If you are looking for wheat or barley, drying WWWs on the table actually is recommended (as long as your table is the same quality of the seeds or higher). If a WWW dries into [[Wheat Seed|wheat seed]]s or [[Barley Seeds]] and is left on the table, it will germinate; even if you do not wish to use the germinated seeds to malt into beer, only ''four'' germinated seeds are required to plant one tile of wheat/barley rather than the usual five. This, combined with the positive influence of a high-quality herbalist table, makes germinating seeds quite desirable, especially if you do not already have a satisfactory wheat or barley crop.

==Notes==

When placing down many Herbalist Tables in a house (perhaps for a silk factory), it is possible to save a significant amount of floor space by arranging the tables off of the grid and on adjacent pixels. This is accomplished by right-clicking the table while lifting it and holding down the control key to move the table off of the axis. When placed completely adjacent, Herbalist Tables each take up only 7/11ths of a tile.

==Quality==

The quality of anything produced on a table is the average of the precursor item and the table quality. Wild Windsown Weeds are the exception - the output quality of seeds does not depend on the quality of the table. 

The quality of an Herbalist Table is:

<math>\frac{_{q}AvgFibre + _{q}AvgBoard + _{q}AvgBlock}{3}</math>

To check a existing Herbalist Table quality, use {{GM|$Adventure|In$spect|...}}

==Time==

The lengths of time it takes to get the various items from their precursor items on an Herbalist Table are as follows:

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Honeybun Dough}}

A honeybun once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 175 energy, gives 4 [[:Attributes#Agility|agility]] [[:Getting_Started#Increasing_Stats|FEPs]] and fills 2.5% of the hunger bar.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the [[oven]], fuel it with 4 [[Branch|branches]] and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as honeybuns, be certain that they have the same bake time or else said other foods will be unfinished.

Horse.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | caption = Stallion » Mare » Foal | | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(8/11)-(-8/11)round6}} | | terrain = (''None, Domestic Areas'') | | hp = unknown | | loot = [[Fresh Horse Hide]], [[Entrails]] x4, [[Intestines]] x3, [[Raw Horse]] x8, [[Bone Material]] x10, [[Suckling's Maw]] from youngs. }} [[Category:Domesticated]] [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] {{PAGENAME}} are domesticated creatures, kept mainly as transportation. In order to obtain Horses, you must [[Taming|tame]] [[Wild Horse]]s. {{PAGENAME}}s can be lead around by right clicking a [[Rope]] onto the animal. {{PAGENAME}} eat units of [[Fodder]] per day (a [[Food Trough]] holds 2000 units; 1L of [[swill]] = 10 fodder). Pregnant mares eat ('''???'''){{verify|food consumption}} times as much at ('''???'''){{verify}} units per day. A lactating mare eats ('''???'''){{verify}} units of food per day, plus 0.1 unit for every 1L of milk produced.{{verify}} In grasslands biome, the horses feed from the natural grass as well, feeding less from the [[Food Trough]]. ==Mare== [[File:Horsestats.png|frame|''Horse information window'']] Mares are female {{PAGENAME}}s. They are kept for their ability to produce Foals, as well as for their meat. At quality 10, one mare can feed up to 2.5 foals. The "Milk #" stat of the mare will need to be increased for it to feed more foals without risk of starvation to the foals. Mares do '''not''' produce usable milk like [[Cattle]], [[Sheep]] and [[Goats]]. ==Stallion== Stallions are male {{PAGENAME}}s. Left with a mare, the stallion will impregnate her, indicated by a distinct mating animation. The non-breeding males are often preferred, over Mares, for pulling Wagons and for riding. This is because the non-breeding males tend to be of lesser value to the owner, in case the horse needs to be abandoned or is killed. Note: A stallions will ignore open-gate boundaries when it spots a willing(''none pregnant assumed'') and accessible mare. ==Foal== Foals are adolescent {{PAGENAME}}s. They will grow into either a mare or a stallion in 10 real life days. In addition to the regular products, foals give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered. Foals must have access to any mare that has given birth, for their source of milk. Foals will begin to starve if there is no access to one or more mares. Each starvation tick will ruin random stats, till the foal dies. ==How to acquire== To obtain a Horse you have to tame a [[Wild Horse]]. Feed a Wild Horse a clover, then use a rope to hitch it to a [[Hitching Post]] or tree. Once hitched, it must be tamed about once a day by engaging it in combat until it submits. After each taming session, the Horse must be re-hitched to prevent it from despawning. After about four days (depending on how many "Taming Points" you gain after each round of combat) the Wild Horse will transform into a Stallion or Mare. It may take three or more tames to obtain both so that more horses can be bred. ==Breeding== A Mare will take 4.5 real life days to give birth. A foal takes 10 real life days to grow up. Breeding for animals with higher quality stats is much more difficult than it is to grow higher quality crops. The quality of any of the foal's stat is the average of the male and female's stat, +20 -> -5 {{verify}}, and then soft capped by the breeding quality of the male involved. Since you're typically looking for multiple stats, on top of getting more breeding stat, this can prove to be a slow task. Mares can have twins on rare occasions. You can tell the gender of foals by the information window title, it will say stallion for males and mare for females. Fodder quality must be the same or higher than the breeding horses feeding from it. If fodder quality is lower, the horses will suffer quality decreases till they are at the same quality of the fodder. When trough fodder is depleted, the horses will feed from the biome tiles. The tiles are quality 10 and the horses will begin to suffer quality losses as they eat from the biome. Once all biome tiles within the feeding area become dirt, the horse(s) will begin to starve, suffer heavy quality/stat losses and die. One real life day without fodder can easily ruin a month worth of breeding effort. ==Milk== While mares produce milk for foal's, they can't be manually milked. ==Special stats== Horses have three unique values which can be bred: Endurance, Stamina, and Metabolism. :'''Endurance''' determines how much pony power the horse has. :'''Stamina''' determines how fast the horse regenerates pony power from its food meter. This stat is arguably the most important stat for breeding riding horses. :'''Metabolism''' determines how much pony power each unit of food is converted to. See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information. ==Tips & Tricks== *It is a good idea to separate the Males from the Females, to ensure that you can select the highest quality Male to mate with. Also you can castrate your lower quality stallions to stop them from breeding and acquire [[bollocks|Bollock]]. *You should use your lowest quality horse/bull to pull a wagon, since quality has no effect on wagon-pulling and if you have to flee and leave your wagon behind, then the loss will not be as bad. *Horses can be equipped with [[Saddlebags]], which act as an inventory for the animal. Saddlebags have a 4x4 inventory space. *You can hearth home with a horse, while mounted or leashed. *The horse tracks its last rider, so if you dismount while visiting someone's claim, you can still remount without losing the horse, much the same as with boats. *It is possible to use emotes while riding a horse. *If you whistle (emote) at a tamed horse, it will run to you. *If you keep a Rope in your Inventory, your character will auto-leash your horse when you dismount from it. (''rope in belt will not auto-leash'') ==Notes== *Doors: Although its possible to go through doors on horseback, its just not straightforward. The troublesome exception is the cave-entry which has become way harder to enter on horseback (''cave-exit not effected by this''). An old April Fool's prank. Jorb and Loftar promised horses during Legacy. The original prank was that if you set many carrots and straw around an [[Aurochs]], and left it there, it would turn into a horse. This obviously led to some suspicion, and so Jorb created the following animation for a horse walking to further fool everyone. Many carrots and much straw was lost. [[File:Horse - April Fools joke.gif]] ==Gallery== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Horse]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
horse-droppings.webp | Horseback flower-dropping. {{Game Development}}

sections.Breeding:
A Mare will take 4.5 real life days to give birth.

A foal takes 10 real life days to grow up.

Breeding for animals with higher quality stats is much more difficult than it is to grow higher quality crops. The quality of any of the foal's stat is the average of the male and female's stat, +20 -> -5 {{verify}}, and then soft capped by the breeding quality of the male involved. Since you're typically looking for multiple stats, on top of getting more breeding stat, this can prove to be a slow task.

Mares can have twins on rare occasions.

You can tell the gender of foals by the information window title, it will say stallion for males and mare for females.

Fodder quality must be the same or higher than the breeding horses feeding from it.  If fodder quality is lower, the horses will suffer quality decreases till they are at the same quality of the fodder.  When trough fodder is depleted, the horses will feed from the biome tiles.  The tiles are quality 10 and the horses will begin to suffer quality losses as they eat from the biome.  Once all biome tiles within the feeding area become dirt, the horse(s) will begin to starve, suffer heavy quality/stat losses <s>and die</s>.  One real life day without fodder can easily ruin a month worth of breeding effort.
sections.Foal: Foals are adolescent {{PAGENAME}}s. They will grow into either a mare or a stallion in 10 real life days. In addition to the regular products, foals give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered.  Foals must have access to any mare that has given birth, for their source of milk.  Foals will begin to starve if there is no access to one or more mares.  Each starvation tick will ruin random stats, till the foal dies.

Once hitched, it must be tamed about once a day by engaging it in combat until it submits. After each taming session, the Horse must be re-hitched to prevent it from despawning. After about four days (depending on how many "Taming Points" you gain after each round of combat) the Wild Horse will transform into a Stallion or Mare. It may take three or more tames to obtain both so that more horses can be bred.
sections.Mare:
[[File:Horsestats.png|frame|''Horse information window'']]

Mares are female {{PAGENAME}}s. They are kept for their ability to produce Foals, as well as for their meat.  At quality 10, one mare can feed up to 2.5 foals.  The "Milk #" stat of the mare will need to be increased for it to feed more foals without risk of starvation to the foals. Mares do '''not''' produce usable milk like [[Cattle]], [[Sheep]] and [[Goats]].

The original prank was that if you set many carrots and straw around an [[Aurochs]], and left it there, it would turn into a horse. This obviously led to some suspicion, and so Jorb created the following animation for a horse walking to further fool everyone. Many carrots and much straw was lost.

sections.Special stats:
Horses have three unique values which can be bred: Endurance, Stamina, and Metabolism.
:'''Endurance''' determines how much pony power the horse has.
:'''Stamina''' determines how fast the horse regenerates pony power from its food meter.  This stat is arguably the most important stat for breeding riding horses.
:'''Metabolism''' determines how much pony power each unit of food is converted to.

See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information.
sections.Stallion:
Stallions are male {{PAGENAME}}s. Left with a mare, the stallion will impregnate her, indicated by a distinct mating animation. The non-breeding males are often preferred, over Mares, for pulling Wagons and for riding.  This is because the non-breeding males tend to be of lesser value to the owner, in case the horse needs to be abandoned or is killed.

Note: A stallions will ignore open-gate boundaries when it spots a willing(''none pregnant assumed'') and accessible mare.
sections.Tips & Tricks:
*It is a good idea to separate the Males from the Females, to ensure that you can select the highest quality Male to mate with. Also you can castrate your lower quality stallions to stop them from breeding and acquire [[bollocks|Bollock]].

*You should use your lowest quality horse/bull to pull a wagon, since quality has no effect on wagon-pulling and if you have to flee and leave your wagon behind, then the loss will not be as bad.

*Horses can be equipped with [[Saddlebags]], which act as an inventory for the animal. Saddlebags have a 4x4 inventory space.

*You can hearth home with a horse, while mounted or leashed.

*The horse tracks its last rider, so if you dismount while visiting someone's claim, you can still remount without losing the horse, much the same as with boats.

*It is possible to use emotes while riding a horse.

*If you whistle (emote) at a tamed horse, it will run to you.

*If you keep a Rope in your Inventory, your character will auto-leash your horse when you dismount from it. (''rope in belt will not auto-leash'')

Ice Block.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| name       = Ice Block
| image      = File:Ice block.png
|
| xobst      = 0.998
| yobst      = 0.998
|
| producedby = [[requires::Pickaxe]] on a '''frozen water tile'''
|
| lift       = Yes
}}

Ice Block is an object that can be produced from frozen water tiles during the [[Seasons|winter season]], when most rivers and bodies of water freezes over along with growing snow enveloping most of the world.

Player can right click the Ice Block to "shave snow" from it, producing anywhere from 10-16 [[snow]] pieces per block.

Ice Blocks, when stored, can serve as a quick and easy way for players to obtain snow for later to craft [[Cold Compress|Cold Compresses]] (as they can melt and cannot be stored for long) and [[Fruit Sorbet|Fruit Sorbets]]. This is especially useful for players who live far away from mountains with snowcaps, as that is the standard way of acquiring snow during warmer seasons. 



==How to Acquire==

{{GM|$Adventure|$Dig}}

Ice Block can be produced by digging from a frozen water tile using a [[pickaxe]].

==Notes==

*Ice Block can be loaded onto a [[Cart]] for transport, but cannot be loaded onto a [[Wagon]].
*Ice Block will eventually melt, however players can preserve them for much longer by storing them in a [[cellar]].
**Players has reported that the cellar storage method can prevent the Ice Block from melting almost up to the next winter season (given that they harvest and store the Ice Blocks toward the end of the current winter season - confirmed, cellar ice disappeared on the 13th of the 6th month, 2 days before winter start).
[[File:Ice Block Basement.png|800px|example of optimal storage of Ice Blocks in cellar]]

{{Game Development}}

Intestines are used with the [[Sausage Making]] skill to produce sausages.

It can be replaced by [[Stitched Casing]] ([[Entrails]] x3 and [[String]] x1) when making [[Sausage Making|sausages]]

==How to Acquire==
Any creature larger than a fox will leave behind intestines when butchered. The larger the creature, the more intestines you will harvest.

The quality of intestines obtain is based on the quality of the animal [[softcap]]ped by your [[sharp tool]] and [[survival]]

sections.How to Acquire:

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as carrot cakes, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will either turn into [[ashes]] or be unfinished.

The {{PAGENAME}} will be ready in approximately 18 minutes 15 seconds.

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

Leek.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 2 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | producedby = [[requires::Leek Crop]] | specific = Any Onion | | slot = 6L or 6R }} Leeks can be planted as a crop or can be used as an ingredient in several cooking recipes * Leeks can planted to produce 1-3 Leeks * Leeks are used in recipes such as [[Liver & Onions]] or [[Perched Perch]] ==How to Use== In order to plant {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. Note: Leeks can be equipped, where your character will spin it while standing still (reference to the Loituma Girl meme from ages of old) {{Game Development}}

Lettuce Crop.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Seeds of Lettuce]] }} [[Category:Crops]] {{PAGENAME}} is a type of [[Farming|Crop]] which can be grown to yield farming products. ==Planting and Harvesting== '''Planting''' *To plant seeds you must first create a [[Terraforming|Plowed Field]]. *LEFT CLICK the seeds in your inventory and then RIGHT CLICK on the plowed field you wish to plant them on. *SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a seed, your cursor will change then select an area to plant. *''You may use a bucket to hold up to 1000 seeds.'' '''Harvesting''' *RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest. *SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from. *''Seeds will automatically stack into a bucket if equipped.'' ==Growing== {{PAGENAME}} growing speed affected by [[Bee Skep]], [[Realm#Realm Bonuses|Realm Bonuses]], [[Credos#Farmer|Farmer Credo]] and hidden ground spots. ===Stages=== {{PAGENAME}} have five stages of growth: [[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_1.png|100px]] Stage 1: The lettuce field just planted. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_2.png|100px]] Stage 2: The lettuce field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_3.png|100px]] Stage 3: The lettuce field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_4.png|100px]] Stage 4: The lettuce field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_5.png|100px]] Stage 5: Harvesting the lettuce field provides 1 [[Head of Lettuce]] and 5-12 [[Seeds of Lettuce|lettuce seeds]] depending on your farming [[skills]] and [[Credos#Farmer|farmer credo]].
{{Game Development}}

sections.Growing: {{PAGENAME}} growing speed affected by [[Bee Skep]], [[Realm#Realm Bonuses|Realm Bonuses]], [[Credos#Farmer|Farmer Credo]] and hidden ground spots.
sections.Planting and Harvesting:
'''Planting'''
*To plant seeds you must first create a [[Terraforming|Plowed Field]]. 
*LEFT CLICK the seeds in your inventory and then RIGHT CLICK on the plowed field you wish to plant them on. 
*SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a seed, your cursor will change then select an area to plant.
*''You may use a bucket to hold up to 1000 seeds.''

'''Harvesting'''
*RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest.
*SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from.
*''Seeds will automatically stack into a bucket if equipped.''

[[File:Lettuce_Crop_Stage_5.png|100px]] Stage 5: Harvesting the lettuce field provides 1 [[Head of Lettuce]] and 5-12 [[Seeds of Lettuce|lettuce seeds]] depending on your farming [[skills]] and [[Credos#Farmer|farmer credo]].

Lettuce Leaf.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Farming]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Head of Lettuce]]
| producedby = Hand
| specific   = Salad Greens, Leaf
|
| energy     = 125
| hunger     = 1.5
| con        = 0.5
| dex        = 0.5
|
| sat1       = Vegetables
}}

==How to Acquire==

Split are obtained by splitting a Head of Lettuce, along with [[Seeds of Lettuce]].

The amount harvested is based on your farming skills, ranging from 1 to 3.

==How to Use==

Lettuce Leaves are used as [[Salad Greens]] in several crafting recipes, including [[Chicken Salad]] and [[Rabbit Food]]. Eating the leaves plain fills 125% of the [[energy]] bar, and gives 0.5 [[Constitution]] and 0.5 [[Dexterity]] in [[FEP]]s.

They can also be used as fodder in [[Food Trough]]s and in [[Chicken Coop]]s.

A Lettuce Leaf counts as "Any Leaf" for appropriate recipes.

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]

Liberty Caps can be eaten by right clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 300% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 0,5 [[Psyche]] in [[FEP]]s.

Furthermore, when eaten raw {{PAGENAME}} you get [[Buff#The Mind Liberated|The Mind Liberated]] buff, which buff [[Psyche]] and decrease [[Agility]]. At least 10 Liberty Caps are needed to get "crazy high". The buff goes off fast so eat them in one go.

==Photosensitivity Warning==
Right after eating Liberty Caps, and occasionally while affected by "The Mind Liberated" will cause flashing lights to appear on screen. These simulate psychedelic hallucinations. Frequency and intensity of effects scales with the buff amount.

=Notes=
To gain the "The Mind Liberated" effect {{PAGENAME}} must be consumed in a way which grants the character [[FEP]]s which means consuming them while at high enough energy or using a feast option at table.

| skillreq   = [[Farming]]

| producedby = [[requires::Extraction Press]]
| specific   = Vegetable Oil
}}

[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

Linseed Oil is the extracted oil from pressed [[Flax Seeds]]. 1000 seeds pressed in the press produce 10 liters of Linseed Oil. The quality of the [[Extraction Press]] affect the oil quality.

Can be stored in barrels and buckets.

It is used for making [[Painting|Paint]] and [[Hartshorn Salve]], primarily.

Lynx.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(5/11)-(-5/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 45 | | armor = 35 | hp = 400 | fhp = 150 | | loot = [[Fresh Lynx Hide]], [[Intestines]] x3, [[Entrails]] x2, [[Raw Lynx]] x6, [[Bone Material]] x3, [[Lynx Claws]] x1-4 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Large yellow cat who patrols the forest for food. They are the animal with the most Agility in the game. They normally should run away from you, but have a good chance to [[execute]] you if they decide to aggro. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== They behave extremely strangely, unlike any other aggressive animal: they will flee within a certain radius of a player, then charge seemingly out of nowhere and only initiate combat at the very last second. Very agile will likely hit twice or more for every attack you make ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:4 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Chomp}} {{:Shredding Paw}} {{:Dark Heart Move}} | {{:bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== You'll need horse with full stamina to catch them if they'll run, because only horse on 3rd-4th speed can catch up lynx. Beware that with 50 AGI they attack faster than you. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Lynx]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Lynx Claws]] and [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== * To get to you, lynx will destroy any objects obstructing their path with lower than Soak 25, meaning only [[Palisade]]s and [[Brickwall]]s will stop their bloodlust. {{Game Development}}

Mammoth.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(7/11)-(-7/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(15/11)-(-15/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 80 | | armor = 125 | hp = 4000 | fhp = 2800 | | loot = [[Fresh Mammoth Hide]] x4, [[Animal Fat]] x2-5, [[Intestines]] x10, [[Entrails]] x10, [[Mammoth Meat]] x25, [[Mammoth Tusk]] x2, [[Bone Material]] x16 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] "Mammoths are big, rare, and dangerous. They may kill you, but only seldom attack." -jorb ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Rarely aggressive, but are usually faster than a horse while walking and sometimes even faster when running.
Take care when fighting mammoths - especially on horses, as after >70% openings they can use an ability similar to "[[Hearth Magic|Raw Hide!]]" and stun nearby horses. This can quickly cause you to get knocked down. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Crush Underfoot}} {{:Trumpeting Fury}} {{:Trunk Gunk}} {{:Trunk Roll}} {{:Stampede}} | {{:Overwhelm}} |} ===Strategy=== [[File:mammoth_strategy_4.png|thumb|''A properly positioned [[Hearthling]] as seen - with the use of Boundary Boxes displayed through the Hurricane Client'']] Current (as of '''[[World]] 16.1''') strategy involves not fighting the mammoth in a fair fight, but rather abusing the palisades being indestructible by mammoths.
What you need is a [[Claim|claimed]] enclosed palisade in the mountain area. (As a precaution - wait at least 24h after building the palisade before trying to bring a mammoth inside, they may attempt to destroy it before it builds up sufficient soak strength.)
The palisade needs to have a small (preferably rectangular) compartment with a gate and a single empty square without the palisade built there for luring the mammoth inside (see images for reference).
[[File:mammoth_strategy_1.png]] [[File:mammoth_strategy_2.png]]
Once the palisade is setup, grab yourself a horse and look around for a mammoth (never attempt luring a mammoth on foot unless it's literally next to your palisade) - if found, attack it and run straight into the compartment then wait for the mammoth to walk into it, once it's inside just run through the gap in the wall and after that you can try closing the gate behind it. (usually the gate can stay open, as even in peace mode the animal does not run through the gate - it does rarely happen though)
'''IMPORTANT - Do not attempt to lure a mammoth inside with a character that has [[Rage]] unlocked, as it will make it impossible to run through the gates and you will have to either run away from the mammoth or use [[Hearth Magic|Quell the Beast]] to escape.
''' If you have caught yourself a mammoth, you are free to attack it, but beware, as mammoths use a ranged attack called [[Mammoth|Trunk Gunk]] which hits through armor, and to protect yourself your [[Hearthling]] needs to be partially covered by the palisade segment.
[[File:mammoth_strategy_3.png]]
To safely fight the mammoth you should use any melee weapon with extra range such as [[Boar Spear]], [[Giant Needle]] or [[B12]] and position yourself outside its attack range.
If you are experienced, want to learn how to or already do not have any issues positioning yourself perfectly to stop the animal from attacking you then feel free to use a sword. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Mammoth Meat]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for and [[Bone Material]].
[[File:Mammoth_skull.png|150px]] Mammoth Skull. A leftover after butchering the mammoth - can be collected for [[Mammoth Tusk]]. == Notes == * Corpse cannot be lifted. * After butchering leaves a liftable skull - can be used for [[Totemic Gate]] or as a display trophy. {{Game Development}}

Take care when fighting mammoths - especially on horses, as after >70% openings they can use an ability similar to "[[Hearth Magic|Raw Hide!]]" and stun nearby horses. This can quickly cause you to get knocked down.

(usually the gate can stay open, as even in peace mode the animal does not run through the gate - it does rarely happen though)

Meatpie.rawWikitext: {{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Baking]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Unbaked Meatpie]] | producedby = [[requires::Oven]] | | energy = 700 | hunger = 3.5 | con = 4 | str = 5 | | sat1 = Meat | sat2 = Bread }} [[Category:Baked Goods]] {{GM|copy=Unbaked Meatpie}} Meatpie, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[food]] fills 700% of the [[energy]] bar, gives 5 [[Strength]] and 4 [[Constitution]] in [[FEP]]s. Any raw meat type works with this recipe. ==How to Acquire== Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe in order to produce the dough. Place the dough into the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[Branch|branches]] and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as meat pie, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will either turn into [[ashes]] or be unfinished. ==Variable Ingredients== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center" ! colspan="2" | Ingredients ! colspan="4" | FEPs(at q10) |- ! ==How to Acquire==

Metal Shovel.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 2
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Metal Working]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Bar of Hard Metal]], [[requires::Block of Wood]] x3
| producedby = [[requires::Anvil]], [[requires::Smithy's Hammer]]
|
| slot       = 6L and 6R
}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Clothes & Equipment|$Tools|$Metal Shovel}}

A {{PAGENAME}} is a tool used to perform various actions. it can be used to dig for [[Clay]], [[Soil]], [[Sand]] and [[Snow]] for 25% per tick, to quickly remove [[Stump|Tree Stump]]s, to dig up [[Bush]]es, and to plant [[Seed of Tree or Bush|Tree Seeds]]

While using [[Survey|Land Survey]]s, a {{PAGENAME}} digs a base speed of 4 units of soil per action, modified by quality.

==How to Acquire==

First you need to wield a [[smithy's hammer]] in one hand. Then right click an [[anvil]] with proper materials in your inventory.

==How to Use==

*To dig for Clay, Soil, Sand, and Snow: Use the Dig action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|$Dig|...}}
*To remove a stump: Use the Destroy action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|Destro$y|...}}
*To dig up a bush: Use the Lift action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|$Lift|...}}

To plant a Tree seed:

#Pick up a tree seed from your inventory and use it on the ground (left click on the seed, right click on the ground)
#With your shovel equipped, select the flower menu option available to plant your seed
''See [[Tree]] for more details on tree farming''

==Quality==

* [[{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Quality]] = <math>\frac{\frac{_{q}Metal+ _{q}Wood}{2} *  9 + {_{q}Anvil* 4 + _{q}Smithy's Hammer}* 3}{16}</math> and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[2]{Strength *Smithing}</math>.

*Max soil: <math>4*Floor(\sqrt{\frac{_{q}MetalShovel}{10}})</math> Ergo: q[10.0..39.9]=4, q[40.0..89.9]=8, q[90.0..159.9]=12, etc. (''result of q[1.0..9.9] unknown, probably 2'')

Milk.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | objectsreq = {{#ask:[[Specific::Milk]]}} | producedby = [[requires::Bucket]], any drinking vessel | specific = Milk }} [[Category:Dairy]] [[Category:Liquids]] [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] {{Specific-property-text}} Milk is used to make [[Butter]], [[Curd]]s, and [[Batter]], and is therefore an ingredient of several [[Baked Goods]].
Milk can be stored in any Drinking Vessel, a [[Bucket]], [[Barrel]], or a [[Cistern]].
Drinking milk increases the [[FEP]] gained and helps reduce satiation from [[Bread]], Forageables and [[Berry]]s. ==How to Acquire== Milk is acquired by selecting a bucket, barrel or any drinking vessel and using it on any milk-able animal via right-click. Cows produce Milk at a rate of Milk Quantity * 0.01 per 10 minutes.
0.1L/10 minutes at Quantity 10. 0.4L/10 minutes at Quantity 40, 2.0L/10 minutes at Quantity 200. *Cows can store a maximum of 25L of Milk *Sheep can store a maximum of 25L of Milk *Goat can store a maximum of 15+ (25L) of Milk *Only 2L can be milked from an [[Aurochs]]. A higher level clover produces higher level milk. *Mixing different types of milk in any container will yield neutral 'Milk' of generic property. ==Drinking== *Milk has no preferred container and therefore cannot gain quality boosts. *Quality 10 milk recovers 10% stamina and drains 20% energy per 0.05L sip. Higher quality milk will decrease the energy drain but stamina recovery remains the same at all qualities. :*{{MT|m=w14|on}}This makes high quality milk useful for performing stamina draining tasks without having to eat as much to recover, thus preserving your hunger bonus. Though energy is always shown as a whole number, it is not simply rounded up or down. For example, Q11 milk will alternate between draining 20% and 19% energy at regular intervals. Other drinkable liquids follow the same rules and formula.{{MT|off}} :*As of World 15, when your Energy bar is lower than 8000%, eating outside of a table does not provide any hunger, satiation or FEP, making this strategy rather useless. *Energy Drain= (\frac{1}{\sqrt{quality/10}} + 1) * 10 *Gives energy if Q >20 :*According to that formula initial quality growth is more effective, and it always higher than 10% per 0.05 sip. Naturally stamina regenerate 10% per 10% energy. ==Notes== *You can use a barrel of milk to feed baby animals if you accidentally kill off their mother or if there is no lactating adult female present. {{Game Development}}

sections.Drinking:
*Milk has no preferred container and therefore cannot gain quality boosts.
*Quality 10 milk recovers 10% stamina and drains 20% energy per 0.05L sip. Higher quality milk will decrease the energy drain but stamina recovery remains the same at all qualities. 
:*{{MT|m=w14|on}}This makes high quality milk useful for performing stamina draining tasks without having to eat as much to recover, thus preserving your hunger bonus. Though energy is always shown as a whole number, it is not simply rounded up or down. For example, Q11 milk will alternate between draining 20% and 19% energy at regular intervals. Other drinkable liquids follow the same rules and formula.{{MT|off}}
:*As of World 15, when your Energy bar is lower than 8000%, eating outside of a table does not provide any hunger, satiation or FEP, making this strategy rather useless.
*Energy Drain= <math>(\frac{1}{\sqrt{quality/10}} + 1) * 10</math>
*Gives energy if Q >20
:*According to that formula initial quality growth is more effective, and it always higher than 10% per 0.05 sip. Naturally stamina regenerate 10% per 10% energy.
sections.How to Acquire:
Milk is acquired by selecting a bucket, barrel or any drinking vessel and using it on any milk-able animal via right-click. Cows produce Milk at a rate of Milk Quantity * 0.01 per 10 minutes.
0.1L/10 minutes at Quantity 10. 0.4L/10 minutes at Quantity 40, 2.0L/10 minutes at Quantity 200. *Cows can store a maximum of 25L of Milk *Sheep can store a maximum of 25L of Milk *Goat can store a maximum of 15+ (25L) of Milk *Only 2L can be milked from an [[Aurochs]]. A higher level clover produces higher level milk. *Mixing different types of milk in any container will yield neutral 'Milk' of generic property.
sections.Notes:
*You can use a barrel of milk to feed baby animals if you accidentally kill off their mother or if there is no lactating adult female present.

{{Game Development}}
