Moose.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(6/11)-(-6/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-10/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 50 | | armor = 65 | hp = 800 | fhp = ~800 | | loot = [[Fresh Moose Hide]] x2, [[Intestines]] x4, [[Entrails]] x3, [[Animal Fat]], [[Raw Moose]] x14, [[Bone Material]] x10, [[Moose Antlers]] x1 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] The moose or elk, is the largest and heaviest extant species in the Deer family. They are notorious for their extreme size and strength, as well as their intimidating intelligence, as moose are known to attempt to drown their victims, and short of that will try to crush their ribcage and lungs. =Combat= ==Behaviour== Moose are aggressive and have a decent chance to [[execute]] you if they KO you. ===Combat Moves=== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! '''Attacks''' ! '''Restorations''' |- | {{:Low Horn Swipe}}{{:Mule Kick}}{{:Thunder Over}}{{:Bucking}}{{:Blood & Gore}}{{:Heavy Antlers}}{{:Breathing Rage}}{{:Dark Heart Move}} | {{:Rampant Rage}}{{:Unstoppable}}{{:Careful Approach}}{{:Bristle}} |} ==Strategy== Ride a horse so you can kite the Moose. You will need about 100+ Melee Combat and 100 Agility to deal damage and keep yourself safe in the fight. =In-Game Example(s)= [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Moose]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Moose Antlers]] and [[Bone Material]].
{{Game Development|1}}

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Mushrooms]]

Morels are mushrooms that can be [[foraging|foraged]] with 150 [[Exploration]] * [[Perception]].

Morels are only found during [[Seasons|autumn]].

==Uses== 

Can be boiled by placing them in [[cauldron]]'s inventory grid and lighting the cauldron while it has sufficient water. [[Parboiled Morels]] give [[Perception]] FEPs when eaten.

Can be placed into a [[herbalist table]] to dry them. [[Dried_Morels|Dried morels]] give [[Strength]] FEPs when eaten.

Mouflon.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(6/11)-(-6/11)round6}} | | baseq = 25 | | hp = 200 | fhp = 120 | | loot = [[Fresh Mouflon Hide]], [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] x3, [[Raw Wild Mutton]] x6, [[Bone Material]] x3, [[Mouflon Horn]] x0-2 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Mouflon (which is the name's both singular and plural form) are a species of wild sheep native to Turkey and Iran and are considered endangered. When tamed they become a [[Sheep]] and, due to their lower HP, are the easiest and often first creature for a new player to tame. They will produce milk, wool and meat, and they reproduce quite fast, and so are useful for [[Suckling's Maw|Suckling's Maws]] and testicles. You can feed them a clover. When doing so you gain the ability to leash them with a rope and, if desired, take either 1 wool from them, whose quality will be equal to the clover's. See [[Taming|this page]] for more info about taming wild animals. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you attack them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:4 ! Restorations:2  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Low Horn Swipe}} {{:Mule Kick}} {{:Sudden Charge}} {{:Thunder Over}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Swift Evasion}} |} ===Strategy=== Mouflon are about as strong as a badger but have quite high agility, at around 40-50. They throw out mule kicks and low horn swipes very fast and often spam swift evasion. They're difficult to stand and deliver to as they whittle down your SHP and ruin your armor until you've got similar agility, or have enough UA/MC to deal enough openings to force them into a defensive state, so early game when hunting them, use your stone or tinker axe to deliver a mighty cleave or full circle while their red opening is high to force them to flee and take them out as they run. When taming a mouflon, wear good armor and expect it to get damaged. Peace it out and spam quick barrage or sideswipe until done. Ignore your openings; if you can't you're not strong enough to tame animals yet. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Wild Mutton]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== {{Game Development}}

{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = unknown
| objectsreq = [[requires::River Pearl Mussel]] x4, [[requires::Tuber]] x2, [[requires::Vinegar]] x0.05 L, optional:[[requires::Spices]]
| producedby = [[requires::Cauldron]]
|
| energy     = 500
| hunger     = 2
| cha        = 3
| psy        = 2
| dex2       = 4
|
| sat1       = Fish
}}

[[Category:Seafood]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|S$eafood|$Moules Frites}}

==How to Acquire==

You need a cauldron or clay cauldron with at least 1 liters of hot water to make a Moules Frites. The resulting item quality is influenced by the quality of the water and the ingredients, and is [[softcap]]ped by <math>\sqrt{{Cooking}*{Perception}}</math>. Firewood does not play a role in the quality formula.


Any [[River Pearl|River Pearls]] that would have been found in the mussels will be returned to your inventory.



==Ingredients==

Beetroots add Dexterity. Carrots add Perception. Turnips add Will. Cavebulb adds Psyche. 

==Meta==

In terms of energy gain, it would make more sense to eat the ingredients raw than cook them. In terms of [[FEP]]s, there are much more variables. For example, high-quality Cauldron will greatly boost the quality (and therefore FEP yield) of boiled food. Note that fresh characters, depending on amount of bonuses they have to FEP gain, might fill their entire food bar with a single boiled mussel.

{{Game Development}}

Mulberries can be gathered from a Mulberry Tree. They can be eaten directly or used as a fruit in recipes. They can be used to grow more Mulberry Trees by planting them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] or planting them in the ground directly with a shovel.

==How to Acquire==

Right click on a [[Mulberry Tree]] and the option to pick a Mulberry will be presented.

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Mushroom & Onion Pirozhki Dough}}

Mushroom & Onion Pirozhki, once baked, can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option.

This [[food]] fills 350% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 4 [[Constitution]] and 4 [[Dexterity]] in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the oven, then fuel the [[Oven]] with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as mushroom & onion pirozhki, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ashes]].


4 x [[Branch]]s in the [[Oven]] will cook it and never burn it.

The mushroom & onion pirozhki will be finished in approximately 16 minutes and 40 seconds.

sections.How to Acquire:
Nuggets are made by using the craft with a lit [[Crucible]] while holding a [[Bar of Common Metal]]. This gives 10 nuggets of the respective metal, usable in crafting, or can be smelted back to a bar.

In the case of a [[Bar of Hard Metal]], the Crucible must be fueled with [[Coal]].
sections.Quality:
The quality of a nugget is always the same as the bar being crafted into nuggets. Neither a character softcap or the qualities of the Crucible/fuel affects the quality of nuggets.

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Nutjerky}}

==How to Acquire==

Place an [[Unbaked Nutjerky]] in a [[Fireplace]] and allow to bake for 18 minutes.

Alternatively, [[Kiln]] or [[Oven]] with 4 branches can be used.

==[[FEP]]==

FEPs obtained will depend entirely on the meat used. Types of nuts and leaves used will not matter.

==Notes==

Quality softcapped by <math>\sqrt[3]{Perception * Survival * Lore}</math>

Ingredient variability is only applied on what type of meat you use in the recipe; it does not matter what type of nuts or leaves you use.

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

sections.How to Acquire:
Place an [[Unbaked Nutjerky]] in a [[Fireplace]] and allow to bake for 18 minutes.

Alternatively, [[Kiln]] or [[Oven]] with 4 branches can be used.
sections.Notes:
Quality softcapped by <math>\sqrt[3]{Perception * Survival * Lore}</math>

Ingredient variability is only applied on what type of meat you use in the recipe; it does not matter what type of nuts or leaves you use.

Odd Tuber.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem        = 1
| yitem        = 1
|
| objectsreq   = Hand or [[requires::Wooden Shovel]] or [[requires::Metal Shovel]]
| producedby   = [[Terraforming#Digging|Digging]] [[soil]]
| specific     = Tuber
|
| lpgain       = 1750
| studytime    = 16:00
| mentalweight = 2
| expdrain     = 12
}}

The {{PAGENAME}} is a [[Curiosity]] which can be used in the [[Study Report]] to earn [[Learning Points]].

== How to Acquire ==

Can be found randomly while [[Terraforming#Digging|digging]] for [[Soil]].

==Quality==

The quality is equal to the soil being dug.

{{Game Development}}

[[Category:Meat Dishes]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Meat Dishes|O$dds & Ends}}

==How to Acquire==

You need a frying pan and an open fire to make Odds & Ends.

==Quality==

Ingredients Q = <math>\frac{_{q}AvgOdd Tubers + _{q}AvgMeat}{2}</math>

Odds & Ends Quality = <math>\frac{(_{q}Ingredients)* 3 + _{q}FryingPan}{4}</math>

Ingredients Q is truncated before being worked with the [[Frying Pan]].

[[Softcap]]ped by [[Perception]] and [[Cooking]].

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

{{PAGENAME}}s can be gathered from an Olive Tree. They can be eaten, and the remaining [[Olive Kernel|kernel]] can be planted to grow more Olive Trees.

==How to Acquire==

Right click on an [[Olive Tree]] and the option to pick a Olive will be presented.

==Notes==
Olives can be turned into [[Olive Oil]] using an [[Extraction Press]].

[[Category:Baked Goods]]

{{GM|copy=Unbaked Olive Bread}}

This [[:Category:Baked Goods|food]] fills 5% of the [[hunger]] bar, 500% of the energy bar.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce [[Unbaked Olive Bread]]. Place the dough in an [[Oven]], then fuel it with 4 [[Branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as Bark Bread, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[Ash]].

Time of baking is approximately 16 minutes 40 seconds.

{{Game Development}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Wildgrub|$Onioned Escargot}}

Onioned Escargot is a food that is crafted by cooking a [[Any Snail]] over boiled cauldron.
The dish is useful for rapidly filling the [[Hearthling]]'s energy.

==Quality==

The [[Quality]] of the Onioned Escargot is Average ingredients and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[2]{Perception*Cooking}</math>.

==Variable Ingredients==

Red onions increase Intelligence+1 by 1, Yellow onions increase Charisma by +1. Leeks increase Constitution by +1.

Orca.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = 2.344 | yobst = 7.162 | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 80 | | armor = 100 | hp = 20000 | fhp = 14000 (Bleeds for 400 hp per tick when fleeing) | | loot = Orca Chunk x8:
[[Raw Orca]] x6(48 total),[[Animal Fat]] x2(16 total),
Orca Carcass:
[[Orca Brain]], [[Orca Tooth]] x5, [[Whale Bone Material]] x12 }} Orcas are large toothed whales which live in [[Deep Water|Very Deep Ocean]]. They can spawn solo or in pods of 2-4. To butcher a creature, you must first tow it to the [[Knarr]] or [[Snekkja]] and take it to the shore, secondly drag on the ground with the help of a horse. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Orcas are aggressive, and will likely engage in combat with nearby players. Aggroing any one orca will cause the entire pod to also engage. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:0 ! Restorations:0  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | | |} ===Strategy=== Beware, Orcas are strong creatures, capable of outrunning a [[Knarr]], also the problem in numbers. Note that unlike other creatures, orcas never split - so you can't aggro them one by one. You can Safespot them by luring them to the edge of Very Deep Ocean, they will not follow you onto Deep Ocean terrain. ==In-Game Example(s)== ==Notes== *Dead Orca can be hooked to knarr and lifted on сoast. Then dragged on land by the horse. *Butchered in several pieces that can be lifted. *The quality of butchering tool affects the quality of the orca brain during the initial butchering {{Game Development}}

Oysters are often found in clusters but more can be seen based on [[Perception|perception]] and [[Exploration|exploration]]. Oyster quality is based on nodes similar to other resources. ==How to Acquire== They can be found in the shallow water of oceans. You can "Pick" them up in the same manner you would would a stinging nettle or rustroot. [[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]
[[Category:Mushrooms]]

A type of edible mushroom. [[{{PAGENAME}}]]s can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 100% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Strength]] in [[FEP]]s.

== How to Acquire ==

{{PAGENAME}}s can be found in 3 types of [[Forest Terrain|forests]] and 1 type of [[Grassland|grassland]]. If you happen across one, and have the foraging skill, you can harvest it. You need at least 150 [[Perception]] * [[Exploration]] score to spot {{PAGENAME}}s. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].

Parasol mushrooms can only be found in the autumn.

}}

[[Category:Mushrooms]]

A type of edible mushroom. Parboiled Morels can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 125% of the [[energy]] bar and gives 1 [[Perception]] in [[FEP]]s.

== How to Acquire ==

[[Morels]] can be found in [[Forest Terrain|forests]]. If you happen across one, and have the foraging skill, you can harvest it. There is no known way to purposely produce Morels, so they can only be found by chance and by increasing exploration and perception skills. Once foraged, place the Morels in the inventory of a cauldron that is boiling (you can place them in even before you light it, but cooking progress will not be made until the cauldron is boiling) and wait.  After some time has passed (approximately 18 minutes) the morels will be cooked and may be eaten.

==Gallery==

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the [[oven]], then fuel the oven with 4 [[branch]]es and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as pea pies, be certain they have the same bake time, otherwise they will turn into [[ash]].

Time of baking is 16 minutes and 40 seconds.

==How to Acquire==
{{PAGENAME}} is acquired from butchering a [[Cleaned Pelican]].
==Quality==
The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

Pelican's Pouch.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 2
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Plucked Pelican]]
| producedby = Hand
}}

Functions as a container. Can have a random ocean fish inside. Can put other items freely inside, most amusingly you can put the cleaned remains of the [[Pelican]] inside it's own Pouch. This has no function other than potentially saving inventory space (most effectively you want to carry [[Dead Pelican]]s to your base completely intact for best space efficiency.)

Items can unintentionally go inside the Pouch, for example if you use "shift+ right click" for "take all" on a container or a stockpile.

The pouch must be empty in order to be stuffed, so you don't need to worry about deleting valuables hidden inside a pouch when preparing it.

{{Game Development}}

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients for [[Plum Pudding Dough]], attach yourself into cauldron with boiling water and execute the recipe to produce the dough. Place the dough in the [[oven]], fuel it with 4 [[Branch|branches]] and light it. When baking other foods at the same time as Plum Puddings, be certain that they have the same bake time or else said other foods will be unfinished. (currently, all doughs should require 4 branches (equivalent to 2 coal) worth of fuel to bake in an oven.)

Poppy Crop.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Seeds of Poppy]] }} [[Category:Crops]] {{PAGENAME}} is a type of [[Farming|Crop]] which can be grown to yield farming products. ==Planting and Harvesting== '''Planting''' *To plant seeds you must first create a [[Terraforming|Plowed Field]]. *LEFT CLICK the seeds in your inventory and then RIGHT CLICK on the plowed field you wish to plant them on. *SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a seed, your cursor will change then select an area to plant. *''You may use a bucket to hold up to 1000 seeds.'' '''Harvesting''' *RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest. *SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from. *''Seeds will automatically stack into a bucket if equipped.'' ==Growing== {{PAGENAME}} growing speed affected by [[Bee Skep]], [[Credos#Farmer|Farmer Credo]] and hidden ground spots. ===Stages=== {{PAGENAME}} have four stages of growth: [[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_1.png|100px]] Stage 1: The poppy field just planted. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_2.png|100px]] Stage 2: The poppy field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_3.png|100px]] Stage 3: The poppy field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_4.png|100px]] Stage 4: The poppy field growing. Сannot be picked.
[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_5.png|100px]] Stage 5: Harvesting the poppy field provides 5-12 [[Seeds of Poppy|poppy seeds]] and 1 [[Poppy Flower]] depending on your farming [[skills]] and [[Credos#Farmer|farmer credo]].
[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_5.png|100px]] Secret Stage 6: After minimum 24 RL hours harvesting the poppy field may provides 10-24 [[Seeds of Poppy|poppy seeds]] and 1 [[Poppy Pod]] depending on your farming [[skills]] and [[Credos#Farmer|farmer credo]].

sections.Growing: {{PAGENAME}} growing speed affected by [[Bee Skep]], [[Credos#Farmer|Farmer Credo]] and hidden ground spots.
sections.Planting and Harvesting:
'''Planting'''
*To plant seeds you must first create a [[Terraforming|Plowed Field]]. 
*LEFT CLICK the seeds in your inventory and then RIGHT CLICK on the plowed field you wish to plant them on. 
*SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a seed, your cursor will change then select an area to plant.
*''You may use a bucket to hold up to 1000 seeds.''

'''Harvesting'''
*RIGHT CLICK on a crop to harvest.
*SHIFT+RIGHT CLICK on a crop, your cursor will change then select an area to harvest from.
*''Seeds will automatically stack into a bucket if equipped.''

[[File:Poppy_Crop_Stage_5.png|100px]] Secret Stage 6: After minimum 24 RL hours harvesting the poppy field may provides 10-24 [[Seeds of Poppy|poppy seeds]] and 1 [[Poppy Pod]] depending on your farming [[skills]] and [[Credos#Farmer|farmer credo]].

Poppy pods can be eaten as are, but can also be dried on a herbalist table for {{time|G&Rhm|3|0|0}} into [[dried poppy pod]]s. [[Dried poppy pod]]s can be ground to [[poppy grist]], which can be boiled in a [[cauldron]] and thus reduced to [[opium]]

==How to Acquire==

Can be obtained at the secret stage (After the last and which is fairly random in terms of when it is actually reached) of the [[Poppy Crop]].

Generally allowing poppies a few extra Real-Life days after they have fully grown before you harvest them will yield poppy pods, with a greater percentage of your poppy crop reaching this secret stage the longer you wait (more testing needed)

metaobj.caption: No line/hook » No hook » Fully equipped
metaobj.objectsreq: [[requires::Tree Bough]] x3; [[requires::Block of Wood]] x1, [[requires::String]] x4, [[requires::Leather]] x1, [[requires::Cloth]] x1;[[requires::Fishline]], [[requires::Fish Hook]], [[requires::Fishing Lure]]

Primitive Casting-Rod.rawWikitext: {{infobox metaobj | caption = No line/hook » No hook » Fully equipped | | xitem = 1 | yitem = 3 | | skillreq = [[requires::Fishing]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Tree Bough]] x3; [[requires::Block of Wood]] x1, [[requires::String]] x4, [[requires::Leather]] x1, [[requires::Cloth]] x1;[[requires::Fishline]], [[requires::Fish Hook]], [[requires::Fishing Lure]] | producedby = Hand | | slot = 6L or 6R }} {{GM|$Craft|$Clothes & Equipment|Hook, $Line & Sinker|Primitive Casting-Rod}} The {{PAGENAME}} is, as the name suggests, a tool used to catch fish, one of the staple food source early on for beginners. It must be equipped to one hand and is used by the Fish command on a body of water. For more information check the [[Fishing]] page. The {{PAGENAME}} uses [[Fishing Lure|lures]] instead of bait. (The lures menu-section become available after crafting the {{PAGENAME}}.) ==How to Acquire== Create a pole with the crafting recipe. Now, you need to add a line, a hook, and some kind of fishing lure before you can use the fishing pole. '''[[Hook]]''' The most accessible hook is the [[Bone Hook|bone hook]], which is made from [[Bone Material|bones]], easily obtained from animals, particularly [[Chicken|chickens]] and [[Squirrel|squirrels]]. The [[Metal Hook|metal hook]] requires a [[Nugget of Common Metal]]. '''[[Fishline]]''' As of {{Patch|Fishing for Finery}} fishline is now a crafted item with 8 varieties. The simplest one is [[Bushcraft Fishline]], made out of 2 [[String]]. '''[[Fishing Lure]]''' To fish with this casting rod you need to have some kind of lure equipped. Early accessible fishing lures are: [[Woodfish]], [[Rock Lobster Lure]], [[Feather Fly]] and [[Pinecone Plug]]. If you have a farm set up, then [[Poppy Wobbler]] is a good choice too. To equip the fishing pole with fishing line and other necessities, left click on the item to select it and then right click it onto the fishing pole. If you want to swap something out, you can take it apart again by right clicking on the fishing pole and selecting Unequip, which will return all the parts to your inventory. ==How to Fish== Cast into any body of water. After a few seconds, a list of fish appear with the chance to catch each type of fish. The number of options to appear is based on the character's [[Attributes#Will|Will]] and [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]]. The type of fish that appear and the chance to catch them are determined by your fishing gear, the moon phase, and the local fish node, along with a chance to catch that particular fish. The first percentage is the chance that the fish will bite and the second percentage is the chance to land the fish if it bites. The final percentage is the likelihood that you will obtain the fish. If you do not select an option, you will automatically pick the fish with the highest catching chance. ==Notes== *[[Attributes#Will|Will]] and [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]] determine the number of fish you can choose from using the following formula: 1+floor(\sqrt{will * survival}/20) * {{Patch|Parka Blewit}} now shows "Chance to Bite" and the "Chance to Land" * Fishing mechanics were changed in {{Patch|Swan Fishing}} {{Game Development}}

sections.How to Acquire:
Create a pole with the crafting recipe.

Now, you need to add a line, a hook, and some kind of fishing lure before you can use the fishing pole.


'''[[Hook]]'''
The most accessible hook is the [[Bone Hook|bone hook]], which is made from [[Bone Material|bones]], easily obtained from animals, particularly [[Chicken|chickens]] and [[Squirrel|squirrels]]. The [[Metal Hook|metal hook]] requires a [[Nugget of Common Metal]].

'''[[Fishline]]'''

As of {{Patch|Fishing for Finery}} fishline is now a crafted item with 8 varieties. The simplest one is [[Bushcraft Fishline]], made out of 2 [[String]].


'''[[Fishing Lure]]'''

To fish with this casting rod you need to have some kind of lure equipped. Early accessible fishing lures are: [[Woodfish]], [[Rock Lobster Lure]], [[Feather Fly]] and [[Pinecone Plug]]. If you have a farm set up, then [[Poppy Wobbler]] is a good choice too.

To equip the fishing pole with fishing line and other necessities, left click on the item to select it and then right click it onto the fishing pole. If you want to swap something out, you can take it apart again by right clicking on the fishing pole and selecting Unequip, which will return all the parts to your inventory.
sections.How to Fish:
Cast into any body of water.

After a few seconds, a list of fish appear with the chance to catch each type of fish. The number of options to appear is based on the character's [[Attributes#Will|Will]] and [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]].

The type of fish that appear and the chance to catch them are determined by your fishing gear, the moon phase, and the local fish node, along with a chance to catch that particular fish. The first percentage is the chance that the fish will bite and the second percentage is the chance to land the fish if it bites. The final percentage is the likelihood that you will obtain the fish.

If you do not select an option, you will automatically pick the fish with the highest catching chance.

| hunger     = 0.5

|
| sat1       = Fruit
}}

{{PAGENAME}} are a simple [[Charisma]] food produced by drying [[Grapes]].

==How to Acquire==

Place [[Grapes]] on an [[Herbalist Table]]. They will turn into raisins in 50 RL minutes.

==Notes==

Turning grapes into raisins will ''not'' produce any [[Grape Seeds]], unlike eating or pressing the grapes. Be careful not to forfeit opportunity for planting higher quality vines.

==Quality==

The quality is the average between the Herbalist Table and Grapes.


<math>RaisinsQ = \frac{TableQ + GrapesQ}{2}</math>

{{Game Development}}

Raspberries can be gathered from a Raspberry Bush. They can be eaten or planted to grow more Raspberry Bushes.

Plant them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] with 4 [[Soil]] and 1 liter of [[Water]], then placed them on an [[Herbalist Table]] to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

Raw Adder.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Clean Adder Carcass]]
| producedby = [[requires::Sharp Tool]]
| specific   = Raw Meat
}}

==How to Acquire==
{{PAGENAME}} meat is acquired from butchering a [[Clean Adder Carcass]].

==Quality==

The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>_{q}Tool < _{q}Corpse</math> then <math>_{q}Meat = (_{q}Corpse + _{q}Tool)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < _{q}Corpse</math> then <math>_{q}Meat = (_{q}Corpse + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

==Quality==

#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

==Variable Ingredients==

As a raw meat ingredient, {{PAGENAME}} can be used in a variety of recipes. The table below shows the amount of FEPs added/removed from the original recipe's FEPs. Use it when considering which meat makes the best use of this ingredient for the stat you want to raise. 

Make sure to consider individual meat types can be used in specific recipes that other meat types cannot ({{PAGENAME}} can be used to make [[Cavedog]] on top of the below recipes, for instance). 

Also take note other required ingredients and possible spices in recipes can change the final FEP values (example: [[Onion Skewer]] will also consider what type of onion you're using).

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center"
!Recipe
! colspan="5" | FEP changes per meat (at q10)
|-
|[[Autumn Steak]]
|
|-
|[[Butcher's Surprise]]
|0.2 PER, -0.25 CHA (x2)
|-
|[[Encumbered Roast]]
|0.2 PER
|-
|[[Jotun's Morsel]]
| 
|-
|[[Meat-in-Jelly]]
| 
|-
|[[Onion Skewer]]
| 0.63 PER
|-
|[[Pumpkin Stew]]
| 0.95 PER, -0.125 WIL +2 (x2)
|-
|[[Raw Kebab Roll]]
|
|-
|[[Steak & Tubers]]
|
|-
|[[Unbaked Meatpie]]
|
|-
|}

| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|

| producedby = Hand

}}

Crab Meat is an easily-accessible [[Strength]] and [[Dexterity]] food. It is used to make [[Crab Cakes]] and [[Clambake]]. It can be equipped on a [[Fishing Pole]] to be used as [[Bait]].

==How to Acquire==

Crack open a [[Crab]] or a [[Crab Claw]] to receive Crab Meat.

==Quality==

Crab Meat quality is [[softcap|softcapped]] by the player's [[Abilities#Survival|Survival]] ability.

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Meat Dishes|$Raw Kebab Roll}}

Create a meat dish ready to be cooked on a spit.

==How to Acquire==
Have 8 of any raw meat. You can also add 3 spices to improve the kebab.

==Notes==
*Meat and spices has a significant effect on end product.
*Provides 10 pieces of [[Kebab Meat]] when ready.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
SpitroastedRawKebab.png | frame | A raw kebab roll put on a spit.
SpitroastedKebab.png | frame | Roasted Kebab.
</gallery>

Raw Lynx.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Lynx|Lynx corpse]]
| producedby = [[requires::Sharp tool]]
| specific   = Raw Meat
}}

==How to Acquire==
{{PAGENAME}} meat is acquired from butchering a [[Lynx]] corpse.

==Quality==

The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

|

| producedby = Hand
}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Wildgrub|$Pitbaked Goods|$Mushroom-Burst Glutton}}

An uncooked [[Mushroom-Burst Glutton]] made of a cleaned animal carcass stuffed full of mushrooms.

==How to Use==

Place into a [[fireplace]] at full heat and wait for 35 minutes to receive a [[Mushroom-Burst Glutton]].

sections.How to Use: Place into a [[fireplace]] at full heat and wait for 35 minutes to receive a [[Mushroom-Burst Glutton]].

| objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Horse]]
| producedby = [[requires::Sharp Tool]]

== How to acquire ==
Raw Wildhorse is acquired from butchering a [[Wild Horse]]


== Quality ==
The quality of Raw Wildhorse while butchering is determined by quality of the animal and [[Softcap|softcapped]] by your sharp tool quality and survival.


*If (ToolQ < HideQ) then <math>HideQ=(HideQ+ToolQ)/2</math>


*If (Survival < HideQ) then <math>HideQ=(HideQ+Survival)/2</math>



Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

Red Apple.rawWikitext:
<small>''"Apple" redirects here. For other cultivar, see "[[Green Apple]]".''</small>
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| producedby = [[requires::Apple Tree]][[seed of::Apple Tree| ]]
| specific   = Fruit
|
| energy     = 150
| hunger     = 0.5
| con        = 1
|
| sat1       = Fruit
}}

A staple fruit, {{PAGENAME}}s can be gathered from an Apple Tree. They can be eaten, and the remaining [[Red Apple Core|core]] can be planted to grow more Apple Trees. They can be baked into [[Apple Pie|pie]]s or squashed into [[Applejuice]] in a [[Winepress]] which can be drunk or fermented into [[Cider]].

==How to Acquire==

Right click on an [[Apple Tree]] and the option to pick an Apple will be presented.

==Notes==

When picking apples you sometimes receive a [[Worm-Eaten Apple]] instead.


When tree planting using a Treeplanter’s pot, note that you can put an apple into the pot, but it will die immediately when dried.  Instead, only use an apple core. (More testing needed, might have been my low skills)

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Garden Greens|Re$d-Shred Salad}}

This [[:Category:Foods|food]] fills 400% of the [[energy]] bar, and gives [[Constitution]], [[Intelligence]], [[Charisma]], and sometimes [[Dexterity]] [[FEP]]s, based on which ingredients are used.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the ingredients and execute the recipe to make red-shred salad. In order to craft this recipe you will need to discover all the ingredients.

==Variable Ingredients==

Variable ingredients allow different fep outcomes according to the particular items used. These are added to the base FEPs for the food type.

| yitem      = 1

| producedby = [[requires::Redcurrant Bush]][[seed of::Redcurrant Bush| ]]
| specific   = Fruit, Berry, Seed of Tree or Bush
|
| energy     = 50
| hunger     = 0.1
| agi        = 0.1
|
| sat1       = Berries
}}

{{PAGENAME}}s can be gathered from a Redcurrant Bush. They can be eaten or planted to grow more Redcurrant Bushes.

Plant them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] with 4 [[Soil]] and 1 liter of [[Water]], then placed them on an [[Herbalist Table]] to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

Reindeer.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(12/11)-(-6/11)round6}} | | baseq = 30 | | armor = 0 | hp = 200 | fhp = 110 | | loot = [[Fresh Reindeer Hide]], [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] x2, [[Raw Reindeer Venison]] x6, [[Bone Material]] x4, [[Reindeer Antlers]] }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] The reindeer, also known as the caribou, is a species of deer with circumpolar distribution, native to Arctic, sub-Arctic, tundra, boreal and mountainous regions. Reinder also spawn more commonly in the snows of Winter. Their most important gameplay function is that they can be [[Taming|tamed]] to become a [[Tame Reindeer]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you aggro them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:2 ! Restorations:2  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Low Horn Swipe}} {{:Mule Kick}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Reindeer attack quickly and have the potential to snowball with openings. Riding a horse is recommended as you can kite and lower your openings. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be cleaned and butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Reindeer Venison]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Reindeer Antlers]] and [[Bone Material]].
{{Game Development}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|Render Fat}}

Can be used as an alternative to butter in various recipes. Created with 3 pieces of [[Animal Fat]] in a boiling [[cauldron]].

==Quality==

<math>Q_{Rendered Animal Fat}=\frac{AvgQ_{Animal Fat}*6+Q_{Cauldron}+Q_{Water}}8</math> and '''''(not yet tested)''''' [[Glossary#Softcap|softcapped]] by the player's [[Perception]] and [[Abilities#Cooking|Cooking]]

==Notes==
* Since a piece of (non-rendered) Animal Fat can be made from 3 pieces of any meat, you can turn 9 pieces of meat into a single Rendered Animal Fat.

Rennet.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| skillreq     = [[requires::Cheesemaking]]
| discoveryreq = [[Milk]](any)
| objectsreq   = [[requires::Suckling's Maw]], [[requires::Vinegar]] (0.3 liters)
| producedby   = [[requires::Cauldron]] (with 1.0 liters)
}}

[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|$Rennet}}

Rennet is used to make [[Curd]], which is required to produce [[Cheese]].

==How to Acquire==

In order to craft Rennet you need an empty [[Bucket]], [[Suckling's Maw]] and at least 0.3 liters of [[Vinegar]] in your inventory.

Furthermore, you need a [[Cauldron]] to make rennet. Add Water and fuel to the cauldron, light it and execute the recipe.

The quality of the Water, Vinegar, and Suckling's Maw used all appear to play a role in quality. Cauldron quality also appears to have an affect.
Rennet quality is softcapped by perception and cooking.  Container and fuel quality do not matter.

When crafted it will fill up the container with 1 liter of Rennet. Pour the rennet along with [[Milk]] into a [[Curding Tub]] to start producing curd.

To discover Rennet, one must first discover some type of [[Milk]] in addition to the required ingredients.

==Formula==

Crafting: <math>_{q}Vinegar*2 + _{q}Water + _{q}Suckling's Maw*2 + _{q}Cauldron\over 6</math>

Note: For Clay Cauldron the water quality is halved first. ... <small><math>(\frac{_{q}Water}{2})</math></small>


[[Softcap]]ped: <math>\sqrt{Cooking*Perception}</math>

River Pearl Mussel.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Foraging]]
| objectsreq = None
| producedby = Hand
| terrain    = [[terrain::Shallow Water|River Shallow Water]]
| specific   = Edible Seashell, Frutti di Mare
|
| perexp     = 760
}}

[[Category:Foraged]]
[[Category:Home Decor]]

Raw mussels can be cooked into [[Boiled River Pearl Mussel|Boiled Mussels]]. While boiling, there is a chance for the mussel to contain a [[River Pearl]]. Mussels are often found in clusters but more can be seen based on [[Perception|perception]] and [[Exploration|exploration]]. Mussel quality is based on nodes similar to other resources.

To  boil it you require [[Fishing]].

==How to Acquire==
Mussels can be found '''exclusively''' in the shallow water of rivers. You can "Pick" them up in the same manner you would would a stinging nettle or rustroot. Generally, you start to see mussels around 760 per*exp (eg. 19*40). 

==Gallery==

</gallery>

Eating a serving of roasted [[Ant Meat]] fills 400% [[energy]] and 1% [[hunger]], and gives 2 [[Will]] and 2 [[Agility]] in [[FEP]]s.

Immediately After cooking your Raw Meat, you will get '''Sizzling {{PAGENAME}}'''. The '''Sizzling''' Buff grants 25% extra FEP for 30 minutes, after which it will turn back into {{PAGENAME}}.

==How to Acquire==

Light a [[Fire]] or [[Fireplace]], then use the {{K|C}}raft -> Coo{{K|k}}ing -> {{K|R}}oasted Meat function to create {{PAGENAME}}.

==Quality==

The quality of Roast Meat is [[Softcap]]ped by your [[Survival#Survival|survival]] and [[Perception#Perception|perception]].

| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Hunting]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Raw Beaver]]
| producedby = [[requires::Fire]] (lit) or [[requires::Fireplace]] (lit) or [[requires::Grid Iron]] (assembled)
|
| energy     = 400
| hunger     = 1
| agi        = 1
| wil        = 1
|
| sat1       = Meat
}}

[[Category:Roast Meat]]

==How to Acquire==

Light a [[Fire]] or [[Fireplace]], then use the {{K|C}}raft -> Coo{{K|k}}ing -> {{K|R}}oasted Meat function to create {{PAGENAME}}.
All Roast Meat provide 400% Energy for 1% Hunger

Immediately After cooking your Raw Meat, you will get '''Sizzling {{PAGENAME}}'''. The '''Sizzling''' Buff grants 25% extra FEP for 30 minutes, after which it will turn back into {{PAGENAME}}.

==Quality==

The quality of Roast Meat is [[Softcap]]ped by your [[Survival#Survival|survival]] and [[Perception#Perception|perception]].

[[Category:Roast Meat]]

==How to Acquire==

Light a [[Fire]] or [[Fireplace]], then use the {{K|C}}raft -> Coo{{K|k}}ing -> {{K|R}}oasted Meat function to create {{PAGENAME}}.
All Roast Meat provide 400% Energy for 1% Hunger

Immediately After cooking your Raw Meat, you will get '''Sizzling {{PAGENAME}}'''. The '''Sizzling''' Buff grants 25% extra FEP for 30 minutes, after which it will turn back into {{PAGENAME}}.

==Quality==

The quality of Roast Meat is [[Softcap]]ped by your [[Survival#Survival|survival]] and [[Perception#Perception|perception]].

[[Category:Roast Meat]]

== How to Acquire ==

Light a [[Fire]] or [[Fireplace]], then use the {{K|C}}raft -> {{K|F}}ood -> {{K|R}}oast Meat function to create {{PAGENAME}}.
All Roast Meat provide 400% Energy for 1% Hunger

Immediately After cooking your Raw Meat, you will get '''Sizzling {{PAGENAME}}'''. The '''Sizzling''' Buff grants 25% extra FEP for 30 minutes, after which it will turn back into {{PAGENAME}}.

==Quality==

The quality of Roast Meat is [[Softcap]]ped by your [[Survival#Survival|survival]] and [[Perception#Perception|perception]].

Eating a serving of roasted [[Troll]] fills 800% [[energy]] and 1% [[hunger]], and gives 6 [[Strength]]+2 and 4 [[Psyche]]+2 in [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

You need to lit a [[fire]] in order to roast meat.

{{K|C}}raft > {{K|F}}ood > {{K|R}}oast Meat

The quality of the roasted meat depends on your [[Perception]] and [[Survival]].

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Wildgrub|$Roasthopper}}

Roast hopper is a food that is crafted by cooking a [[Grasshopper]] over a fire.  The dish is useful for rapidly filling the [[Hearthling]]'s energy.

==Quality==

The [[Quality]] of the Roasthopper = Grasshopper and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[2]{Perception*Survival}</math>.

Roe Deer.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = 0.422 | yobst = 1.078 | | deadly = no | | baseq = 20 | | armor = 10 | hp = 150 | fhp = 80 | | loot = [[Fresh Roe Deer Hide]] x1-2, [[Entrails]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Raw Roe Venison]] x8, [[Bone Material]] x4, [[Roe Deer Antlers]] (male only) }} The first wild creature to feature both genders. Males have horns and are the only ones to attack, while females flee. =Combat= ==Behaviour== Not aggressive until provoked. Attacking one {{PAGENAME}} also provokes all male members of the flock. They're extremely fast on foot; you'll need a horse on at least 3rd, sometimes even 4th speed to outpace them. == Strategy == Roe deer are lesser versions of their [[Red Deer|Red]] cousins. They are weak in HP and power, but extremely fast hitting. Early on, it's best to fight them in a boat or near a cave you can duck into; fighting on horseback is possible but they're so quick on their feet one deer will waste most of your pony power. You'll need around 40 AGI and MC/UA, and some weapon and melee move, to comfortably tussle with them. They attack quickly, but their damage output is low; let them stack openings on you while you spam Quick Barrage or Sideswipe until their openings are at 60-70%. Opportunity Knocks can help here. Duck into a cave or row your boat back and restore yourself when your openings reach 40% or so. Once their openings are high, use Cleave or Full Circle. Just one or two hits will leave them fleeing. It's best to peace them out, then either reaggro later, or put an arrow in them, to finish them off. {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! '''Attacks''' ! '''Restorations''' |- | {{:Mule Kick}}{{:Bear Down}} | {{:Bristle}} |} =In-Game Example(s)= [[File:Dead Roe Deer.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:Skinned Roe Deer.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Roe Venison]].
[[File:Skeleton Roe Deer.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]] and [[Roe Deer Antlers]].
=Notes=

[[Category:Dungeon Loot]]

Royal Jelly is an item obtained inside [[Beehive Dungeon]] from the bee chests scattered inside certain rooms. 

==How to Use==

Royal Jelly can be used to increase the larvae production of a [[Bee Larvae]] and raise it's quality by +1.

Right-click the item on a Bee Skep to improve it, you can only improve a skep with Royal Jelly once but you can improve the same skep with [[Propolis|Propolis.]] 

A visual difference is noticed in the entrance of the skep showing the white jelly.

Salt.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Masonry]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Rock Salt]] or [[requires::Salt Crystal]]
| producedby = [[requires::Quern]]
| specific   = Spices
}}

{{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|Grind $Salt}}

It's a [[Spices|spice]] that can be used in various recipes for additional Hunger buff.


==How to Acquire==
You need a [[Quern]] to make a salt form [[Rock Salt]] or [[Salt Crystal]]s.
*Grinding salt produce 0.2-0.35 kg from 1 piece of [[Rock Salt]] and around the same amount from [[Salt Crystal]]s.

==Quality==
*[[Salt]] Quality is Average ingredients and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by <math>\sqrt[2]{Dexterity*Masonry}</math>.

*The [[Quern]] has no effect on quality.

==Notes==
*Also known as Halite.
*As a [[Spices|Spice]] uses 0.01 kg in recipes.
*As a powder disappears instantly if dropped to the ground. You may want to avoid using keyboard shortcuts involving Control-key to avoid accidents, or hold salt in a [[Bucket]] to be safe.
*When grinding salt there needs to be at least some free place* to receive the ground salt (like a free inventory slot (character or other container), bucket, barrel, or a pile of ground pepper with some space left.) or the grinding will stop after trying. (''... unlike some other crafting processes that take/remove the used ingredients before returning the crafted product.'')

{{Game Development}}

Seeds of Cucumber.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Windsown Weed]] | producedby = [[requires::Cucumber Crop]] | specific = Crop Seeds }} [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] [[Category:Seeds]] {{PAGENAME}} are used to plant [[Cucumber]]s You need 5 {{PAGENAME}} to plant one tile of this crop. You can stack {{PAGENAME}} in stacks of 50 in your inventory, or store 1000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Bucket]], or store 10000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Barrel]]. ==How to Acquire== Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]]. ==How to Use== In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. ==Notes== [[Cucumber]]s are a [[Trellis]] Crop and can only grow on [[Trellis]]es

When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between ?? and ?? {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]]. ==How to Use== In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. {{Game Development}}

You can stack {{PAGENAME}} in stacks of 50 in your inventory, or store 1000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Bucket]], or store 10000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Barrel]]. ==How to Acquire== Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]]. As of the {{Patch|Opium Nomad}} update, harvesting poppies as a flower yields less seeds. Harvesting a poppy with a [[Poppy Pod]] yields the normal amount of seeds (10-15 depending on skill), but gives no flower. Note that there is no indicator of when this transition occurs. ==How to Use== In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. ==Notes== {{PAGENAME}} are also used to make [[Ring of Brodgar Dough]]

Sheep.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | caption = Ram » Ewe » Lamb | | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(5/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | | terrain = (''None, Domestic Areas'') | | baseq = 10 | | hp = 200 | fhp = 100 | | loot = [[Sheep's Milk]], [[Wool]], [[Fresh Sheepskin]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x2, [[Raw Mutton]] x3, [[Bone Material]] x3, [[Suckling's Maw]] from youngs. }} [[Category:Domesticated]] [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Sheep are tamed from [[Mouflon]]s. See [[Taming]] for more information about taming. Sheep are the only source of [[Wool]] other than tamed [[Goat]]s and feeding [[Clover]]s to Mouflons or [[Wildgoat]]s. Sheep can only 'store' a maximum of 3 wool at a time. Like any other livestock, slaughtered sheep also provide meat, innards, and hide, and their [[Sheepskin]] is required to create a [[Kozhukh]]. Female sheep also produce [[Sheep's Milk|milk]], which can be refined into [[Cheese]]s unique to Sheepsmilk or into other dairy-related products. ==Animals== *A ram is a male sheep. They can impregnate female sheep (ewe). *An ewe is a female sheep. After being impregnated they give birth to a lamb in 4.5 days, and then start producing milk, of which they can store a maximum of 25L. *A lamb is an adolescent sheep. They will grow into either a ram or an ewe in 10 real life days. In addition to the regular products, lambs give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered. ===Wool=== Sheep produce [[Wool]] at a rate of 1 wool every 8 hours RL at Wool Quantity 5. You can increase the speed of growth by raising sheep with higher Wool Quantity, as well as the quality by raising sheep with higher Wool Quality, through breeding of sheep with high Food Eaten quality as well as with high Quantity/Quality levels. See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information. ==Tips & Tricks== It is a good idea to separate your rams from your ewes, to ensure that you can select the highest quality ram to mate with your ewes when you want lambs. *''Note: Although the game has some auto-naming for newborns, with different names for male and female. This is however not the case with sheep. Both male and female sheep's are initially named "Dolly", so make sure to check (and rename when needed)''. ==Gallery== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Sheepskin]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Mutton]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
{{Game Development}}

You can increase the speed of growth by raising sheep with higher Wool Quantity, as well as the quality by raising sheep with higher Wool Quality, through breeding of sheep with high Food Eaten quality as well as with high Quantity/Quality levels.

See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information.

{{PAGENAME}} can be gathered from a [[Blackthorn Bush]]. They can be eaten or planted to grow more Blackthorn Bushes.

Plant them in a [[Treeplanter's Pot]] with 4 [[Soil]] and 1 liter of [[Water]], then placed them on an [[Herbalist Table]] to begin the sprouting process. If it sprouts, you have 8 hours to plant it. If it dies, you must empty the pot and start over again.

Snow.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| objectsreq = dig on '''[snow tiles]''' by hand or a [[shovel]], or [[requires::Ice Block]] using [[pickaxe]]
}}

'''Snow''' can, outside the winter season, always be found covering the peaks of [[Mountain]] biomes and can be obtained by digging. It is used to craft [[Snowman|Snowmen]], [[Snowball|Snowballs]], and, most importantly, the healing item [[Cold Compress]].

Snow normally decays 12 hours after being collected<!-- ?:also in winter season -->. To keep the snow from melting it needs to be stored in a cellar. Which slows/stops the melting for about a whole game year. How the snow is stored, stockpiled or in containers, don't matters.

Snow can be melted by placing it into a lit [[cauldron]]. Cauldron should have at least 5 liters of boiling water. Each snow piece converts to 1 liter of water roughly in 1 minute which adds to cauldron. Water quality is not affected by cauldron.

==How to Acquire==

Use {{GM|inline=|$Adventure|$Dig}} on snow covered ground.

*For mountain snow the hearthling will keep digging the same tile.
*For winter snow covered ground it will just do on tile, exposing the terrain below it.
** To collect snow from more than one tile use {{key|Shift|Right-Mouse-Click}} on the snow and then select the area you like to clear of snow (''shovel highly advised''). (''this option don't works for mountain snow.'')

Spindly Taproot.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Foraging]]
| objectsreq = None
| terrain    = [[terrain::Forest]]
| specific   = String
|
| perexp     = 10
|
| energy     = 100
| hunger     = 1
}}

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]

A wild taproot that is a source of [[food]] and [[string]].

"If an item/building requires a string, you can use Spindly Taproot as a substitute, so think of it as a string."

== How to Acquire ==

Spindly Taproots can be found growing in the wild, where they can be picked.

It can be eaten without preparation or used as string.

They are the easiest forage item to find, lowest possible find was 10 PER*EX(10 perception, 1 exploration)

==Quality==

[[Hardcap|hardcapped]] by [[Survival]].

Spitroast Cave Angler.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| image_basemeat = fish
|
| xitem          = 1
| yitem          = 1
|
| skillreq       = [[requires::Hunting]], [[requires::Fishing]]
| objectsreq     = [[requires::Cave Angler]]
| producedby     = [[requires::Roasting Spit]] and [[requires::Fireplace]]
|
| energy         = 400
| hunger         = 0.5
| int            = 4.5
| str            = 3
|
| sat1           = Fish
}}

[[Category:Spitroast Fish]]

==How to Acquire==

With a [[Roasting Spit]] over a [[Fireplace]], Right click the [[Cave Angler]] corpse onto the spit. Roasting [[Fishing|fish]] this way increases the amount of meat by 50%, but also denies the collection of Entrails, Intestines, Bones, and Scales.
Immediately after cooking your [[Fishing|fish]], you will get '''Sizzling {{PAGENAME}}'''. The '''Sizzling''' Buff grants 25% extra FEP for 30 minutes, after which it will turn back into {{PAGENAME}}.

You get 16 pieces of meat and 2 [[Bone Ash]].

==Quality==

The quality of spitroast fish is equal to <math>\frac{_{q}Roasting Spit + _{q}Fish}{2}</math> and is [[Softcap]]ped by the [[Survival]] of the character who places the fish on the spit.
And [[Bone Ash]] quality is halved.

Stale Grapejuice.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | image = File:Grapejuice.png | | skillreq = none | objectsreq = [[requires::Grapejuice]] }} [[Category:Liquids]] [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] ==How to Acquire== Grapejuice will decay into Stale Grapejuice when left longer than 5 hours in any container other than a [[barrel]] or a [[demijohn]] (for example, leaving it in the [[Extraction Press]] or in a [[bucket]]).
Grapejuice placed in a demijohn will ferment into Wine.
Grapejuice placed in a barrel will not ferment or decay.
==Use== There seems to be no use other than to ferment into [[Vinegar]], which happens by itself after a week, unless put in a barrel. If you add more grapejuice later, the fermentation doesn't reset but averages.

|

| cha        = 2
| str        = 2
| con2       = 2
|
| sat1       = Vegetables
| sat2       = Meat
}}

[[Category:Meat Dishes]]
[[Category:Vegetables]]

{{GM|$Craft|$Food|$Meat Dishes|Stea$k & Tubers}}

'''The food satiation needs verification.'''

==How to Acquire==

You need a frying pan and an open fire to make Steak & Tubers.

==Quality==

Ingredients Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Meat + _{q}Onion + _{q}AvgTuber}{3}</math>

Steak & Tubers Quality = <math>\frac{(_{q}Ingredients)* 3 + _{q}FryingPan}{4}</math>

Ingredients Q is truncated before being worked with the Frying Pan.

==Ingredients==

Variable ingredients are 1 meat, 1 onion and 2 tubers.  So far yellow onion is charisma and red onion is strength for -this recipe-.  The meat has two sets of attributes which can be mixed.  Carrots give one set and beetroots the other set with a semi mix for one of each.  One carrot/beetroot gives a more volatile outcome, sometimes good (more feps) or sometimes bad (less feps).

==Notes==

* It counts as both [[:Category:Meat|Meat]] and [[:Category:Vegetables|Vegetables]][//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?f=39&t=48519&p=634410#p634410]

{{Game Development}}

|

}}

[[Category:Home Decor]]

Stinging Nettle is an herb which can be found growing in forest terrain. A minimum of 25 [[Perception]]*[[Exploration]] is needed to find Stinging Nettle, making it one of the first plants a hearthling can find.
*Stinging Nettles can be used as [[string]], with the sole exception of nettles not being usable as a fishing line.
*Stinging Nettles are used in a few recipes, such as [[Nettle Pants]] and [[Nettle Shirt]].
*Picking a Stinging Nettle will occasionally give you the Nettle Burns wound which increases (''yes, increases'') survival by 1. Wearing any type of gloves makes you entirely incapable of getting Nettle Burns.

==Notes==
* Can be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].
* Can be stored in a [[Stockpile]].

:''Not to be confused with [[Wood Strawberry]]''

==How to Acquire==

The strawberry grows wild on the [[Flower Meadow]], [[Sombre Bramble]], [[Deep Tangle]] and [[Greens Ward]] terrain. They can also be grown in a [[Garden Pot]].

==How to Use==

Strawberries can be eaten by right clicking on them and clicking the "eat" option.

Strawberries can be used in recipes that ask for "[[Berry]]".

Following recipes specifically require Strawberries:
* [[Strawberries in Cream]]
* [[Strawberry Cake]]

</gallery>

==Trivia==

*Strawberries got their name before straw was used to protect them. Though the name's origin is not known for certain, it's possible that it describes the plants' runners.

Suckling's Maw.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = none
| objectsreq = [[requires::Cattle|Calf]], [[requires::Sheep|Lamb]], [[requires::Goat|Kid]] or [[requires::Horse|Foal]]
| producedby = any [[Sharp Tool]]
}}

Suckling's Maw is used to produce [[Rennet]] for making cheese, and [[Goldbeater's Skin]] for making [[Jewelry]].

==How to Acquire==

Slaughter a young [[Cattle|calf]], [[Sheep|lamb]], [[Goat|kid]] or [[Horse|foal]] of either gender, equip any [[sharp tool]] and butcher the animal to acquire one suckling's maw. It must be done within a few real days of the animal's birth, else you will not be able to collect the maws.

==Quality==
The quality will be same as quality of the meat, thereby affected by meat quality multiplier of the animal, and softcapped by tool quality and farming skill.

==Notes==

* Acquired during 'Clean' action

Tame Reindeer.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | image = file:Tame Reindeer.png | caption = Bull » Calf » Cow | | xobst = {{#expr:(2/11)-(-2/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(12/11)-(-6/11)round6}} | | terrain = (''None, Domestic Areas'') | | hp = (unknown) | | loot = [[Raw Tame Reindeer]] x5, [[Animal Fat]] x1, [[Intestines]] x1, [[Entrails]] x1, [[Fresh Tame Reindeer Hide]] x2, [[Bone Material]] x6, [[Reindeer Antlers]] from bulls, [[Suckling's Maw]] from calfs. }} [[Category:Domesticated]] [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] {{PAGENAME}} are tamed [[Reindeer]]s. See [[Taming]] for more information about taming. ==Animals== *'''Bull''' is a male reindeer that can impregnate(indicated through a mating animation) female reindeer. Also can pull [[Sleigh]]s. *'''Cow''' is a female reindeer. After being impregnated, they give birth to a calf(can have twins and triples on usual occasions) in 4.5 days. Also can pull [[Sleigh]]s. *'''Calf''' is an adolescent reindeer . They will grow either into a bock or doe(indicated in the top left side of their respective information windows) in 10 days. In addition to regular products, calves give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered. ==Stats & Quality== *The quality of an animal or any of stat is the one of parent quality\stat, +20 -> -5, and then [[softcap|softcapped]] by the breeding quality of the male involved and quality of eaten [[Swill]]. **See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information. ===Meat=== *Adult reindeer produce 5 [[Raw Tame Reindeer]] at Meat Quantity 10, modified by Meat Quantity stat. *Meat quality equal to reindeer's quality and modified by Meat Quality stat. ===Hide=== *Reindeer produce 2 [[Tame Reindeer Hide]] and quality equal to reindeer's quality, modified by Hide Quality stat. ==Tips & Tricks== *It is a good idea to seperate your bulls from your cows , to ensure that you can select the highest quality buck to mate with your does when you want calves. You can additionally castrate your lower quality bulls to stop them from breeding and to acquire [[Bollock]]. ==Gallery== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead Deer. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Tame Reindeer Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned Deer. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Tame Reindeer]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] Deer Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].

Troll.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-10/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-10/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 80 | | armor = 125 | hp = 1000+ | fhp = ~40% health | | loot = [[Fresh Troll Hide]] x2, [[Troll Meat]] x40, [[Trollbone]] x8, [[Troll Skull]]
and, depending on variation: *[[Troll Tusks]] *[[Troll's Third Eye]] *[[Troll Tail]] *[[Troll Hair]] *[[Troll Nose]] *[[Troll Mushrooms]] *[[Troll Ears]] *... }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Note: As Trolls are so rare, much of this information could be incorrect or up for debate, as some amount of it is still from Legacy. Trolls are the strongest creatures in the game, with a lot of HP, and the strongest hits per each strike. Trolls are also extremely rare, appearing when mining out a tile underground. They have about a 0.002% chance of spawning from each mining grid, although the Cave Hermit credo raises this probability significantly. They do not have ability to cross water, but don't try killing them from a boat in water or such since Trolls have a cave-in ranged attack. After gaining 3-4 ip it will cause a cave-in immediately ko'ing any character. Their level is dependent on the depth of the mine they are found in. They have 1000 HP per-mining-level, starting with 1000 HP at mining level 1 (Caves) and reaching 5000 HP at mining level 5. They can kill new clear character (alt) in 1 hit dealing about 300-750 damage in 1 hit, at the same time knocking out our character, and in the next hit - killing character. To hit, Troll sometimes has to gain 2 IP; It's giving a little moment before hit. Note: Troll spawning is not limited to Mine-tiles, but can also spawn on caved-in tiles (while mining). Also, there appears to be some evidence to suggest that trolls have nodes in the same way that ore and stone types have areas where they are more likely to appear. Raw Hide and Meat are [[softcap]]ped by [[Survival]] and your carving tool. Bones are only softcapped by Survival. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Trolls will beat their chest for at least 10 seconds after spawning, usually up to few minutes to give the player a chance to get away while they still can, however if a horse is in the range of the troll it can instantly aggro the horse and then the player. If troll can't reach you, he'll start using attacks that cause cave-in's. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:4 ! Restorations:2  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Troll Punch}} {{:Troll Smile}} {{:Devour}} {{:Dark Heart Move}} | {{:Thide}} {{:Peek in the Depth}} |} ===Strategy=== Level 3 Troll - is the same powerful as [[Mammoth]], but higher trolls are the strongest creature in the game. They can easily kill players due to the high damage amount of damage they deal and their cave-in attack which, if hit, will instantly KO the player. Trolls hit twice per attack, allowing them to do openings and deal damage even on the first attack round. ==Types of Trolls== Trolls come in different variations, each with their own unique drop. Combinations of these are possible. Food (Energy = 2000, Hunger = 2, INT= 25, PER = 75, PSY = 20) ---- You can watch from one eye to another (random, every ingame hour) |- |[[File:Troll-TypeTail.jpg|150px]] |Tailed |[[File:Troll_Tail.png|link=]] |[[Troll Tail]] |Food (1x2, Energy = 2000, Hunger = 2, AGI = 50, CON = 50, PSY = 20) |- |[[File:Troll-TypeHair.jpg|150px]] |Hairy |[[File:Troll_Hair.png|link=]] |[[Troll Hair]] |Base LP = 4000 Study Time = 9 Mental Weight = 4 EXP Cost = 250 |- |[[File:Troll-TypeNose.jpg|150px]] |Nosed |[[File:Troll_Nose.png|link=]] |[[Troll Nose]] |Food (2x1, Energy = 2000, Hunger = 2, AGI = 40, STR = 40, PSY = 20)) |- |[[File:Troll-TypeMushroom.jpg|150px]] |Mushroom |[[File:Troll_Mushrooms.png|link=]] |[[Troll Mushrooms]] |Food Energy 400% Hunger 1% Q100: PSY = 1.5 Satiation: Mushroom 10(5) and Game 5(5) ---- can be planted in garden pot |- |[[File:Troll-TypeEars.png|150px]] |Eared |[[File:Troll Ears.png]] |[[Troll Ears]] |Food (1x2, Energy = 2000, Hunger = 2, PER = 80, PSY = 20) |} ==In-Game Example(s) - Legacy== [[File:Legacy-DeadTroll.png]] [[File:Legacy-Troll 2.jpg]], [[File:Legacy-Troll 3.jpg]], [[File:Legacy-Troll dead.jpg]], [[File:Legacy-Troll butch.jpg]], [[File:Legacy-Troll skl.jpg]] {{Game Development}}

==How to Acquire==
{{PAGENAME}} meat is acquired from butchering a Troll corpse.

==Quality==

The quality of {{PAGENAME}} while butchering is determined by [[quality]] of the animal corpse and is [[softcap|softcapped]] by your [[sharp tool]] [[quality]] and [[survival]].
	
#If <math>ToolQ < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + ToolQ)/ 2</math>
#If <math>Survival < CorpseQ</math> then <math>MeatQ = (CorpseQ + Survival)/ 2</math>
Tool quality softcap is applied before the survival softcap.

The [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Curiosity]] which can be used in the [[Study Report]] to earn [[Learning Points]].

It can also be eaten, filling a massive 2000% [[energy]] for 2% [[hunger]], and giving 75 [[Perception]], 25 [[Intelligence]] and 20 [[Psyche]] in [[FEP]]s.

By right clicking the eye and selecting "peer into", it is also possible to look through it to see a random place in the world where someone has placed another troll's eye. After 10 to 20 minutes, the location displayed will change. If you want to show off a nicely decorated room, putting a troll's eye on the wall is a good idea.

This [[:Category:Foods|food]] fills 3% units of the [[hunger]] bar, and gives 3 [[Intelligence]], 3 [[Perception]], and 5 [[Charisma]] [[FEP]]s.

==How to Acquire==

You need a Churn to make Tuber Mash.

==Ingredients==

Variable ingredients are tubers. Spices are optional. Like all other food items, using spices will result in the quality of the Tuber Mash being the average between the initial ingredients, the churn used and the spice (Example: Non-spiced Q10 Tuber Mash would become Q25 if the player decides to use a Q40 spice).

Laurel Leaves increase the INT FEP by +3.5.

Vinegar.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| skillreq   = [[requires::Winemaking]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Stale Grapejuice]], [[requires::Wine|Stale Wine]], [[requires::Cider|Stale Cider]], [[requires::Perry|Stale Perry]] or [[requires::Mead Must|Stale Mead Must]]
}}

[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

You need vinegar to make [[rennet]].

==How to Acquire==
To produce vinegar, you need an [[Extraction Press]] and a lot of [[grapes]] (50 grapes for 10L). Leave the [[Grapejuice|grapejuice]] in the press or put it in a [[bucket]] and wait 24h for it to turn [[Stale Grapejuice|stale]]. After one week the stale grapejuice turns into vinegar. Wine left in any container other than a barrel or a [[Wine Bottle]] for longer than a week will also turn into vinegar.

Alternatively [[Apple]] via [[Applejuice]], [[Pear]] via [[Pearjuice]] and [[Mead Must]] made from [[Honey]] can be turned into vinegar.

Vinegar can be stored in any liquid container.

==Quality==
The quality of the vinegar depends solely on the quality of the base liquid.

Walrus.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(5/11)-(-5/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(15/11)-(-11/11)round6}} | | baseq = 50 | | armor = 75 | hp = 900 | fhp = 600 | | loot = [[Fresh Walrus Hide]] x2, [[Intestines]] x3, [[Entrails]] x4, [[Animal Fat]] x1-3, [[Raw Walrus]] x12, [[Bone Material]] x8, [[Walrus Tusk]] x0-2 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] " ''Added Walrii. These feisty badboys chillax on beaches. They are agressive, swim, and should quite generally be approached with a fair bit of prejudice.'' " ~ Jorb Large, fat and tough creatures resting on beaches. Walrii are aggressive, and will likely engage in combat with nearby players. Walrus are slow on land and can be easily outrun on a horse, however, in water they require a [[Snekkja]] or [[Knarr]], as they are quicker than a rowboat and low quality ships, and, in rare occasions, faster than a Snekkja. Walrus are able to inflict [[Seal Finger|Seal Finger]] [[wounds]] with their attacks, additionally these can also be obtained from butchering their corpses. The chance for wound from butchering is reduced if wearing [[Poor Man's Gloves]] or [[Plate Gauntlets]]. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Aggresive, initiating attack with a walrus will also get the attention of other nearby walrus. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:3  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:'Rus Tusk}} {{:Chomp}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Shredding Paw}} {{:Dark Heart Move}} | {{:Unstoppable}} {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Tail Splash}} |} ===Strategy=== Do not attempt to fight more than a single Walrus at once, if fighting a group, try to line them up and attack only one at once . If fighting in water, be incredibly aware that they use a restoration (See Combat Moves > Tail Splash) that can deal damage in a small area around the Walrus, so the above tactic is a must-do or else you will be hit real hard. Preferably fight them on a Horse, as they are relatively slow outside water. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh {{PAGENAME}} Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Walrus]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Walrus Tusk]] and [[Bone Material]]. ==Notes== *To get to you, walrii will destroy any objects obstructing their path with lower than Soak 25, meaning only [[Palisade]]s and [[Brickwall]]s will stop their bloodlust. {{Game Development}}

Water.rawWikitext:
:''This page is about the collectable resource. For the terrain, see [[Water Terrain]]'' {{infobox metaobj | skillreq = none | objectsreq = Any Container | producedby = [[requires::Well]], [[terrain|Deep Water]], [[terrain|Shallow Water]] | specific = Water }} [[Category:Liquids]] [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] Water is a resource that requires a container that can hold liquid and is used for many forms of crafting. Most water is quality 10. Occasionally parts of the map will have higher quality Water [[Nodes|nodes]]. Because if this, high quality water is fairly valued and may be traded as commodity. Water is one factor in obtaining high quality [[Tree|trees]]. For more information on finding high quality water, see [[Finding high quality water, clay, and soil]]. Water can also be used to regain stamina. ==Drinking== *Quality 10 water recovers 10% stamina and drains 20% energy per 0.05L sip. Higher quality water will decrease the energy drain but stamina recovery remains the same at all qualities. :*This makes high quality water useful for performing stamina draining tasks without having to eat as much to recover, thus preserving your hunger bonus. Though energy is always shown as a whole number, it is not simply rounded up or down. For example, Q11 water will alternate between draining 20% and 19% energy at regular intervals. Other drinkable liquids follow the same rules and formula, making them decent substitutes for water if you have a surplus since it is much easier to increase their quality. *Energy Drain = (\frac{1}{\sqrt{quality/10}} + 1) * 10 :*According to that formula initial quality growth is more effective, and it always higher than 10% per 0.05 sip. Naturally stamina regenerate 10% per 10% energy. ==How to Acquire== Water can be acquired from a [[Well]] or from any [[Terrain|Water]] tile (left click on container and then right click on water). Note: To get the water discovery for a character, one needs to get some water directly from a fresh water source. Take water from a well, lake, river, or distill [[Salt Water]] in a [[Still]]. Taking water stored in other containers will not give the water discovery. ==How to Use== All containers can be used to collect, move, drink, and store water. Generally, a [[Kuksa]], [[Waterskin]], or [[Waterflask]] is used to carry water on your character to regain stamina while travelling.
If large quantities of water are needed, carrying a [[Bucket]] to drink from is also an option. For a list of containers which can hold liquid, visit the [[liquids]] page. ==Quality== Water quality from open-water (''River, lake, Ocean, etc'') is hard-capped by survival. But only down to a minimum quality of 10. (''unlike water drawn from a [[wellspring]], which don't enforces such minimum. Or a [[well]], which has no active soft- or hard-capping''.) {{Game Development}}

Wheat Flour.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Seeds of Wheat]] x5 | producedby = [[requires::Quern]] or [[requires::Milling Machine]] | specific = Any Flour }} [[Category:Powders]] [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] {{GM|$Craft|$Processing & Materials|$Cooking & Ingredients|$Grind Flour}} Wheat Flour, made from [[Seeds of Wheat]], is an ingredient in all [[:Category:Baked Goods]]. '''Warning: Dropping flour will cause it to disappear.''' ==How to Acquire== With Wheat Seeds in your inventory, right-click on the [[Quern]] you wish to use, wait until your character bumps up against it, and use the craft command to begin grinding flour. Alternatively, you can right click a [[Milling Machine]] while holding wheat seeds to produce flour.
'''Note:''' Though the crafting windows says otherwise, you don't need 5 seeds to grind flour. You can grind anything between 1 and 5 seeds at once. Each seed will produce 0.10 kg of flour when ground. It's advised to use buckets when making large quantities of flour. ==Quality== Only the [[Seeds of Wheat]] determine the quality of the flour, the [[Quern]] has no effect. [[Skills#Farming|Farming]] and [[Dexterity]] acts as a [[softcap]] and will lower the quality of your Flour when using seeds that are higher quality than your softcap. Using a milling machine instead of a quern removes the softcap.

Wild Horse.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(8/11)-(-8/11)round6}} | | baseq = 30 | | hp = ~320 | fhp = 200 | | loot = [[Fresh Wildhorse Hide]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x2, [[Raw Wildhorse]] x6, [[Bone Material]] x5 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Wildhorses wander about in most grassland biomes. They can be fed a [[Clover]] and then ridden. Right click a clover on a wildhorse, wait for the wildhorse to take the clover. Right click on the wildhorse and select Giddyup! '''Be careful and watch Pony Power'''. If it reaches 0, the horse will throw you off and deal exactly half your MHP in SHP damage. To circumvent this, you can use the Horse Whisper [[Hearth Magic]] to augment your pony power, or simply dismount.
Note: If really close to the horse when feeding the clover. It will take it immediately. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you aggro them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:3  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Mule Kick}} {{:Bucking}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Thunder Over}} {{:Sudden Charge}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Rampant Rage}} {{:Swift Evasion}} |} ===Strategy=== Horses are tough, but have less Agility than you might expect. They'll spam Mule Kick and Bear Down often, and occasionally use Sudden Charge. Swift Evasion can ruin a good Cleave or Full Circle setup. You'll need a decent melee weapon like a [[Tinker's Throwing Axe|Tinker Axe]] to deal sufficient damage. Once you hit about 200 damage a horse will flee at the fastest speed of any animal in the game. You'll need to be on another horse and chase at your highest speed, cleave for 150+ damage at ~180 health, or simply follow the blood path and wait for the horse to bleed out. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Wildhorse Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Wildhorse]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
==Notes== *Attempting to pick up any small animal while riding the horse will result in the animal's death. Young hearthlings can take advantage of this fact to easily hunt [[Hedgehog]]s, [[Rabbit]]s, [[Squirrel]]s, and [[Mole]]s. This bypasses the [[Survival]] skill cap on the animal (''upon picking it up(?)''). {{Game Development}}
