Wild Windsown Weed.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Foraging]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Drying Frame]] or [[requires::Herbalist Table]]
| producedby = Hand
| terrain    = [[terrain::Forest]], [[terrain::Grassland]], [[terrain::Swamp]]
|
| perexp     = 80
}}

{{PAGENAME}}, when dried, converts into a seed of some kind that you can then plant. They have the quality of a random crop in the world, hardcapped by the [[Farming]] score of the Hearthling who picks it up.

World 15 has reworked acquiring crops from the wild and functions as a catch-up mechanic. Wild Windsown Weeds will generally reflect the quality of crops grown in the world, making them a good way to get better crops as you raise your farming score.

==How to Acquire==
Wild Windsown Weeds begin to appear when you have a [[Perception]] * [[Exploration]] score of 80. They can be found on any forest, grassland or swamp type terrain.

==Quality==
The quality of Wild Windsown Weeds is determined by its own unique nodes and it is [[Hardcap|hardcapped]] by [[Farming]]. Some seed qualities are determined by real crops growing somewhere in the world. However, max quality is determined by highest quality in the world for that crop type.

==How to Use==
The Wild Windsown Weed must be dried on either an [[Herbalist Table]] or a [[Drying Frame]] to get crop seeds. The drying time on a drying frame is 2 in-game days (up to 16 hours depending on drying frame quality), and the drying time on an herbalist table is 8 in-game hours or about 2.6 hours of real time.

Seed quality is '''not''' hardcapped by Herbalist Tables, making them the quicker way to dry WWWs once you have access.

Wine.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| skillreq   = [[requires::Winemaking]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Grapejuice]]
| producedby = [[requires::Demijohn]]
}}

[[Category:Alcohol]]
[[Category:Liquids]]
[[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]]

{{PAGENAME}} is an alcoholic drink acquired by processing [[Grapejuice|Grape Juice]].
{{PAGENAME}} buffs the effects of [[Food Satiations|Vegetables]] and [[Food Satiations|Meat]] by 0.5%, [[Food Satiations|Cheese]] by 1.0%  per gulp at [[Quality|Q10]]. Each drink will also add a negative satiation against itself, thus granting diminishing return on the buff.

==How to Acquire==

Build a [[Demijohn]] and fill it with [[Grapejuice]]. Juice turns to {{PAGENAME}} after one week. In order to halt the fermented {{PAGENAME}}'s further progression into [[Vinegar]], one must store it in a barrel or a [[Wine Bottle]].

==How to Use==

The proper way to drink {{PAGENAME}} is from the [[Wine Glass]], not drinking out of a proper vessel halves the effective quality of your {{PAGENAME}}.

==Drinking==

*Quality 10 {{PAGENAME}} recovers 10% stamina and drains 20% energy per 0.05L sip. Higher quality {{PAGENAME}} will decrease the energy drain but stamina recovery remains the same at all qualities. 
:*This makes high quality {{PAGENAME}} useful for performing stamina draining tasks without having to eat as much to recover, thus preserving your hunger bonus. Though energy is always shown as a whole number, it is not simply rounded up or down. For example, Q11 {{PAGENAME}} will alternate between draining 20% and 19% energy at regular intervals. Other drinkable liquids follow the same rules and formula.
*Energy Drain = <math>(\frac{1}{\sqrt{quality/10}} + 1) * 10</math>
:*According to that formula initial quality growth is more effective, and it always higher than 10% per 0.05 sip. Naturally stamina regenerate 10% per 10% energy.

==Quality==

The quality of the {{PAGENAME}} depends solely on the quality of the grapejuice.

==Quick Facts==

* Alcohol does not reduce travel weariness like it did in Legacy.
* It is possible to enter Knocked Out state by overusing alcohol.

{{Game Development}}

Wolf.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(10/11)-(-7/11)round6}} | | deadly = yes | | baseq = 50 | | armor = 50 | hp = 500 | fhp = 400 | | loot = [[Fresh Wolf Hide]] x2, [[Intestines]] x4, [[Entrails]] x3, [[Raw Wolf]] x10, [[Bone Material]] x6, [[Wolf's Claw]] x2-8 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Wolves are an uncommon but loathed sight in the Hearthlands. They are nightmarishly aggressive. They can be found in mountains or occasionally in forests if the moon is full. They travel in packs of three to eight, attacking and killing everything that moves. If one attacks you, or you attack one, they all attack at the same time. Wolves are extremely dangerous because not only do they have a quite high chance to [[execute]] you should they KO you, this chance to maul you to death is rolled for each and every wolf, meaning you have an extremely tiny chance to not have at least 1 decide to kill you. If a pack knocks you out, it's all over. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Wolves are extremely aggressive, and will likely engage in combat with nearby players and animals. Aggroing any one wolf will cause the entire pack to also engage. While they aren't incredibly strong on their own, there is no way to split them, meaning you must always fight all at once. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! {{GM|$Craft|$Clothes & Equipment|$Tools|Wooden Sho$vel}}

A {{PAGENAME}} is a tool used to perform various actions. it can be used to dig for [[Clay]], [[Soil]], [[Sand]] and [[Snow]] for 15% per tick, to quickly remove [[Stump|Tree Stump]]s, to dig up [[Bush]]es, and to plant [[Seed of Tree or Bush|Tree Seeds]]

While using [[Survey|Land Survey]]s, a {{PAGENAME}} digs a base speed of 2 units of soil per action, modified by quality.

==How to Acquire==

Gather the proper materials in your inventory and craft by hand

==How to Use==

*To dig for Clay, Soil, Sand, and Snow: Use the Dig action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|$Dig|...}}
*To remove a stump: Use the Destroy action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|Destro$y|...}}
*To dig up a bush: Use the Lift action with your shovel equipped {{GM|$Adventure|$Lift|...}}

To plant a Tree seed:

#Pick up a tree seed from your inventory and use it on the ground (left click on the seed, right click on the ground)
#With your shovel equipped, select the flower menu option available to plant your seed
''See [[Tree]] for more details on tree farming''

[[Category:Forageable Plants]]
[[Category:Foraged Foods]]
[[Category:Mushrooms]]

A type of edible mushroom. Yellowfeet can be eaten by right-clicking on them and selecting the "Eat" option. This [[:Category:Foods|food]] restores 125% of the [[energy]] bar, gives 1 [[Intelligence]] and 1 [[Strength]] in [[FEP]]s.

== How to Acquire ==

Yellowfeet can be found in [[Forest Terrain|forests]]. If you happen across one, and have the foraging skill, you can harvest it. There is no known way to purposely produce Yellowfeet, so they can only be found by chance and by increasing exploration and perception skills. You need at least 200 [[Perception]] * [[Exploration]] score to spot Yellowfeet.

Adder.title: Adder

sections.Behaviour: They are highly aggressive critters. They'll attack unprovoked.
sections.Strategy:
Adders can enter [[Water Terrain|Shallow Water]] freely. ''(They used to be unable to do so. Out-of-date guides may tell you to simply flee into the water.)''

Unlike other small creatures, adders cannot be trampled by [[Horse]]s. They can be trampled by [[Cart]]s, however, so drag a Cart with or without a Horse to kill them easily.

There's no real reason to try to fight them, just use the method above. If you for some reason want to or got attacked by one at an inopportune time, use a lot of yellow Sweeping defenses, as their yellow attack Serpent's Strike is what applies the venom. Keep your yellow opening low at all times or suffer the consequences.
sections.Venom:
[[Adder Bite]] wounds, increases in severity over time and come with a hefty -15 STR per wound. Eventually, the wound will start healing and dissipate. There is a chance that [[Nerve Damage]] occurs with adder bites, and it is speculated that it's either from the frequency or amount of adder bites a character has.<sup>[//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?f=39&t=57510&start=50#p739374]</sup>

[[Snake Juice]] is an effective treatment.

=Notes=
*You can use a wild or tame horse to pull a cart around mountains and rack up many adders easily. There's still risk of drawing the ire of other mountain denizens, or simply having an unfortunate mishap with the adders themselves. You may want to use an "alt" character to collect snakes. Cart method does Not apply Survival hardcap.
*1x2 [[Fresh Adder Hide]]s yields only 1x1 [[Adder Hide]]s, making it less efficient as generic hide. However they have specific use in [[Adderfang Amulet]] and [[Snakeskin Belt]].
*Adders go after small animals like rabbit or squirrel. Which can be used to get them off your tail. (''Saw a snake getting killed by a Wood Grouse Cock, but don't know who started the fight'')
*As of update Whitemetal Rain you can now be a Snake Eater and walk through the rain and feast on a tree frog.

{{Game Development|1}}

[[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]]

[[Adder|Adders]] are snakes, occasionally seen in certain [[Forest|Forests]] and common in [[Mountain|Mountains]]. Their fangs carry potent venom, so keep a wide berth. Fighting them like other animals is almost pointless. (See Strategy section below.) Their fangs and skins can be made into useful equipment like a [[Snakeskin Belt|belt]], [[Snakeskin Boots|boots]], [[Snakeskin Stripes|lacing]], an [[Adderfang Amulet|amulet]], and their fangs can make an [[Snake Juice|antivenom]] if you do end up getting nommed on.

=Combat=

==Behaviour==
They are highly aggressive critters. They'll attack unprovoked. 

===Combat Moves===
{| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"
! '''Attacks'''
! '''Restorations'''
|-
|
{{:Serpent's Strike}}{{:Chomp}}
|
{{:Careful Approach}}
|}

==Strategy==
Adders can enter [[Water Terrain|Shallow Water]] freely. ''(They used to be unable to do so. Out-of-date guides may tell you to simply flee into the water.)''

Unlike other small creatures, adders cannot be trampled by [[Horse]]s. They can be trampled by [[Cart]]s, however, so drag a Cart with or without a Horse to kill them easily.

There's no real reason to try to fight them, just use the method above. If you for some reason want to or got attacked by one at an inopportune time, use a lot of yellow Sweeping defenses, as their yellow attack Serpent's Strike is what applies the venom. Keep your yellow opening low at all times or suffer the consequences.

==Venom==
[[Adder Bite]] wounds, increases in severity over time and come with a hefty -15 STR per wound. Eventually, the wound will start healing and dissipate. There is a chance that [[Nerve Damage]] occurs with adder bites, and it is speculated that it's either from the frequency or amount of adder bites a character has.<sup>[//www.havenandhearth.com/forum/viewtopic.php?f=39&t=57510&start=50#p739374]</sup>

[[Snake Juice]] is an effective treatment.

=Notes=
*You can use a wild or tame horse to pull a cart around mountains and rack up many adders easily. There's still risk of drawing the ire of other mountain denizens, or simply having an unfortunate mishap with the adders themselves. You may want to use an "alt" character to collect snakes. Cart method does Not apply Survival hardcap.
*1x2 [[Fresh Adder Hide]]s yields only 1x1 [[Adder Hide]]s, making it less efficient as generic hide. However they have specific use in [[Adderfang Amulet]] and [[Snakeskin Belt]].
*Adders go after small animals like rabbit or squirrel. Which can be used to get them off your tail. (''Saw a snake getting killed by a Wood Grouse Cock, but don't know who started the fight'')
*As of update Whitemetal Rain you can now be a Snake Eater and walk through the rain and feast on a tree frog.

{{Game Development|1}}

Bat.title: Bat

sections.Behaviour: They'll attack you if you get get close, and fellow bats in their gang (within a tile or two) will join in. If you're the one to attack only one will fight back. They don't fight with gang members, so you if equip a [[Bat Wing|bat cape]] they'll ignore you unless you attack them.
sections.Strategy:
Bats can be outrun on Sprint speed so if you anger some just chug a bit and make a dash for a cave exit.

<s>Bat can be killed in one shot from [[Hunter's Bow]] with 10 [[Marksmanship]] skill.
Find a single bat, target it from ~15 tiles range and approach slowly using Ctrl+LClick.
Shoot at an appropriate distance, around 4 tiles.</s> - ''No longer true as of the 2020 archery changes.''

It is possible to comfortably kill a bat with 20 [[Unarmed Combat]] and 20 [[Agility]] without taking little damage. Just spam Punch or something similar. Once you have a decent weapon like a Tinker Axe it'll be better to use Quick Barrage and Full Circle or Uppercut and Sideswipe.

They generally just spam a basic green attack and occasionally use Wingbeat which can batter you up a bit if your openings are high and they can bite your neck if your yellow opening gets high which leeches HP from you onto them. They can only use Bristle which requires IP so their defense is lacking and they're easily killed with brute force.
sections.Notes:
*Bats '''will''' aggro even at crawl speed, although the aggro range is much smaller.
*Bats will not aggro you while wearing a [[Bat Wing]], making it very useful in navigating caves.
*Cleaned Bat can be [[Roasting Spit|spit-roasted]] for [[Intelligence]], [[Perception]] and [[Agility]] [[FEP]]'s.

*You can prevent bat spawning by paving the cave tiles.
*The quality of a dead bat is [[hardcap]]ped by [[Survival]] of the Hearthling who killed it.
sections.In-Game Example(s):

{{Game Development}}

Bat.rawWikitext: {{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(1/11)-(-1/11)round6}} | | baseq = 15 | | hp = 90 | fhp = 60 | | loot = [[Dead Bat]]: [[Fresh Bat Hide]], [[Entrails]], [[Tiny Brain]], [[Raw Bat]], [[Bone Material]], [[Bat Wings]] }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Bats are aggressive flying creatures. They can fly over water and their speed is greater than Hearthling's running. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== They'll attack you if you get get close, and fellow bats in their gang (within a tile or two) will join in. If you're the one to attack only one will fight back. They don't fight with gang members, so you if equip a [[Bat Wing|bat cape]] they'll ignore you unless you attack them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:3 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Wingbeat}} {{:Vampirism}} | {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Bats can be outrun on Sprint speed so if you anger some just chug a bit and make a dash for a cave exit. Bat can be killed in one shot from [[Hunter's Bow]] with 10 [[Marksmanship]] skill. Find a single bat, target it from ~15 tiles range and approach slowly using Ctrl+LClick. Shoot at an appropriate distance, around 4 tiles. - ''No longer true as of the 2020 archery changes.'' It is possible to comfortably kill a bat with 20 [[Unarmed Combat]] and 20 [[Agility]] without taking little damage. Just spam Punch or something similar. Once you have a decent weapon like a Tinker Axe it'll be better to use Quick Barrage and Full Circle or Uppercut and Sideswipe. They generally just spam a basic green attack and occasionally use Wingbeat which can batter you up a bit if your openings are high and they can bite your neck if your yellow opening gets high which leeches HP from you onto them. They can only use Bristle which requires IP so their defense is lacking and they're easily killed with brute force. ==Notes== *Bats '''will''' aggro even at crawl speed, although the aggro range is much smaller. *Bats will not aggro you while wearing a [[Bat Wing]], making it very useful in navigating caves. *Cleaned Bat can be [[Roasting Spit|spit-roasted]] for [[Intelligence]], [[Perception]] and [[Agility]] [[FEP]]'s. *You can prevent bat spawning by paving the cave tiles. *The quality of a dead bat is [[hardcap]]ped by [[Survival]] of the Hearthling who killed it. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:Sleepbat.png]] {{Game Development}}

Bog Turtle.title: Bog Turtle

sections.How To Acquire:
Turtles can be found commonly on [[Swamp]], [[Bog]] or [[Fen]]. They do not engage in combat and do not flee. You will need [[Hunting]] to pick up a turtle or killing them through trampling with a horse.

Also can be obtained instead of boulders while digging a [[Cellar]] at 9 lvl of caves.
sections.Notes:
*Spitroasting turtle still gives [[Bog Turtle Shell]].
*Bog Turtle Shell is one of the most accessible sources of [[Finebone]] to a fresh character.
*Most likely represents 'European pond turtle' (Emys orbicularis).

Bog Turtle is a swamp creature that can be butchered for [[Bog Turtle Shell]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Bog Turtle]] meat. It takes up a 2x1 space in the inventory. 


==How To Acquire==

Turtles can be found commonly on [[Swamp]], [[Bog]] or [[Fen]]. They do not engage in combat and do not flee. You will need [[Hunting]] to pick up a turtle or killing them through trampling with a horse.

Also can be obtained instead of boulders while digging a [[Cellar]] at 9 lvl of caves.

==Notes==

*Spitroasting turtle still gives [[Bog Turtle Shell]].
*Bog Turtle Shell is one of the most accessible sources of [[Finebone]] to a fresh character.

[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be plucked for [[{{PAGENAME}} Feather]].

Garefowl.rawWikitext:
{{infobox_creature | xitem = unknown | yitem = ... | xobst = unknown | yobst = ... | | baseq = 25 | | hp = 85 | fhp = 40 | | loot = [[Garefowl Feather]] x2-3, [[Entrails]], [[Raw Garefowl]] x1-3, [[Bone Material]] x2, [[Wishbone]] }} Garefowl, also known as Great Auks, are an extinct species of flightless seabird visually similar to modern-day penguins, though not closely related. Spawns in large flocks. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you attack them. Spawn in large flocks, but aggroes individually. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:2 ! Restorations:1  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Fell Scratch}} {{:Feather Flurry}} | {{:Bristle}} |} ===Strategy=== Roughly the same as a Fox. With 15-20 combat stats spamming punch or sideswipe works fine. You'll need to be on a horse to catch it when it flees (they are faster inside water), unless you deal a high enough damage move to kill it before it begins to. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be plucked for [[{{PAGENAME}} Feather]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Plucked {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for, [[Entrails]] and [[Raw {{PAGENAME}}]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]] and [[Wishbone]].
==Trivia== The Garefowl, also known as the Great Auk, was the original penguin. When what we now call penguins were discovered, they were mistaken for Garefowl and called garefowl/penguins, despite being an entirely different species that just so happened to be very similar. Due to a volcanic event that took place near a garefowl breeding ground killing many, and the fact they were an endangered species already, the species was left with only a few dozen survivors on an islet named Eldey, where they were killed for their skin and bones to preserve in museums. The last known garefowl was killed protecting its egg by a group of men employed by a merchant seeking its hide. With no garefowl left in the world, the modern penguin stole its name permanently. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great%20auk Wikipedia article]. {{Game Development}}

Mole.title: Mole

sections.Hunting:
Capturing a mole is relatively easy:
1. Get close to the mole.
2. Start running.
3. It may try to escape by burrowing underground, but it won't go far.
3.1 '''Note''' that mole can't burrow on any non-grassland biome.
4. Click it again.
5. Repeat 4 or 5 times and the mole will stop burrowing, and you can chase it down.
(''W15 note: On [[Cloud Range]] terrain some mole burrowed away at least ten times.)

Squirrel.title: Squirrel

sections.How to Acquire:
Squirrels can be found in both deciduous and pine forests.

To hunt squirrels one needs the [[Hunting|hunting skill]].

To catch a squirrel, right-click on it to pick it up from the ground. If you have space for a 1x2 item the squirrel will appear in your inventory.

Speed at which squirrels run away seems to vary. You should be able to catch most squirrels with run speed (fastest you can use on forest terrain) as long as you have enough stamina.
sections.Notes:
* Unlike [[Chicken|chickens]], squirrel will stay alive in any container for an indefinite amount of time, as they don't need food.
* Butchering products are hard-capped by Survival (''and/or tool - {{verify||Butchering hard-cap}}'')

Roughly the same as a [[Badger]]. With 25-30 combat stats spamming punch or sideswipe works fine. You'll need to be on a horse to catch it when it flees, unless you deal a high enough damage move to kill it before it begins to. Cleaving for 90 at ~75 HP should do.

Wildhorse.title: Wild Horse

sections.Behaviour: Passive until you aggro them.
sections.Strategy: Horses are tough, but have less Agility than you might expect. They'll spam Mule Kick and Bear Down often, and occasionally use Sudden Charge. Swift Evasion can ruin a good Cleave or Full Circle setup. You'll need a decent melee weapon like a [[Tinker's Throwing Axe|Tinker Axe]] to deal sufficient damage. Once you hit about 200 damage a horse will flee at the fastest speed of any animal in the game. You'll need to be on another horse and chase at your highest speed, cleave for 150+ damage at ~180 health, or simply follow the blood path and wait for the horse to bleed out.
sections.In-Game Example(s):
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Wildhorse Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Wildhorse]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
sections.Notes:
*Attempting to pick up any small animal while riding the horse will result in the animal's death. Young hearthlings can take advantage of this fact to easily hunt [[Hedgehog]]s, [[Rabbit]]s, [[Squirrel]]s, and [[Mole]]s. This bypasses the [[Survival]] skill cap on the animal (''upon picking it up(?)'').

{{Game Development}}

Wildhorse.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | xobst = {{#expr:(4/11)-(-4/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(8/11)-(-8/11)round6}} | | baseq = 30 | | hp = ~320 | fhp = 200 | | loot = [[Fresh Wildhorse Hide]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x2, [[Small Brain]], [[Raw Wildhorse]] x6, [[Bone Material]] x5 }} [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] Wildhorses wander about in most grassland biomes. They can be fed a [[Clover]] and then ridden. Right click a clover on a wildhorse, wait for the wildhorse to take the clover. Right click on the wildhorse and select Giddyup! '''Be careful and watch Pony Power'''. If it reaches 0, the horse will throw you off and deal exactly half your MHP in SHP damage. To circumvent this, you can use the Horse Whisper [[Hearth Magic]] to augment your pony power, or simply dismount.
Note: If really close to the horse when feeding the clover. It will take it immediately. ==Combat== ===Behaviour=== Passive until you aggro them. ====Combat Moves==== {| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Attacks:5 ! Restorations:3  |- style="vertical-align: top;" | {{:Mule Kick}} {{:Bucking}} {{:Bear Down}} {{:Thunder Over}} {{:Sudden Charge}} | {{:Careful Approach}} {{:Rampant Rage}} {{:Swift Evasion}} |} ===Strategy=== Horses are tough, but have less Agility than you might expect. They'll spam Mule Kick and Bear Down often, and occasionally use Sudden Charge. Swift Evasion can ruin a good Cleave or Full Circle setup. You'll need a decent melee weapon like a [[Tinker's Throwing Axe|Tinker Axe]] to deal sufficient damage. Once you hit about 200 damage a horse will flee at the fastest speed of any animal in the game. You'll need to be on another horse and chase at your highest speed, cleave for 150+ damage at ~180 health, or simply follow the blood path and wait for the horse to bleed out. ==In-Game Example(s)== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Wildhorse Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Wildhorse]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].

Young hearthlings can take advantage of this fact to easily hunt [[Hedgehog]]s, [[Rabbit]]s, [[Squirrel]]s, and [[Mole]]s. Cattle.title: Cattle

sections.Animals:
*'''Bull''' is a male cattle that can impregnate(indicated through a mating animation) female cattle (cow), pull [[Wagon]]s(slower than [[Horse]]) and equipped with [[Packrack]] to carry things.
*'''Cow''' is a female cattle. After being impregnated, they give birth to a calf(can have twins on rare occasions) in 4.5 days then start to produce milk. Also can be equipped with [[Packrack]] to carry things.
*'''Calf''' is an adolescent cattle. They will grow either into a bull or cow (indicated in the top left side of their respective information windows) in 10 days. In addition to regular products, calves give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered.
sections.Stats & Quality:
*The quality of an animal or any of stat is the one of parent quality\stat, +20 -> -5, and then [[softcap|softcapped]] by the breeding quality of the male involved and quality of eaten [[Swill]].
**See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information.
sections.Milk:
*Cows produce [[Cow's Milk]] at the rate of 0.01 every 10 minutes in RL at Milk Quantity 10, speed modified by Milk Quantity stat and can store a maximum of 25 L. 
*Milk quality equal to cow's quality and modified by Milk Quality stat.
sections.Meat:
*Adult Cattle produce 10 [[Raw Beef]] at Meat Quantity 10, modified by Meat Quantity stat. 
*Meat quality equal to cattle's quality and modified by Meat Quality stat.
sections.Hide: *Cattle produce 1 [[Cow Hide]] and quality equal to cattle's quality, modified by Hide Quality stat.
sections.Tips & Tricks: *It is a good idea to seperate your bulls from your cows, to ensure that you can select the highest quality bull to mate with your cows when you want calves. You can additionally castrate your lower quality bulls to stop them from breeding and to acquire [[Bollock]].
sections.Notes: Herder credo (possible other parts of the game as well) refers to "Cattle" as "ochs." If you have quest to slaughter or flay an "ochs" you can use any cattle to complete them regardless of age or sex. (Modern English word "ox" refers exclusively to male cattle and usually implies castrated draft animal.

Cattle.rawWikitext:
{{infobox creature | caption = Calf » Cow » Bull | | xobst = {{#expr:(3/11)-(-3/11)round6}} | yobst = {{#expr:(9/11)-(-9/11)round6}} | | terrain = (''None, Domestic Areas'') | | hp = (unknown) | | loot = [[Cow's Milk]], [[Raw Beef]] x10, [[Animal Fat]], [[Intestines]] x2, [[Entrails]] x3, [[Tiny Brain]], [[Fresh Cow Hide]], [[Bone Material]] x6, [[Suckling's Maw]] from calfs. }} [[Category:Domesticated]] [[Category:Tmp_xyobj(neg)]] [[File:breeding_cows.png|thumb|A breeding pair of cows]] {{PAGENAME}} are tamed [[Aurochs]]en. See [[Taming]] for more information about taming. When it comes to quantity, Cows are the best possible source of [[Cow's Milk|milk]], far outproducing [[Goat]]s and [[Sheep]] on a per-animal basis, and they produce a significant amount of [[Raw Beef|meat]] and [[Fresh Cow Hide|hide]]. Additionally, adult Cattle can be equipped with [[Packrack]]s for portable storage, and Bulls can be used to pull [[Wagon]]s. ==Animals== *'''Bull''' is a male cattle that can impregnate(indicated through a mating animation) female cattle (cow), pull [[Wagon]]s(slower than [[Horse]]) and equipped with [[Packrack]] to carry things. *'''Cow''' is a female cattle. After being impregnated, they give birth to a calf(can have twins on rare occasions) in 4.5 days then start to produce milk. Also can be equipped with [[Packrack]] to carry things. *'''Calf''' is an adolescent cattle. They will grow either into a bull or cow (indicated in the top left side of their respective information windows) in 10 days. In addition to regular products, calves give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered. ==Stats & Quality== *The quality of an animal or any of stat is the one of parent quality\stat, +20 -> -5, and then [[softcap|softcapped]] by the breeding quality of the male involved and quality of eaten [[Swill]]. **See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information. ===Milk=== *Cows produce [[Cow's Milk]] at the rate of 0.01 every 10 minutes in RL at Milk Quantity 10, speed modified by Milk Quantity stat and can store a maximum of 25 L. *Milk quality equal to cow's quality and modified by Milk Quality stat. ===Meat=== *Adult Cattle produce 10 [[Raw Beef]] at Meat Quantity 10, modified by Meat Quantity stat. *Meat quality equal to cattle's quality and modified by Meat Quality stat. ===Hide=== *Cattle produce 1 [[Cow Hide]] and quality equal to cattle's quality, modified by Hide Quality stat. ==Tips & Tricks== *It is a good idea to seperate your bulls from your cows, to ensure that you can select the highest quality bull to mate with your cows when you want calves. You can additionally castrate your lower quality bulls to stop them from breeding and to acquire [[Bollock]]. ==Notes== Herder credo (possible other parts of the game as well) refers to "Cattle" as "ochs." If you have quest to slaughter or flay an "ochs" you can use any cattle to complete them regardless of age or sex. (Modern English word "ox" refers exclusively to male cattle and usually implies castrated draft animal. ==Gallery== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead Cow. Can be skinned for [[Fresh Cow Hide]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned Cow. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Animal Fat]] and [[Raw Beef]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] Cow Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
{{Game Development}}

Chasm Conch.title: Chasm Conch

sections.Behaviour:
Passive until you aggro them. When aggroed hides in its conch and starts fighting only after recieving at least 40 000 armor damage. Can regen health slowly while huddled in its shell.

During battle if it can't reach you, starts using [[Trunk Gunk]] - like a [[Mammoth]].
sections.Strategy: Stronger than a bear, but very slow - about 2nd walking speed. Has a lot of armor (200/400), high piercing weapons (like [[Boar Spear]] or unarmed combat) are more effective than usual. It's a good idea to stack a lot IP and deal a lot of openings before it starts to fight back.
sections.In-Game Example(s):
[[File:Chasm Conch Alive.png|150px]] Alive Chasm Conch.
[[File:Chasm Conch Dead.png|150px]] Dead Chasm Conch. Can be butсhered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]], [[Chasm Conch Meat]], [[Horrid Love Dart]] and [[Grotesque Tumor]].
[[File:Chasm Conch Butchered.png|150px]] Butсhered Chasm Conch. Can be chipped for [[Shard of Conch]].
sections.Notes:
*Dead Chasm Conch can't be lifted.
*After butchering, conch can be chipped like usual boulder.
sections.Unconfirmed:
*Despawns when map is unloaded, like all creatures (with the exception of [[Troll]]s.)
*Can spawn at second cave layer (L2).
*Possibly more common the deeper you go.

Fishing Lure.title: Fishing

sections.In-Game Text:
...
There are two types of fishing: Rod and pole. Pole fishing uses various forms of live or fleshy bait -- such as earthworms, entrails, or ants -- whereas Rod fishing uses lures. Fishing poles catch fish immediately whenever they bite, but the more demanding Rod fishing rather requires you to actually land the Fish by figuring out the many riddles of the sport. The truly rare fish of the world are caught with rods.
sections.How to Fish:
To fish you need four categories of items combined together with the fishing skill. These items are:
#  A Fishing Pole
#  Fishing Line
#  Fish Hook
#  Lures or Bait
sections.Fishing Pole:
Craft Either a [[Bushcraft Fishingpole]] or a [[Primitive Casting-Rod]].

*[[Primitive Casting-Rod]] must be used with Lures. Lures are not consumed as fish are caught. This is called Lure-Fishing

*[[Bushcraft Fishingpole]] uses bait. Fish are caught automatically and the Bait is Consumed and replaced per fish. This is called Bait-Fishing
sections.Fishing Line:
As of {{Patch|Fishing for Finery}} update. [[String]]s, as fishing lines, where replaced with dedicated crafted [[Fishline|fishing lines]].

{{#ask:[[Specific::Fishline]]|format=ul}}
(''No specific per-fishline details known'')

Fishing line occasionally break, so be sure to have backups. Higher quality fishing line tends to break less often.

The color of the fishing line is reflective of the type of line.
sections.Fish Hook:
A fish hook is added to the fishing pole by left clicking on the hook and then right clicking on the fishing pole. Fish hook types include:
*[[Bone Hook]]
*[[Chitin Hook]]
*[[Gold Hook]]
*[[Metal Hook]]

Fish hook are also occasionally lost. Using your prized gold nugget passed down by your ancestors might not be wise. Higher quality fish hooks tend to be lost less often.
sections.Bait:
Bait is expendable meaning you can catch at maximum one fish per bait. 

Some types of bait include: {{#ask: [[Category:bait]]}}
sections.Lure:
Lures are durable, but they can be lost. {{SharedText|unlock_lures}}

Some types of lure include: {{#ask: [[Category:Fishing_Lure]]}}
sections.Fishing Trash: When something is dropped in a body of water, it is added to a global pool of items that can be fished up anywhere in the world or found buried on the beach. Some people use this feature for charity by throwing their old equipment in the river. You can also drop a written parchment to send a message to a random fisherman as if it were a message in a bottle.
sections.Fishing:
'''Quoting Jorb:'''
Fish are now a limited and localized resource, working in the same way as any other localized resource. You should notice fish jumping in the water if the area has fish. What you catch when fishing depends on the following factors: Time of day, [[Water Terrain|location]], [[Fishing Lure|lure]], [[Fish Hook|hook]], [[Fishing Pole|pole]] and [[Fishing Line|line]] type as well as the quality level of each piece of fishing equipment.
sections.Bait Fishing: Bait-Fishing is a semi-automated and a Semi-random activity using a Bushcraft Fishing Pole. Bait, line, and hook combo seem to have no strong correlation between fish types caught. Equip your pole, gather an inventory of bait, and begin fishing. You will automatically catch, recast, and re-bait your hook while bait fishing. The type of fish caught are random, based on the node you are fishing from, however the quality is hard-capped by your [[Survival]]. If you are simply looking to catch fish with no real desire for a certain type of fish, this is your easiest option.
sections.Lure Fishing:
Lure-Fishing using a Primitive Casting Rod is a more precise and manual activity. Lure, line, and hook type all play role in what species you will be able to catch. Every fish have certain line, hook, and lure combos that work best for it. If you are having difficulties catching a fish or keeping your lure, be sure to try new combinations. Equip your pole, gather some tackle, and begin fishing. You will be presented a list of fish to "aim" for, with a given % of how likely they are to bite. This list is a function of your [[Survival]] and [[Will]], so the higher both stats are, the larger your list will be. Max number of fishes in a list is limited by 10 and apparently is equal to <math>\lceil \sqrt{Will * Surv}/20 \rceil</math>. You can choose what kind of fish you want to catch. If you do not pick any, the game will automatically choose the fish with the highest % of biting.

If you can't find the fish you are looking for, move away from the current location to find another fish node. Note that there the chance to catch a specific type of fish within the node has an optimal location where it is maximized. Try different spots nearby and go in the direction where the chance increases until you find the best spot (works similar to maximum quality within forageable nodes, but for the catch chance). While the list normally shows only three types of fish, you may be able to catch more types of fish in the spot using the same lure, line and hook combination. For some types of fish (cave fish, for example), you may find the [[Fisher's Request for a Catch]] magic really useful (can boost the chance to bite from about 30% to 80%, for example).

Fishing Lure.rawWikitext:
{{infobox skill | ptext1 = The fathoms give nothing,
Yield not a single thing,
But to the fishermens' snares. | ptext2 = You have mastered some basic fishing techniques, and can craft rods, poles, hooks, and lures. | ptext3 = There are two types of fishing: Rod and pole. Pole fishing uses various forms of live or fleshy bait -- such as earthworms, entrails, or ants -- whereas Rod fishing uses lures. Fishing poles catch fish immediatley whenever they bite, but the more demanding Rod fishing rather requries you to actually land the Fish by figuring out the many riddles of the sport. The truly rare fish of the world are caught with rods. | | skill_cost = 200 | skills_required = Foraging }} The fishing skill allows you to craft and use [[Bushcraft Fishingpole]]s and [[Primitive Casting-Rod]]s and to fish for different [[:Category:Fish|types of fish]]. The fishing skill benefits from completion of the [[Credos]] - Fisherman. ==In-Game Text== ... There are two types of fishing: Rod and pole. Pole fishing uses various forms of live or fleshy bait -- such as earthworms, entrails, or ants -- whereas Rod fishing uses lures. Fishing poles catch fish immediately whenever they bite, but the more demanding Rod fishing rather requires you to actually land the Fish by figuring out the many riddles of the sport. The truly rare fish of the world are caught with rods. ==How to Fish== To fish you need four categories of items combined together with the fishing skill. These items are: # A Fishing Pole # Fishing Line # Fish Hook # Lures or Bait ===Fishing Pole=== Craft Either a [[Bushcraft Fishingpole]] or a [[Primitive Casting-Rod]]. *[[Primitive Casting-Rod]] must be used with Lures. Lures are not consumed as fish are caught. This is called Lure-Fishing *[[Bushcraft Fishingpole]] uses bait. Fish are caught automatically and the Bait is Consumed and replaced per fish. This is called Bait-Fishing ===Fishing Line=== As of {{Patch|Fishing for Finery}} update. [[String]]s, as fishing lines, where replaced with dedicated crafted [[Fishline|fishing lines]]. {{#ask:[[Specific::Fishline]]|format=ul}} (''No specific per-fishline details known'') Fishing line occasionally break, so be sure to have backups. Higher quality fishing line tends to break less often. The color of the fishing line is reflective of the type of line. ===Fish Hook=== A fish hook is added to the fishing pole by left clicking on the hook and then right clicking on the fishing pole. Fish hook types include: *[[Bone Hook]] *[[Chitin Hook]] *[[Gold Hook]] *[[Metal Hook]] Fish hook are also occasionally lost. Using your prized gold nugget passed down by your ancestors might not be wise. Higher quality fish hooks tend to be lost less often. ===Bait=== Bait is expendable meaning you can catch at maximum one fish per bait. Some types of bait include: {{#ask: [[Category:bait]]}} ===Lure=== Lures are durable, but they can be lost. {{SharedText|unlock_lures}} Some types of lure include: {{#ask: [[Category:Fishing_Lure]]}} ==Fishing Trash== When something is dropped in a body of water, it is added to a global pool of items that can be fished up anywhere in the world or found buried on the beach. Some people use this feature for charity by throwing their old equipment in the river. You can also drop a written parchment to send a message to a random fisherman as if it were a message in a bottle. ==Fishing== '''Quoting Jorb:''' Fish are now a limited and localized resource, working in the same way as any other localized resource. You should notice fish jumping in the water if the area has fish. What you catch when fishing depends on the following factors: Time of day, [[Water Terrain|location]], [[Fishing Lure|lure]], [[Fish Hook|hook]], [[Fishing Pole|pole]] and [[Fishing Line|line]] type as well as the quality level of each piece of fishing equipment. ===Bait Fishing=== Bait-Fishing is a semi-automated and a Semi-random activity using a Bushcraft Fishing Pole. Bait, line, and hook combo seem to have no strong correlation between fish types caught. Equip your pole, gather an inventory of bait, and begin fishing. You will automatically catch, recast, and re-bait your hook while bait fishing. The type of fish caught are random, based on the node you are fishing from, however the quality is hard-capped by your [[Survival]]. If you are simply looking to catch fish with no real desire for a certain type of fish, this is your easiest option. ===Lure Fishing=== Lure-Fishing using a Primitive Casting Rod is a more precise and manual activity. Lure, line, and hook type all play role in what species you will be able to catch. Every fish have certain line, hook, and lure combos that work best for it. If you are having difficulties catching a fish or keeping your lure, be sure to try new combinations. Equip your pole, gather some tackle, and begin fishing. You will be presented a list of fish to "aim" for, with a given % of how likely they are to bite. This list is a function of your [[Survival]] and [[Will]], so the higher both stats are, the larger your list will be. Max number of fishes in a list is limited by 10 and apparently is equal to \lceil \sqrt{Will * Surv}/20 \rceil. You can choose what kind of fish you want to catch. If you do not pick any, the game will automatically choose the fish with the highest % of biting. If you can't find the fish you are looking for, move away from the current location to find another fish node. Note that there the chance to catch a specific type of fish within the node has an optimal location where it is maximized. Try different spots nearby and go in the direction where the chance increases until you find the best spot (works similar to maximum quality within forageable nodes, but for the catch chance). While the list normally shows only three types of fish, you may be able to catch more types of fish in the spot using the same lure, line and hook combination. For some types of fish (cave fish, for example), you may find the [[Fisher's Request for a Catch]] magic really useful (can boost the chance to bite from about 30% to 80%, for example). ==Fish Data== '''Data about fishline and lure highlighted in bold and underline is correct for World 16''' ===Fresh Water Fish=== Fish that can be found in rivers and lakes. {| class="wikitable stickyHeaders" style="text-align: center; width: 75%;" |- class="sticky" ! style= "width: 5%" | Type ! style= "width: 1%" | Size

Goat.title: Goat

sections.Animals:
*A billy is a male goat. They can impregnate female goats.
*An nanny is a female goat. After being impregnated they give birth to a kid in 4.5 days, and then start producing milk, of which they can store a maximum of 25L.
*A kid is an adolescent goat. They will grow into either a billy or an nanny in 10 real life days. In addition to the regular products, kids give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered.
sections.Wool:
Goats produce [[Mohair]] at a rate of 1 every 8 hours RL at Wool Quantity 5. The speed of growth can be increased by breeding for Wool Quantity. The quality can be increased by breeding for Wool Quality.

See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information.
sections.Tips & Tricks: It is recommended to separate billy goats from nanny goats, to ensure only the highest quality billy impregnates the nanny goats. Also, lower quality billy goats may be castrated to stop them from mating. Castration produces one [[bollocks|Bollock]].
sections.Gallery:

[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skinned.png|150px]] Skinned {{PAGENAME}}. Can be butchered for [[Intestines]], [[Entrails]] and [[Raw Chevon]].
[[File:{{PAGENAME}}-skeleton.png|150px]] {{PAGENAME}} Skeleton. Can be collected for [[Bone Material]].
{{Game Development}}

See [[Taming]] for more information. Goats produce [[Mohair]], a type of wool, but they can only 'store' a maximum of 3 Mohair at a time. Since they also produce [[Goat's Milk|milk]], raising Goats can be a direct alternative to herding [[Sheep]]. Of the two, Goats are somewhat more difficult to obtain. ==Animals== *A billy is a male goat. They can impregnate female goats. *An nanny is a female goat. After being impregnated they give birth to a kid in 4.5 days, and then start producing milk, of which they can store a maximum of 25L. *A kid is an adolescent goat. They will grow into either a billy or an nanny in 10 real life days. In addition to the regular products, kids give you a [[Suckling's Maw]] when butchered. ===Wool=== Goats produce [[Mohair]] at a rate of 1 every 8 hours RL at Wool Quantity 5. The speed of growth can be increased by breeding for Wool Quantity. The quality can be increased by breeding for Wool Quality. See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information. ==Tips & Tricks== It is recommended to separate billy goats from nanny goats, to ensure only the highest quality billy impregnates the nanny goats. Also, lower quality billy goats may be castrated to stop them from mating. Castration produces one [[bollocks|Bollock]]. ==Gallery== [[File:{{PAGENAME}}-dead.png|150px]] Dead {{PAGENAME}}. Leather.title: Leather

sections.How to Make Leather:
In order to make leather, follow the steps below. Leather isn't exactly hard to make, but it is a time-gated process.
#Acquire a [[:Category:Fresh Animal Hide|Raw Hide]] from a [[Creatures|Wild Beast]].
#Dry the Raw Hide by placing it in a [[Drying Frame]].
#Wait 14-29 real-time hours for the hide to finish drying.
#Take the Dried Hide and place it in a [[Tanning Tub]].
#Fill Your Tanning Tub with [[Water]].
#Gather four pieces of [[Treebark]] from a [[Tree]].
#Right-click the [[Treebark]] into the tanning tub to create [[Tanning Fluid]].
#Wait 30 real-time hours for the hide to turn into leather.
#Retrieve your finished leather.
sections.Notes:
*Tanning tubs need a minimum 20L of fluid to operate, but can hold up to 40L of fluid. 
*As leather is produced, the volume of fluid goes down.
*Tanning Fluid is consumed upon creation of a unit of leather, not as a function of time.
*If the level of tanning fluid becomes too low, The tanning process will stop (but not reset) 
**This can be seen on the tub graphic as no fluid showing. Add more tanning fluid to finish the process
*2 [[Treebark]] or 1 [[Exquisite Rot]] is required to make 5% concentration per Tanning Tub
*While the necessary concentration of tanning fluid is 5% (2 treebark per tub), you may increase the tanning fluid concentration to 10% (9 treebark per tub). 
**This has no effect on tanning speed, but if your treebark quality is higher than your water quality, you can incrase your tanning fluid quality by adding more bark.
*Each tub can hold a maximum of 4 inventory spaces of hide at a time. For example: 4x [[Rabbit Fur]] or 2x [[Fox Hide]] or 1x [[Aurochs Hide]].
*Tanning fluid can be made directly in containers such as barrels, buckets, and tanning tubs.
*18 IRL minutes should equal to one percent of tanning progress. (30 hours -> 0.3 hours -> 18 minutes)
sections.Quality Formulas:
Tanning Tub Q = <math>\frac{_{q}AvgBoard * 2 + _{q}AvgBlock}{3}</math>

Tanning Fluid Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Water + _{q}TreeBark}{2}</math>

Leather Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Hide * 3 + _{q}Fluid * 2 + _{q}Tub}{6}</math>

{{Game Development}}

Leather.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Tanning]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Prepared Animal Hide]]
| producedby = [[requires::Tanning Tub]] and [[requires::Tanning Fluid]]
}}

Leather is animal hides that have been strengthened through the process of [[Tanning]]. It is a widely used resource in early-mid game crafts and builds and is used to make things like basic armor, tools, clothing, etc, as well as used to create [[Hardened Leather]].
==How to Make Leather==

In order to make leather, follow the steps below. Leather isn't exactly hard to make, but it is a time-gated process.
#Acquire a [[:Category:Fresh Animal Hide|Raw Hide]] from a [[Creatures|Wild Beast]].
#Dry the Raw Hide by placing it in a [[Drying Frame]].
#Wait 14-29 real-time hours for the hide to finish drying.
#Take the Dried Hide and place it in a [[Tanning Tub]].
#Fill Your Tanning Tub with [[Water]].
#Gather four pieces of [[Treebark]] from a [[Tree]].
#Right-click the [[Treebark]] into the tanning tub to create [[Tanning Fluid]].
#Wait 30 real-time hours for the hide to turn into leather.
#Retrieve your finished leather.


==Notes==

*Tanning tubs need a minimum 20L of fluid to operate, but can hold up to 40L of fluid. 
*As leather is produced, the volume of fluid goes down.
*Tanning Fluid is consumed upon creation of a unit of leather, not as a function of time.
*If the level of tanning fluid becomes too low, The tanning process will stop (but not reset) 
**This can be seen on the tub graphic as no fluid showing. Add more tanning fluid to finish the process
*2 [[Treebark]] or 1 [[Exquisite Rot]] is required to make 5% concentration per Tanning Tub
*While the necessary concentration of tanning fluid is 5% (2 treebark per tub), you may increase the tanning fluid concentration to 10% (9 treebark per tub). 
**This has no effect on tanning speed, but if your treebark quality is higher than your water quality, you can incrase your tanning fluid quality by adding more bark.
*Each tub can hold a maximum of 4 inventory spaces of hide at a time. For example: 4x [[Rabbit Fur]] or 2x [[Fox Hide]] or 1x [[Aurochs Hide]].
*Tanning fluid can be made directly in containers such as barrels, buckets, and tanning tubs.
*18 IRL minutes should equal to one percent of tanning progress. (30 hours -> 0.3 hours -> 18 minutes)

==Quality Formulas==

Tanning Tub Q = <math>\frac{_{q}AvgBoard * 2 + _{q}AvgBlock}{3}</math>

Tanning Fluid Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Water + _{q}TreeBark}{2}</math>

Leather Q = <math>\frac{_{q}Hide * 3 + _{q}Fluid * 2 + _{q}Tub}{6}</math>

{{Game Development}}

Peppercorn.title: Peppercorn

sections.How to Acquire:
*Peppercorn can be obtained by drying [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Herbalist Table]] or [[Drying Frame]]
*Harvesting a tile of planted peppercorn gives you up to 3 peppercorn depending on your [[Skills]]

Peppercorn.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj
| xitem      = 1
| yitem      = 1
|
| skillreq   = [[requires::Farming]]
| objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Windsown Weed]]
| producedby = [[requires::Peppercorn Crop]]
}}

[[Category:seeds]]

Peppercorn has no use by itself, and must be cooked in a [[Cauldron]] to make [[Boiled Pepper Drupes]], then dried  on a [[Herbalist Table]] to make [[Dried Pepper Drupes]], then ground with a [[Quern]] to make [[Black Pepper]] before it can be applied to another food to increase it's [[FEP]] gain.

==How to Acquire==
*Peppercorn can be obtained by drying [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Herbalist Table]] or [[Drying Frame]]
*Harvesting a tile of planted peppercorn gives you up to 3 peppercorn depending on your [[Skills]]

==How to Use==
Peppercorn is a [[Trellis]] Crop and therefore must be planted on a [[Trellis]]

Once the above requirements are met, left click a peppercorn and right click the trellis to plant a tile of peppercorn

{{Game Development}}

Pig.title: Pig

sections.Hog: Hogs give 1-2 Boar Tusk when their bones are collected, sows do not.
sections.Advantages To Pigs:
Pigs give [[Raw Pork]] instead of Boar Meat. Pork has all of the same applications as Boar meat and can also where Boar Meat cannot be. Full grown pigs also give two [[Fresh Pigskin]], a 2 tile hide with no known use other than generic hide recipes.

See [[Animal Qualities]] for more information.

In order to obtain pigs you must [[Taming|tame]] [[Boar]]s. Pigs can be lead around by equipping a [[Rope]], right-clicking on the animal, and selecting 'Leash'. Red Deer Antlers.title: Red Deer Antlers

sections.How to Acquire: After hunting and killing a [[Red Deer]], butcher the corpse to receive a number of [[Bone Material]] along with Deer Antlers.
sections.Additional Uses:
*Deer antlers can be boiled in a [[Cauldron]] to make [[Hartshorn]]
*Deer antlers can be crafted with a [[Block of Wood]] to make [[Antler Steak Cutlery]]
*Deer antlers can be placed on a wall as a decorative item
*Deer antlers can be used as a regular [[Bone Material]] when crafting items. This can also be a detriment if you are not careful as you can accidentally turn Deer Antlers into [[Bone Arrow]]s or [[Bone Glue]].

Salt Basin.title: Salt Basin

metaobj.ptext2: Before you stands a pool reflecting the white gold of pure salt, in hues of blue. You have found a natural Salt Basin.

sections.Spawn: {{PAGENAME}} holds up to 5 of [[Salt Crystal]], respawns 1-2 piece every 3 days.
sections.How to Use:
*Using the Inspect action on any localized resource will tell you how many resources are currently available or how long until the next resource regenerates.
*Right clicking a {{PAGENAME}} that has resources will give the user an option to collect the [[Salt Crystal|salt]].
sections.Quality: *The quality of the {{PAGENAME}}'s resource is [[hardcap|hardcapped]] by [[Cooking]], making it unwise to harvest this resource with an unskilled character.
sections.Notes:
*[[Localized Resources]] cannot be claimed by a [[Personal Claim]], but can be claimed by a [[Village Claim]]
**[[Salt Crystal]] is a permanent resource and will not decay.
[[Category:Localized Resource]]

Salt Basin.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | image = File:Salt Basin.jpg | ptext2 = Before you stands a pool reflecting the white gold of pure salt, in hues of blue. You have found a natural Salt Basin. | | xobst = 4.746 | yobst = 3.815 | | lift = No }} {{PAGENAME}} is a [[Localized Resource]], a rare type of resource which regenerates over time in a permanent location - in [[Shallow Water]], [[Mountain|Mountains]] and [[Cave|Caves]] .
Because of their rarity and permanent location, Localized Resources are often very contested and highly valuable.
==Spawn== {{PAGENAME}} holds up to 5 of [[Salt Crystal]], respawns 1-2 piece every 3 days.
==How to Use== *Using the Inspect action on any localized resource will tell you how many resources are currently available or how long until the next resource regenerates.
*Right clicking a {{PAGENAME}} that has resources will give the user an option to collect the [[Salt Crystal|salt]].
==Quality== *The quality of the {{PAGENAME}}'s resource is [[hardcap|hardcapped]] by [[Cooking]], making it unwise to harvest this resource with an unskilled character. ==Notes== *[[Localized Resources]] cannot be claimed by a [[Personal Claim]], but can be claimed by a [[Village Claim]] **[[Salt Crystal]] is a permanent resource and will not decay. [[Category:Localized Resource]] ==Gallery== Salt_Basin.jpg | A Salt Basin with Salt Crystal. Salt_Basin_2.jpg | A Salt Basin without Salt Crystal. {{Game Development}}

Seeds of Flax.title: Seeds of Flax

sections.How to Acquire:
Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed 

After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]].
sections.How to Use:
In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted.
sections.Notes: Seeds of Flax can be turned into [[Linseed Oil]] using an [[Extraction Press]]

Seeds of Flax.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Windsown Weed]] | producedby = [[requires::Flax Crop]] | specific = Crop Seeds }} [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] [[Category:Seeds]] {{PAGENAME}} are used to plant [[Flax Crop]] You need 5 {{PAGENAME}} to plant one tile of this crop. You can stack {{PAGENAME}} in stacks of 50 in your inventory, or store 1000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Bucket]], or store 10000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Barrel]]. ==How to Acquire== Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]]. ==How to Use== In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. ==Notes== Seeds of Flax can be turned into [[Linseed Oil]] using an [[Extraction Press]]

Seeds of Wheat.title: Seeds of Wheat

sections.How to Acquire:
Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed 

After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]].
sections.How to Use:
In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted.
sections.Notes: Placing {{PAGENAME}} on an herbalist table will result in [[Seeds of Sprouted Wheat]]s, which require only 4 seeds to be planted, and are affected by the quality of your herbalist table, so it can be used to increase the quality of your wheat crop faster.

Seeds of Wheat.rawWikitext:
{{infobox metaobj | xitem = 1 | yitem = 1 | | skillreq = [[requires::Farming]] | objectsreq = [[requires::Wild Windsown Weed]] | producedby = [[requires::Wheat Crop]] | specific = Crop Seeds }} [[Category:Powders, Liquids, and Seeds]] [[Category:Seeds]] {{PAGENAME}} are used to plant [[Wheat]], and can also be ground into [[Wheat Flour]] using a [[Quern]] You need 5 {{PAGENAME}} to plant one tile of this crop. You can stack {{PAGENAME}} in stacks of 50 in your inventory, or store 1000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Bucket]], or store 10000 {{PAGENAME}} in a [[Barrel]]. ==How to Acquire== Place a [[Wild Windsown Weed]] on a [[Drying Frame]] or [[Herbalist Table]]. When it dries, it has a chance of becoming {{PAGENAME}}, or any other type of seed After planting {{PAGENAME}} and waiting for its crop to grow, harvesting the crop will yield between 10 and 15 {{PAGENAME}}, depending on your [[Skills]]. ==How to Use== In order to use {{PAGENAME}}, you must first [[Terraforming#Plowing|plow]] a tile of earth, then plant the {{PAGENAME}} in the ground.
There are several ways to do this: *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, LEFT-CLICK and drag the {{PAGENAME}} to the plowed earth and RIGHT-CLICK the desired tile. *with {{PAGENAME}} in your inventory, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. *with a [[Bucket]] filled with {{PAGENAME}} in hand, SHIFT + RIGHT-CLICK the {{PAGENAME}} and hold LEFT-CLICK to drag an area of ground to be planted. ==Notes== Placing {{PAGENAME}} on an herbalist table will result in [[Seeds of Sprouted Wheat]]s, which require only 4 seeds to be planted, and are affected by the quality of your herbalist table, so it can be used to increase the quality of your wheat crop faster.
